user_comment: god these make me sweat These are comfy and stretchy but not breathable At All I swear I am drenched in sweat when I leave work. comfy, stretch, cooling Love these. I have huge thighs/ass and the material of these is much more forgiving than the typical old fashion scrubs I had been trying to squeeze into. No problem squatting in these or any other movements. Breathable and cooling , so they do make me kind of cold if I’m just sitting and charting, but it’s definitely the preferred material for when I’m up making rounds and all that. I got 3 colors, 2 from Amazon, which were a lot cheaper than the pair I bought at my local scrub hub. I’m almost 5’8 and around 235. Usually a size 16. I got an XL in top and bottoms and they are very flattering without being restricting. Very comfortable. Would recommend for all body types.

Nice wool leggings for the price. They fit true to my size. My measurements are 5 ft. 9 in. and 167 lbs. I bought size large. They are comfortable and I like the length. I would buy them again. They are slightly lighter weight than other wool leggings I’ve bought.

Awkward proportions but good workmanship These are every bit as good as Smartwool 200 gsm merino leggings although a bit scratchier, and the elasticized cuff is way better than the Smartwool’s for petite people, keeping all the excess length above your ankle. As another reviewer noted, the proportions are bizarre. The medium has a 28” long inseam paired with a weirdly short rise (7-8”), so it ends up being BOTH too long in the legs AND almost too short in the torso. I’m built like a boy and have a super short torso, and it’s still barely up to my bellybutton. I can’t imagine how it would work on anyone with a long torso or bigger than a 40” hip unless they want to stretch these things to their limits. Highly recommended if you’re a 5’7”, 130 lb, 37” hip, built-like-a-rail Nordic swimmer who likes low-rise wool leggings. Still recommended if you’re a short, slightly overweight rectangle who is going to hide them under snow pants and drysuits. .

Warm, cozy, opaque and breathable. These are equally comfortable lounging around the house (68-70F) and out for chilly walks (35-43F). They are nicely opaque, and wear well. No signs of pilling to date. I’m a pear-shape, currently ~145lb and bought the large. They’re a wee bit loose; could likely have bought the medium for a more fitted look. Because I’m short (5’2”) with short legs, I fold up the bottom cuffs. If worn multiple days (I use them for traveling), the knees do stretch out a little, but return to shape when washed. I wash them on gentle cycle and line dry, or dry on delicate and there’s been no sign of shrinking. Bought two pairs; black and the white/fawn stripes (which are really fun).

A LITTLE HIGH IN THE CROTCH, BUT NICE! I was looking for a wide-legged pant to go with my top. I purchased these in Orange and Red in sizes L, XL and XXL. I ended up going with the Orange pants in XL. At the time, I was about 210 lbs and I am 5'5".These are nice pants. They are a bit thin, so when I wore them, I put on a boy short underneath to smooth out the look. The crotch area is a bit high for my taste. So, even though they fit great, the crotch area can look a bit tight, giving the perception that they are too small, which was not the case. With a slight heel, I was great to go without having to hem them.I felt the Orange color 'popped' better over the Red, but both are nice colors.

Want to love them: sizing down and will review again Updated review: I returned both sizes. They are just awful. The material seemed like it was nice but any friction, and I mean ANY, like just rubbing your hands on them, cause the fabric to deteriorate. I don't even want to imagine what would happen after washing. Also the butt..it's so sad. Just don't. The pants were much better quality than I expected. Heavy but soft, but not fragile material. I've lost a lot of weight and switch between anywhere from M,L,XL depending on the brand (not weight fluctuatation). I have larger breasts, wide hips and butt. Pros: good quality, comfortable, elastic band and button/zipper, big pockets, high waisted but could be worn lower Cons I saw another reviewer ask if these pants give you "sad butt" and I'm here to say and show the proof.... yes... yes they do give you sad butt. My butt is not happy 😆🤧 Length is kind of weird. I'm 5 foot 4" and they definitely don't fit me cropped even pulled up as high as they'll sit without a belt. They are hitting my ankles and top of feet in a way that evokes memories of childhood pants that had shrunk too short. Conclusion I'm going to order a pair in medium and update. I gave the pants a good review because it's not the pants fault I'm in between sizes and short. And I knew what I was getting into with the "sad butt" which hopefully will be remedied by a smaller size. We shall see.

My new favorite: a lovely, natural fiber, comfortable pair of pants for fall and spring weather Overview: These pants are comfortable yet elegant. Ignore the part of the listing which says cotton: they are entirely of linen. The pants are a great weight for fall and spring- a bit warmer, yet still lightweight. The grey color is a lovely neutral tone which matches a variety of colors and styles of tops.Sizing and fit: I am a size 8-10, 5'4" with a high waist. I ordered a size medium. These pants fit me perfectly, with the legs hitting exactly at the ankle (!!!). The cut is a baggy or comfortable fit. The ankles on the pants I received are tapered, more so than I expected from the pictures, and I prefer that. I would NOT consider these plus size by American standards. I used the sizing chart on the listing, so make sure you plug your measurements into that chart- from my experience, it seems accurate.Pros:-These are so, so comfortable and fit really well- not too short, nor too long.-The relaxed fit is really nice for working from home, or a dinner date. They dressed up well with a nice shirt, and also work for casual situations.-The fabric seems to be of a good quality with a fairly tight weave (this is why I recommend the pants for spring/fall weather).-The seams are sewn with what looks like a cotton basting or hemming tape. This means that none of the seams are exposed. This probably makes them more comfortable, and will also increase the longevity of the pants. The fabrication of these pants looks very high quality to me. (See picture)Cons:-Because these are entirely linen, I find them to be slightly scratchy in warmer temperatures (above 70*), so my personal preference is to wear these in mild weather, around 65*- 75*. At cooler temperatures the fabric does not feel scratchy/ prickly to me. However, if you are someone that needs their clothing to be super soft, these might not be the right pants for you.-Because they are entirely linen, they wrinkle easily. If you want to wear them for a night out, I would iron them first. I've included one picture of how they look after ironing, which is marked; the other pictures are of the pants which were initially ironed after wearing them for a while.-The taping in the crotch seams made that area seem a bit bulky at first, but as I wear them it does not bother me.

I wanted to love these! I wanted to love these leggings! The leggings were packaged very well which I appreciated. They were folded very nicely and placed into a little zip pouch that was clear on one side and sort of opaque on the other. Feel of the leggings were...prerty okay. They were kinda soft, but had a bit of a 'glossy sheen' to them -if that makes any sense. I bought the ones with the owls on them. My intention was to use these for workouts. I couldn't wait to try them on! BUT, when I did, the poor owls were stretched to the max, and looked a bit distorted. Also, after I pulled them up they immediately began to slide back down. They were too low on the waste and came a couple inches or so above my ankles (I'm 5ft5). I am a true Medium in leggings, and can sometimes wear a small. In pants I wear from a 7 to a 9 (juniors, and just depends). I have thick muscular thighs from lifting weights, and a pretty decent sized booty. So if you are curvier, these likely may not work for you. If your more on the slim or petite size, then your probably good. These shipped very fast, and I've had no interactions/issues with sellers. Not sure I will bother trying any of the other designs as I'd planned.

I would give the leggings 4.5 stars mainly because the material feels like cotton (the title in my order list also says cotton) but the label says nylon and polyester. However, I really like the fun color of the leggings which is very different from my other leggings. The material feels not as stretchy as some and I guess because it contains some cotton. The leggings fits as expected though. I ordered size small and I’m 5’4” and 115 lbs for reference. There is not much compression around the waist but the leggings don’t really ride down during my 7 mile run. This is an important factor for me because it really frustrates me when I have to constantly pull my leggings up during a long run. It is sweat wicking but like most cotton materials, it doesn’t get dry fast and basically sweat weighs down the leggings a bit. Overall, I like the leggings a lot. I only wore them once and very happy to run in them. Only wish the label description was more accurate.

Bring on the cold weather!! Be still my cuddly-fleece-loving heart -- these fleece lined leggings are amazing!! I have never looked forward to walking the dog in the cold weather more than I am right now! I practically live in leggings 24/7, and I have some thicker cold-weather leggings, but nothing like these. These are fantastic!⛄ These are fleece lined all the way from just below the waistband (the only part not fleece-lined but still very thick) to the ankles. And I am talking sherpa blanket type fleece -- cozy, cuddly fleece. They are pretty thick (about equal to me wearing an under layer + normal thick leggings) but they are very soft and stretchy -- literally like they took a sherpa fleece blanket and made it into leggings. They are also super easy to get on (sometimes the thicker leggings are stiff and don't give as much, but not these) -- they are like slipping on comfy PJs.⛄SIZING: I went with my normal legging size (8/10-Large). For reference I am 5'5", slightly fluffy mid-section. These fit very comfortably. They have a little extra length in the ankle (the way a 24-26" inseam legging normally fits on me) -- but I like that in the cold weather so my ankles aren't exposed to the wind. The waist does have an enclosed top elastic band and I would consider these high waisted (above my belly button), but I do not find these restrictive or compressive at all and they do not roll. I don't think the crotch area is particularly flattering as there is a bit of extra material there along with the central seam-- but I am not wearing these to a fashion show, but to stay warm so I can't really complain. I would recommend staying true to size on these. You can certainly size down if you want compression, but these aren't really for body shaping. I definitely would not size up.⛄There are no seams on the front, back or outside of the leg (only up the middle of the leg so it doesn't show) -- so I would consider these pretty much seamless.I cannot express how cozy and comfortable these are. Right now, I am torn because I don't want to take them off, but I am also melting in the summer heat. I don't usually relish being out in the cold to walk the dog or being at really cold ice arenas for hockey -- but I absolutely cannot wait to see how much of a difference these make! 100% recommend. ❤

Superior Leggings for Winter These pants are truly superior to any warm leggings I’ve worn in the past, and I only wish I had discovered them years ago. They are perfect for those cold New England nights when I need to shovel snow or brush off the car. I’m also planning to take them with me on my upcoming travels to northern Europe during the winter. They are incredibly comfortable and soft making them ideal for any outdoor activities. I carry my weight in my hips and thighs, and these pants fit perfectly without feeling restrictive. There's just the right amount of stretch, so I don't feel like I’m being squeezed into them. At 5'9" and 160 pounds, I often struggle to find pants that are long enough to reach my ankles, as many get pulled up by my hips and thighs. These pants, however, offer the ideal length, which is a rare find for my height and shape. The fleece lining keeps the feel of these pants soft and comfortable, making them even more perfect for cold weather. At this price point, it's truly a product not to pass up! The warmth, comfort, and quality make them an absolute must-have. I honestly wish I could give these more than five stars—they’ve exceeded all of my expectations!

Size up if you are a between size I am 5’6.5”, about 143-145 lbs, with a slightly athletic/muscular build. I tend to wear a 26/27 in pants and the medium fit me nicely. I also tried a size small (also pictured) but they were too tight in the mid thigh. Quick review version: pant material is nicer and thicker than expected, love that the waist band is smooth and not elastic. Nice length and overall shape. I can wear these with a pair of sneakers and the ends won’t drag. Longer/in depth: as mentioned, I have a bit more muscle, especially in the thighs. I first bought the small based on reviews and size guide. The waist fit me well, but the actual leg part was a bit too tight around my mid thigh. My pictures show me in the medium and small. You can see that the small is more tight in the thigh which causes a bit of buckling/a pouch to form at the top. The medium stays flowy through the leg. The waist on the medium is not too much larger and still fits me fine. These pants are good for cold weather or cold offices, but you could also probably wear them with a t shirt and not be too hot.

The material is lightweight (linen-like) and the waist is elastic. The fabric itself is not stretchy, so I would say the waist is only moderately stretchy (limited by the actual fabric itself). I love the look of paper bag waist pants, but just have not found them to be that flattering on my body type (straight, no hips). Not an issue with these pants, just an overall life gripe! I will enjoy these in the summer with a cute pair of casual sandals and maybe a white or black casual tee on top.

SIZE UP! I am 60 y.o., almost 5'1", about 118 soft lbs. Carry most of my weight in lower stomach (30") and butt/hips(39"). The fit here totally depends on your preference. The medium fit well but was stretched a bit at the hips and pockets so I tried a large. They were too baggy but I washed on cold and dried on low and they shrunk just enough - maybe an inch everywhere (inseam is now 25"). The rise is still a bit low which makes them hang longer. They came out of the dryer just the way you see in the 2 "after" pics - no need to iron any serious wrinkles. Love the fabric for summer - light enough but not too thin or see-through. Very comfortable and DO have 3% stretch all around. Getting more colors... pics are before and after wash/dry.

Forever customer I’m 5’3, 265lbs. I love these! The fabric looks more like denim than any other legging/jegging I’ve tried. They have just the right amount of stretch, they aren’t too thin or too thick, and I’ve washed them a half dozen times and so far they’ve held their shape. I love that the waistband is slightly wide, but still thin and flexible. I’ve had multiple abdominal surgeries so I don’t like really thick waistbands or any kind of hardware digging in. With me being shorter, they obviously aren’t quite capris, but I really love these and I hope they keep making them forever because I will buy them till the day I die , lol.

These are lovely! They look and feel like proper jeans (not just like denim-look fabric) but are so stretchy and comfortable. Worst fit ever I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

Nice actually!!! The material is little bit thin, but overall they’re really nice! Very comfortable and actually long enough!! I am 5‘10, 149lbs - and never find any jeggings, leggings or pants that are long enough. I will definitely be buying another pair of these. Very flattering!!

Good fitting jeggings. Look like real jeans. My jeans are all a bit tight after I gained 10 pounds so I have been on the look out for jean-like leggings that didn't look like too much like fake jeans. Not an easy thing to find. These No Nonsense Women's Denim Legging actually look like jeans. They are not thick and stiff like jeans but they have good texture and coloring. I like that, if I roll them up, they have jean-like coloring on the inside so they look very much like a real pair of jeans. I am 5"5", 139 pounds and got size medium. I also tried the size small and they were just too tight around my gut to be comfortable. The mediums fit really well around my stomach, hips, butt, and legs. I do have thin legs so these don't cling super tightly like a pair of high Spandex leggings but that's fine. That makes them look even more like real skinny jeans. The length is good and they seem to be made well. I will probably buy a pair or two more. They're not warm but definitely not see through or flimsy. If they were just a bit thicker for cooler weather, they would be just about perfect. They are the most realistic looking jean leggings I have found so far.

Better than expected! So first off, I weigh 180, 5’6” and usually wear a10/12 in jeans. I ordered a large and they fit perfectly. I was concerned when I read the waist is below the belly button- that was not the case. Waist hit right where it was supposed to. Super comfortable! Color is close to jean material, my hubby thought they were jeans. Only complaint would be I wish they were longer, but I do have long legs so it could just be me. Haven’t washed them repeatedly yet so can’t remark on durability. Bottom line I will be ordering more!

The small is too loose for xsmall wearers I'm 5'2" & at the time of the photos, I was 26 waist 36 hips & around 107 lb. I bought this pair in 2019 & wore it for about 3 years before it became very faded. It is very stretchy, it's flattering except the pockets are kind of narrow & longish? makes your butt look like it is slimmer but longer lool. It's not durable, don't expect to last more than a couple of years, depending on how much you wear & wash it. Back then when I wore this pair the most, I was around 115 - 125 lb & it became my go to pair for everything. I would say, I only recommend this pair for people over 115 pounds, otherwise it would be too lose so if you wear xs, the small will probably be too loose. I hope it helps.

Sturdy, nice dark jegging denim I liked the dark denim. True to size. I’m 5’4, 200+ lbs. This waist band is sturdy but comfy & goes up enough. I liked I could fold at bottom for that casual look. Thick enough not to be flimsy, but thin enough to be comfy. I was cautious & bought 1 for now. I’d buy another👍🏼

Perfect affordable pregnancy pants! These are seriously the best "pants" ever. I usually only wear dresses and leggings (blue jeans are SO uncomfortable), but I wanted something to wear tshirts and sweaters with. I have these in a medium prepregnancy and they fit perfectly (for reference I was between 135-140 pounds, 5'5", and a size 6). I wanted comfortable pants to wear during my pregnancy and even with maternity pants the crotch area would be too tight and cut into me, and with how much my hips spread my thighs felt too restricted (not to mention that maternity clothes cost a small fortune). So I took a gamble and ordered these 2 sizes larger and the XL are PERFECT! (Hoping I can get a little more wear out of them postpartum until I can get my body back) For reference, I am 36 weeks pregnant, 5'5", size Medium in Motherhood Maternity/Old Navy Maternity, and 190 pounds. Most of my pregnancy weight is in my belly and hips. P.S. The length is good too, fits about the same as the mediums. There is a little bunching around my ankles but nothing bothersome, I've worn both tennis shoes and boots comfortably with these.

Get the sizing right! I ordered a large and should have ordered a medium. I am returning these but for nothing that is wrong with the legging itself, just a misjudgment in sizing on my part. I am 5'5 154lbs and usually wear 30/31 or size 10 pants. I have belly and hips but not really any booty or leg meat SO! that being said, I can't speak for other woman in the same height/weight class as I, but for me, these fit loosely with a bit of sag that just didn't look appealing. I think getting the size right makes all the difference in this particular brand of legging, so hopefully this short description and pics will help. No other issues - nice material, very comfortable, the waist didn't pinch the skin, seemed well constructed and I may just order these again except in the right size. ;-)

Happy! I'm happy with this purchase. These are medium weight and don't feel thin or flimsy. Color is classy dark denim. Comfortable! I'm very happy with the value for the price. I am buying these because I need more stretchy clothing for my post partum body for the summer. I think the only thing I might not like is that the waist is rolling down, and then feels a little tight, but I think that's mostly because of my baby belly. I"m 5'-10", 190 lbs, and measure 44" around my butt, and the Large fits perfectly. They are also nice and long! I rolled them up for more of a capri look, but they roll down very long. With no time to shop, I'm turned to amazon for my summer wardrobe, and I'm excited to wear these with some of the cute tops I've purchased on amazon!

Really nice but not a great fit for postpartum These are surprisingly thick for a legging. I wouldn’t be concerned at all about them being transparent. Also I feel like they would be pretty sturdy and warm (once again considering that they are leggings). However I used the size chart and other reviews to determine that I needed a medium. Clearly they are too small for me. I’m 9 weeks postpartum. So a lot of my weight is in my belly. This isn’t high enough or low enough to accommodate my belly. It hits at a terrible spot, I don’t really feel like these are a high rise. Maybe a midrise. They are also extremely tight in the belly and crotch area. I was going to return them but I decided to keep them in hopes they fit when I’m thinner. Currently I am 145 pounds. I’m 5 foot 4 inches. I would say a majority of my excess weight is in my belly. So maybe a large would have been better. I’m really disappointed that they don’t fit now.

Great look and fit I'm 5'8" around 165lbs Was looking for a good comfy pair of jeggings as I've gained around 15lbs. I didn't want to spend too much because I can't wear these to work, so only once a week would I want to wear these casually on my days off. Love the color. The rest of the reviews are correct, they run long, even for me, so I cuffed them at the ends which is fine. They have the appearance of a real jean, strechy, high-waisted and functioning back pockets. Definitely going to buy these in another color. The waist band doesn't roll down like some jeggings and leggings. Fits as expected. I'm a 12 in pants depending on the waist height, so I chose large and they fit great. Hope this helps!

Incorrect sizing chart Sizing is difficult to fully comment on because ... I ordered these based on the sizing chart, XXL indicated as 18 and 20, displayed on Amazon’s product page (image attached). I received an XXL, tried them on, and wondered why the fit was off. The sizing chart that the jeans/leggings come packaged with shows an XXL as 20 and 22. Leggings seem well made, soft jean material, but gathers at the knees. Length is great, I’m 5’ 7”. Waist band falls naturally about an inch below my belly button. These feel like they will slide down if I bend over, maybe my butt’s too big. LOL! If I ordered an XXXL (not available), would fit too large. As a woman, I know that just because these were too snug fitting for me, they may not be for you. But then again, I’m not used to wearing leggings where the material gathers at the knees—not attractive looking. I ordered the dark denim blue. Three images attached.

Will be getting more I like the look and feel of these jeggings. Only worn once so I'll see how they hold up after washing but material feels sturdy. I will be hanging them to dry. Completely obsessed with these pants!!!! First the specifics… I am 5’8.5”, 145 lbs, 24” waist, 35” hips, and long legged! I’m 45.5 years old and I love to deviate from my typical all black aesthic! I ordered a small. Definitely needed an XSmall if they came in XSMall. I wore a 2.5” heel with these. Quality: too good to believe with the price! They are thick satin, not that thin mess. They are heavy, thus why they seem to sag. They are incredibly comfortable. They are sexy! The color is everything! I often worry when wearing satin, that they will stain easy or attract spots quick from lotion, or whatever…nope not these. The stitching is quality and appears to be durable. The fabric is so soft to touch and gives designer. I love the pockets as well. I love my pants long, and these are definitely long. If you are shorter or do not like your pants extra long, have a seamstress hem the pants. Trust me they are worth paying for alterations. I ordered these for a quick date night to a beautifully designed lounge. I ordered the hot pink trench coat last year from Amazon as it was featured in “The Drop”, and I have been waiting on the perfect pants to wear with the gorgeous trench. When I saw these pants, I knew I wanted them paired with my coat. What I would do different or what I intend to do with the pants….have alterations done to fit my waist perfectly, hem them up about 2 inches, alter the crotch area, for a more tailored look. Pair them with my sneakers, and either my Tupac graphic t’s or my 80’s Rock band tshirts (for this quick look, I wore a Leon Bridges tour t-shirt, and tied in the back to show my waist) Other pros-These pants shipped quickly. I used my steamer and they were unwrinkled within 20 minutes, and guess what??? They stayed wrinkle free all night even with a lot of sitting. That’s it, that’s the review…I want every color.

Flattering on my mom bod I'm 8 weeks postpartum and none of my prepregnancy pants are comfortable or flattering anymore. I have some loose skin on my belly and a little pooch. I was looking for something form fitting but not skin tight, and these are it! I ordered this style of pants from several different sellers and these by far are the best! 1. They have a seamless waistband in the front, which makes them both comfortable and more flattering, as they don't dig into the belly. 2. They are made of a silky, buttery fabric that seams to smooth over any dimples and bumps. 3. Perfect length. I'm 5'6 and they hit right at the bottom of the ankle. 4. They aren't awkwardly tight in some areas and then loose in others, like some pants I tried on. 5. They hug the butt just right. My husband even said they made my butt look good! The only downside I noticed: inconsistent sizing. I ordered a small and a medium in the exact same pants. I went with the small. The medium was strangley much shorter than the small and looked more like long capris. For reference, I'm 5'6 and 128 lb. I got the charcoal color.

Decent pants I got these mainly for the pockets for when running errands and traveling. Pockets come in handy on these pants for stuffing things in them on the spot as opposed to having to opening and closing purses/backpacks repeatedly. I personally appreciate the fact that the thigh pockets have flaps that hook /adhere to the pants and not just flaps without a way to secure the pockets. This way things don't fall out of them as easily. The zipper pockets everywhere else are great, too. You have options whether you want to secure your items in there or not. I'm not a fan of cargo pants that just have pockets but no way to secure them. The fabric is thin but opaque. It's smooth and comfortable enough. When walking, the pants make a bit of sound reminding me of parachute pants. I'm not sure how I feel about the sound they make. Fortunately, it's not too loud. I don't love the pants but I don't hate them either. For reference, I'm 5"3, 28 waist, 35 hips, 119 lbs, and got size small. They fit comfortably.

Size small worked for me I'm age 62, a little over 5'6", weigh about 132 lbs. In general I need pants that are "roomy" in the butt. Made for TALL people! These pants promise a blend of professionalism and comfort that seems too good to be true - but for the most part, they deliver! The versatility is their main appeal. They bridge the gap between workwear and workout gear, perfect for my yoga-instructor daughter who also holds a day job at a marketing agency. The idea of a pair of pants that can effortlessly transition from a business setting to a yoga class is incredibly appealing. One of the first things that struck me about these pants is the quality of the material. It's sturdy and durable, suggesting longevity. You can tell they've been made with care and attention to detail, which elevates them from your standard pair of dress pants. However, the length of these pants is where we encountered an issue. My daughter, being a petite 5'4", found them to be excessively long. I ordered a medium size to match her usual 9/10 size, and while they fit perfectly around the waist and hips, the length was a significant setback. It's important to note that if you or the person you're purchasing for is on the shorter side, these pants will likely require hemming. Despite this hurdle, once these pants are altered to fit correctly, I have no doubt they'll become a staple in her wardrobe. The combination of comfort and style is not easily found, and these seem to have nailed it.

So comfortable and SOFT! Did I buy these because I heard they were a Lulu dupe? Absolutely. Am I disappointed? NOPE. Full disclosure, I don’t have enough disposable income to even know what the Lulu item even is or feels like, BUT I do know what cozy feels like—and this IS IT. What I love: 1. The weight of the material—it’s lightweight, but it feels like it would still keep you warm on a chilly spring morning 2. The pockets! They’re real sized pockets that will fit your keys and/or phone! 3. The fit: I’m 5’5” so I went with the longer inseam and I’m glad that I did! My height comes from long legs (as opposed to a long torso), so be sure to choose an inseam that will work best for you! I feel like you could style this with athleisure tops or even a nice sweater and it could still work. I’ll be wearing mine while coaching!

My favorites! These ODODOS leggings are my favorites for workouts and pickleball. I have five of them! The fabric is silky smooth and soft; they slide onto your legs and are extremely comfortable to wear. They are lightweight and stretchy, but not at all see through. NOTE: The patterned fabric is different from the solid fabric. I prefer the printed one, which is a polyester 76%/spandex 24% blend. (The solid fabric is not as silky.) The patterned fabric also hides imperfections like cellulite! The 19” inseam is great for summer and the 23” is perfect for any time. In the photograph I am wearing a 25” inseam, but I am quite tall. (5’8”). I machine wash mine and hang to dry. These are the only leggings I’m buying from now on!

Great quality 5’6”, 155. These are a size Large, I may be able to go down to a medium as I have a bit of room, but they don’t ride down or appear baggy anywhere. They have pockets on both legs large enough for an iPhone Pro Max, and the waist doesn’t roll. They’re above average quality, and not see through. Would definitely recommend.

Love all of my ODODOS yoga pants! I own a few pair of ODODOS yoga pants, both in capri and full length, and I love them all. These, however, are my favorite and I have them in both capri and full length. What I love about this pair in particular is the pattern. I like to have fun, patterned yoga pants, but sometimes I have a difficult time finding matching tops. Not so with these pants - they match with light blue, turquoise, bright orange, white or black and the colors are true (for example, not a weird shade of blue that only seems to exist on these pants). Also, the pattern is vibrant, bright, clearly defined AND the pattern doesn't lighten due to the stretch of the fabric in certain areas. You know how with some yoga pants the pattern is brightly colored on your calves but then lighter in the butt and hip because of stretching? That will not happen with these pants. Which brings me to why I love ODODOS yoga pants overall. They are a heavier, high-quality fabric that stretches with you without looking or feeling thin, and I find the high waist flattering. Another reason I love their full length is that I am 5'8" with about a 31-inch inseam and their full length actually goes to my ankles - fantastic and rare! I also love the pockets - a must for me when it comes to exercise. The pockets are deep and fit my phone or smaller water bottle comfortably. These fit well, wash well and LAST. While I just bought these, I have other ODODOS yoga pants that I've been wearing for 3-4 years. I feel these fit true to size. For reference, I typically wear a size 10 or large pants, again, I'm 5'8" and I weigh 157 pounds.

Finally a true red legging yoga pant that holds me in without looking and feeling like a stuffed sausage! Super squat proof and no rolling on my quite unfirm belly! Soft and pet fir doesnt stick to them! 5'3" 155 these are size small the 7/8 length. Could of gotten medium but I like mone snug

Great fit, but slips at the waist. Overall, these are great! When I put them on for the first time, they fit in all the right places. The problem was when I started to move. The waist band slides down very easily. I think I can resolve this by stitching a ribbon or something into the waist band. I’ve done that with similar pants in the past. The way the material is folded at the waist, it shouldn’t be that hard. In the meantime, I can wear them as loung pants or tuck my shirt into them to (hopefully) create some traction and prevent them from slipping. I don’t think I could’ve fit comfortably into the next size down. I am 5’ 6” and wear size 20, and I purchased the 2X. Also, love the pattern! Colors are true to what you see on Amazon. Pockets are spacious. My iPhone 8 fit into the pocket with plenty of room to spare.

These are “Meh” if you have a booty I gave these leggings a 3star rating because “High waist” to me means it covers my belly button. These leggings sat just below the belly button and seemingly have the same height in the back...where my big booty is....meaning, not enough coverage when I decide to sit down, much less squat. I ordered them in a Medium. These leggings might fit the hips and butt perfectly, if you don’t have much of either, but my waist is 29”, hips are 43” (190 lbs). I considered going up to a Large, but the waist would no longer fit snug and would gape in the back. On a positive note, I am 5’10 and these actually hit at my ankles, which is a rare find in leggings. Also, the red is cherry red, vivid, pretty and there are no see-through issues with these leggings. I’ll wear them for light active days (walking) , but probably wouldn’t order another pair.

Pros and cons / honest review For starters, I am 5’8 and 242lbs. First glance when I took them out of the package, they looked like they could run small as well as the length . They looked like they would not fit my legs, ( I have long legs) but to my surprise, they were very stretchy, length wise was perfection for me. I will say they do run small, I got all XLs. The green and maroon pair were very tight especially in the stomach area. The black pair I couldn’t even get over my calves, but for the green and maroon pair I could definitely get them on. If they had a 2XL, I could see that they would fit better. They are very soft, but also have a thick material which is nice. The colors are true to what you see on the photos. The scrunch in the booty is definitely there but I will say for me personally the scrunch was a little uncomfortable because of how tight the scrunch was. They are SQUAT PROOF, but I will say in the maroon pair especially, it’s not that noticeable on the green pair, right underneath the butt, right where the legs start there is a small area where it is very shear once you do a squat.( look at the other photo, where the blue circle is, that’s where it was shear) I personally don’t like that and don’t see the point of it being like that, but that’s me lol. I am definitely returning these. Hopefully this helps anyone.

If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say they run a tad small if you have bigger hips/butt. I sized up because I was concerned about them being too tight, and I'm glad I did! I could do a large but I really like the looseness of the XL, as it's comfy but still flattering, and with a bodysuit, the fit is perfect in the waist (this one is from Pumiey, btw; I have a review of it as well). They are loose in the tummy area without being too baggy. I'm glad the pleats are super subtle. This style does not have the elastic back, as I don't love that, but I do wish they had some little fake slit pockets in the back, like other slacks do. I'm a little on the shorter side and had some extra length at the bottom, even though my legs are longer in proportion to my body, so I could wear a pair of heels and still be fine, but I don't often wear anything but flats, so I'll probably end up getting them hemmed by an inch or two. The only small complaint I can make is that I wish the button was darker, as I feel the tan one cheapens the look a bit. The material is a bit hard to describe, but I'd say it's pretty standard Amazon loose slack material, if you've tried others. It's thin and a little slick, but not very stretchy, if that makes sense. They feel kind of cool on the inside and they move nicely. I couldn't tell you how well these will hold up in the long run, but I'll try to update after I've worn and washed a handful of times. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these, but maybe size up if you have bigger hips/butt. I've been looking for some cheap slacks to try out because I haven't tried to dress nicely for the office in so long, not since I gained weight years ago, and these are a nice intro back into it. I actually purchased this exact style in B-BEIGE in the same XL size, so I'll post pics of those. I may grab the C style next, since they have tonal buttons and slit pockets in the back, even if they have elastic in the waistband too.

Not tall girl/long legs friendly I'm 5'4" and around 140-145 lbs, but these became high waters on me once I started walking in them. I love the material, and they look super cute, especially with the matching tank, but I wish the inseam was longer. I can't stand when pants ride up, so I always go for an inseam of at least 31-32" to keep them covering my ankles. I also have a bit of a booty, which probably makes them ride up even more, because the length seemed fine when I first put them on. I definitely wish they were longer, and I'm not even tall, so I can't imagine how they'd fit anyone taller or curvier than me.

Overall A Good Option I got these for when I don’t want flared or ankle fitted leggings and they’ve been great for that! The material is thick and offers nice compression. I don’t usually leave photo reviews but hope that my body type helps someone else out. I’m 5’4, 160lbs and most of my weight is carried in my lower stomach. I got a medium and they fit nice and snug in a good way. I also have the full long sleeve body suit in a medium and that’s been great! Because of where the full on compression band is, I have to wear these low otherwise it completely puts my pouch on display. They’re a tad long but once I have shoes on, it isn’t really an issue. The black fit the exact same. Overall, they’re a great option to have on hand, just didn’t give 5 stars because of how the compression band is.

Sizing is questionable but I think I found a solution The quality is nice, very thick and warm. I’m 5’4 and the pants come past my ankles, unlike a few reviews I read who are around my height or shorter. I will say, I could’ve gotten a medium instead of a small, I’m 127. The small is a great size for somone who doesn’t have thighs, a butt or muscles (and that’s ok) but if you’re the toned/have more muscle, thighs and a butt kind of 5’4, get a medium. The sizing. I think if you want a longer pant leg, go up a size. Now, this is just s theory based off of reviews. Also, if you have a high butt or big butt, your pant legs may be higher (or rise up past your ankles). You can size up but you don’t have to. They have enough give but also don’t be delusional. Think about your body and the way clothes fit in you and whatever look you’re trying to convey, give it. Just be comfortable and don’t get too tight clothes, you’ll end up with stomach issues.

Comfy These are super comfortable pants. I don't think these are the most flattering pants for a pudgy, apple shaped middle aged body, but that's not stopping me from wearing them. For size reference, I am 5 foot 7, weigh 175 pounds, have a 36C bust with narrow shoulders, a short torso, and a wide but flat derriere, carry most of my weight in the middle, and wear a US women's size 10 or 30 pants (medium size pants and joggers at Old Navy and H&M), or a woman's 10 or 12 in dresses. I ordered these pants in a large, which fit snugly (see my pics). Overall, these are comfortable wide-leg pants, and I would order more clothing from this brand.

They’re definitely worth it. Cotton material so no yucky gross shiny polyester 👎🏽 I’m only 5’3 and these give the perfect amount of baggy. After a few washes they do shrink up a bit so I recommend washing(inside out)and hang drying. My weight fluctuates so currently my waist measures 26in hip 35in weight 108lbs. If you want these to be fitted than get usual size or size down but if you want more of what’s modeled than size up one or two! These are not super thick but they’re soft and comfortable. Last pic is of me wearing the black pair cuz I haven’t tried these on yet but they’re identical to the black pair except the color obviously 🙄 lol but just to give you an idea on how they look on someone with similar measurements. Soft & Stretchy I ordered the black and blue yoga pants. I just got them in the mail today! They are soft and stretchy. I am 140 lbs and I ordered a size Large. I recommend to order your normal size, don’t size up or down. They are not too tight around my belly just how I like it (in the past I have ordered Mediums, but I’ve noticed they fit me tighter and show my belly flaps a lot more in Medium size, so I ordered the Large). They do hug my belly/ waist a bit, how they should. They also hug my legs nicely as yoga pants should (not TOO tightly). There’s no bad smell to them and I can see them working perfectly for my home workouts. These might be my new favorite yoga pants! The quality of the fabric is what I would expect in yoga pants, but I just wish the price was at least $20 for each. It’s a bit overpriced in my opinion, but I wasn’t disappointed with what I got. If the price was lower I would’ve gotten a few more pairs.

Very soft and comfortable; wish fit were a bit different I've been looking for casual pants to wear around the house. I would have preferred something that did NOT have elastic bands the bottom of the legs, but these looked good otherwise, so I decided to try them. My usual pants size is medium (5'4.5", 150 lbs), but I ordered these in large because I didn't want them to be tight. On the one hand, it's a good thing that I got the large - you can see in the photos that the waist fits snuggly without adjust the loop closure at all. On the other, these pans are pretty baggy on me, which is surprising because I have large thighs - despite this, they look MUCH baggier on me than on the model! The material of these pants is very soft and comfortable. The interior is NOT fleece-lined like regular sweatpants. There is a black lining that looks like it would be heavy, but even though the pants seem pretty substantial, they also feel quite lightweight on. I like the pockets and the front seams, and I like that the closure is elastic with a sliding toggle rather than ties. There is a deep pocket at the back that has a small zipper closure - I get that this is supposed to be for a phone, but since I plan to mainly wear these at home, I could have done without the extra bulk. Overall, these are good quality comfortable lounge pants, and I would recommend them.

I find the shoelace drawstring at the waistband completely unnecessary and will pull that out (with one easy tug) as the waistband is stretchy and my waist is never going to be too small to hold these on. It is just in the way. Nice comfy pants.

Silky Soft and Comfy These yoga pants have a comfortable loose feel and I really like the silky-like fabric. They seem well-made and have a nice cooling effect that’s perfect for summer. The 10s fit perfectly, however I am extra thick around the middle and if you are more of an hourglass type the 8s might be a better fit. The 8s were great but a little too tight in the waist for me. I will echo some other reviews that these are definitely high rise, not mid. Totally fine by me but be aware if you care. Lastly, there was a huge difference in length between sizes so maybe consider that when you’re selecting a size. The 10s hit my ankle, the 8s were more like a cropped length.

Wonky sizing. very unimpressed I carefully used the size chart before ordering, which said that for a 29.9" waist to get a size 12. My daughter has a 30" waist, so I got that size for her (while crossing my fingers, because she usually wears a size 8). Well, I wish I had just gotten her regular size and ignored the size chart, because these don't come close to fitting her. They're way too big at the waist (and as for the length, forget about it). My waist is currently a 32", and these jeans more or less fit at the waist (although they are baggy and frumpy throughout), and even I maybe should have a size 10. Bottom line: IGNORE THE SIZE CHART. Either order your regular size or maybe one size up. And that said, the length is ridiculous. I'm 5'4", which is the exact average height of a woman in the US. and these are too long for me to even wear. Possibly if I wore them with stilettos they wouldn't drag on the ground, but guess what, I'm not wearing them with stilettos. So the entire fit of these jeans is off, the size chart in inaccurate, and the jeans don't look good on. These make me look chunky and are very unflattering. Not a fan at all.

Runs big I am usually a size 27 waist, which is a US size 4. According to the chart that would be a medium. I was afraid if I ordered a small that the waist would be too tight and uncomfortable. However, I normally wear a size small in adult women’s clothing. If you follow the chart, the fit may be a little big, so I would trust your usual size. I fit in the medium, but they definitely are on the bigger side. These look like they wrinkle…I know I am not somebody who will iron them, so I guess I’m wearing them wrinkled. I ordered the top, but it hasn’t come in yet, but it is nice that you can make a set for a very reasonable cost. The fabric is lightweight and seems to be a polyester blend. They’re not super stretchy. There is a drawstring and elastic waist. There are two back pockets, and two leg pockets, which I appreciate. I prefer to keep my phone in my right leg pocket where it doesn’t get in the way or fall out if I have to bend down and it’s secured with a zipper, so it doesn’t fall out.

Vivid, comfortable & stretchy These pants have such a vivid color. The green is very solid green. I feel it’s almost not true to the photos in the description but pretty close. They are stretchier than I thought they’d be and fit well. I am 5’ 5” and about 186 lbs. I hover between a 12-14 depending on the brand and the day. I went with a 14, and they’re spot on. I definitely would not have gotten away with a 12. Sometimes 14s tend to be long on me, but these are perfect bootcut length for my proportions. The rise is like a high mid. I carry my weight in my legs and belly, and while they don’t have tummy control I still feel they are pretty flattering. They are also on the thinner side which avoids feeling bulky like corduroy tends to, but it’s not the choice if you’re looking for something super warm. I would buy these in more colors and likely will, soon.

VERY flattering and soft/comfortable It's true about fashion. Wait long enough and it comes back in style. Flared bottoms and corduroy fabric, I'm back in Jr. high. But these pants, omg, they are NOT the pants from back then. The fabric is solid corduroy, not striped, super soft, and made from cotton and spandex like jeans these days so they have give and stretch. As soon as I opened the package I could tell I was in love. They hug your hips, high waisted, cute jean type buttons for your fly (yes no zipper but adds to the charm) 4 pockets, slimmer thigh to the knee, then flare out to the bottom. I walked into the living room for my husband to see and his eyes got big and said NICE! and now you're in style! (head slap for that).A couple of things. Normally I wear 8, sometimes 10 if no stretch or give. According to their size chart, because I have a 40" hip I went with their size 12. I should have paid more attention to the fabric because upon first try, no problem but slightly bigger than my body, a little sag in the crotch and from the pic you can see touches the ground. If you're wearing high heels or wedge, that's perfect. I could have easily gone to a 10. I decided to wash them last night, alone because they were new and black, on cool water wash, then dried on high. They came out slightly shrunk, which I hoped they would, and then tried them on again. I didn't take pics, but they still were easy to put on, hugged my hips, no crotch sag, waist a little more fitting and the length does not touch the floor anymore, just about less than 1/2" off the floor. I feel like I made them more like a size 10. So give some consideration that these are stretchy and either order your normal size and watch how you wash, or maybe try a size up and see how they fit, possibly knowing you can make them a little smaller in the wash. I did not notice any lint or fading after I washed them.I would say these pants are quality made, cute, cute, cute, very soft and comfortable to wear and will flatter just about any body type with it's cut. You do have to button them up. I'm glad the style is coming back so all shapes can look good, which to me means flattering to your body type. I would say the price to me is on the high side. I've always been thrifty and look for bargains or discounts, especially on a Sr fixed income. If you're willing to pay this for 1 pair of pants, go for it! They will not disappoint.

Great fit and fabric, too long. I love the fit and feel of these! It’s difficult for me to find women’s pants that actually fit my 24” waist, so I tend to purchase children’s pants. These fit my tiny frame well. For reference, I’m 5 foot 3 inches, 107 lbs. The one big problem I have with these is the length. Generally, those that require such a small size are shorter & I noticed many other reviewers complain of this as well. I can’t figure out why they wouldn’t decrease the length with the size??? The fabric is soft and stretchy, but not so stretchy it gives you the bloated belly look (ladies in your 40’s, you know what I’m talking about). The fabric is quite thick though, so if you live in a warmer climate like I do, these can really only be worn in the winter months.

Peekaboo... Everyone Can See You The fit and comfort were definitely on point; loose and flowy. However, they did not look frumpy. I usually size up for length, and I am not sure that was necessary. As a 5'9" girl, this was huge. Too bad I had to return them to the seller. Unfortunately the low rating is all about transparency. They were visible in indirect, natural light standing in my office. I kept the army green pair purchased in the same order, because the transparency was less evident.

Silky, comfortable, flattering. Shorter than expected length These silky high waisted palazzo pants are a timeless classic. The fabric is a silky feeling synthetic material that drapes well, and they are very flattering. When taken straight out of the box, they were a little wrinkled but that is to be expected. With a few minutes in the dryer they flattened right out. The waistband falls at the smallest part of my waist -these are very high waisted, and plenty long in the crotch. The pockets are not too shallow, and can fit my phone without slipping out. The waist band is both elastic and drawstring, so they are comfortable, functional and adjustable. I wish they were longer though- I am 5 foot 6 and with completely flat sandals they were about two inches from the floor (and I prefer a floor length pant). I think if you are shorter than me, the pants would hit the floor or perhaps allow a higher heel if you're close to five feet tall. If you're taller than me (for example 5 foot 8 or more) then they would be more of an awesome Culottes length. I ordered a size Medium, which based on the chart fits a size 8 to 10. I am a size 10 and these fit nicely in the waist (meaning the elastic was a perfect comfortable fit and did not require additional cinching with the drawstring). Overall these are a breezy daytime pant, the type that you see on European women with strappy sandals, a form fitting body suit and a great pair of sunglasses.

Great dress pants, style and quality I was hesitant to purchase these pants based on mixed opinions in posted reviews and overall score. Being a Latina with curves and big thighs, it’s sometimes challenging to find the right fit. For context I wear size 10 or 12 depending on the style, material, cut, etc… I bought these in a 12 to ensure they weren’t too fitted! Finally, classy pants that fit right! I plan on getting them in more colors.

Seems to run big. Nice lightweight pant. I am 5'7" and weight 155lbs, I have a 31" waist. Based on the measurement chart I ordered an XL, I normally wear between a M and a L. Based on their weight/height chart I probably should have ordered a L. These don't look big on me in the picture but they were, the slightest amount of weight in the pockets pulls them right down because the waistband is very loose on me. I got these for review so I can't trade them for a new size...instead I think I will thread a drawstring through the top so I can cinch them tighter.So, I would recommend going off of their size/weight chart for sizing...not the measurements they list.These are incredibly lightweight pants, I think they will be amazing in the summer but they don't work for the winter at all here in Minnesota. They have nice sized hand pockets and big cargo pockets as well. The cargo pockets do not fasten shut. The ribbed material around the waist is very stretchy and comfy.These are very comfy lightweight pants and seem to be good quality, a drawstring would have made them perfect...as is, they slide down too much when I wear them. They are very thin, not see through, but you definitely see underwear lines through them.For the price point they are very nice. I think they will be amazing in the summer. I just wish I had sized down and gone with the size/height chart for sizing and not the measurement chart. There is no way the XL have a 30" waist as listed...it's more of a 32-33" waist.

Enthusiastic Approval. Triumphant! I am so incredibly happy with this product, I had to take pictures and do a review. I don't do this for every purchase (I couldn't possibly) but when I have an experience this positive, I have to acknowledge it so I give credit where credit is due and also provide other shoppers with information to help them with a decision. I bought these pants because I was searching everywhere for pants to match this bright purple blazer I had bought and it is just impossible. I saw these, and I knew it was a gamble, but they showed up in the mail the next day, and it was exactly the same color and hue as it was pictured on the internet. It is a perfect match! Not only that, but the fit is absolutely perfect. I think these are quite possibly my most comfortable pants in my wardrobe now. They are stretchy enough to not need a zipper or button (the ones you see are fake) but the pockets are REAL! All four pockets are nice and deep and functional. The fabric is firm enough so that it doesn't wind up looking like leggings (they look like fancy jeans.) They are formal enough to wear with professional attire. They are the perfect length for me (5'2") and they do not fit too tightly around the waist (a problem I find with way too many pants and skirts. They fit just like a glove. In fact, I was shocked at how perfectly they fit. They were almost out of my price range, but I am very very glad that I sprang for a few extra bucks and I may purchase a few more pairs in different colors now that I see how great a product this is. For your reference, I am 5'2", 122 lbs, 31" waist.

I ultimately went with small and they fit nicely with absolutely no squeezing of my body. I am so comfortable at work and because of the texture of the fabric, they look like form fitting pants rather than leggings. If I order more to wear outside of work, maybe I'll try hanging them to dry and see if that helps.

Very stretchy fabric and smooth. Love them. I got black but I feel like the lighter colors will show wrinkles or cellulite more and perhaps be less flattering. These pants are modest and simple enough to be able to dress up as office/work pants.

😍 and comfortable Ordered medium petite and they fit perfect. I'm 5'3" and weigh 135 for reference. Ordered them in black and brown. The black pair seem to be a smidge shorter. I haven't measured them to see if they actually are. I'm able to wear them with sneakers, suede slippers or clogs and length is perfect. If I went with regular length they would drag the floor.They can be dressed up to be work appropriate or worn for casual or workout wear.I'm probably going to order in grey and cameo (if they come back in stock).

Not office wear, but great anyway… I bought this size Medium in 2 colors - I am 5’6” and 150 pounds and the fit is perfect. I bought them in hopes to “dress them up” as I’ve seen other reviewers mention, but I definitely won’t be able to wear them in my office setting workplace. The side seams (see pic) look like leggings/athletic wear which unfortunately won’t work for me at the office, but I will definitely get use out of these! They are a high rise (almost too high for me) with a loose fit thru the legs. The flare is gradual and flattering, and they hit right at the top of my foot (almost too short with shoes). They don’t do anything for my fluffy middle section, but I usually wear a longer top to cover that. It washes well - I did not dry it but the tag says dry on low. I got the taupe and cameo colors, and they are both nice. They are a comfortable addition to my athleisure wardrobe which seems to be growing lately!

Perfect travel pants Great for long flight to Italy. Soft and don’t wrinkle! I’m 5’2 122lbs. A little thick through the thighs! A petite small was a little snug. Petite medium fit perfect! Bought 2 pair!

Best pants EVER I cannot say enough good things about these pants. They are an athletic fit but I have worn them and dressed them up for work! Added heels and a nice top and I’m comfortable and set for the day!! 10/10 recommend. I went with a small / tall and I’m 5’5” , 150 lbs

New favorite golf pants I’m 5’3” at 130 lbs. I ordered the small and they are perfect. I ordered a medium petite and returned as they were too short and roomy. My favorite pants from now on! If you ladies know how hard is to find the most comfortable pants being 7 weeks postpartum, you might know how happy I am to find the most comfortable one ever! I love these pants - so much, in fact, that after my first pair arrived, I ordered two more pairs! The pants fit true to size. I usually wear a size small, so that's what I ordered, and they fit well. I'm 5'3" and they're a little bit too long for me, they'd work really well on longer legs (I have a 28" inseam). These pants are mid-high waisted, and they stay in place really well. I can move freely in these pants and they don't move around, I can do a full squat and the waistband doesn't roll down. I bought these pants in black, and they are not see through at all. They're fitted through the waist and top of my bum, and then they have a loose fit through the lower bum and legs - very flattering, VERY comfortable. They also have pockets! These pants rock - they're exactly as expected/described, they're comfortable, and I think they look nice on, whether dressed up or down.

Not for us tall ladies-returning...too short I won’t give a bad review because the pants are quite comfortable. It’s a nice light weight material and the waist fit is good (ordered a large). So if you’re 5’7-5’8 or shorter, I’m sure you will like these pants.... but us 6 footers.... keep looking.... these are capris on me. I only selected fit as expected in the review because of the waist fit. I'm 5'3" 105lbs and the smalls fit pretty well, maybe just a tiny bit on the large side but it gives a nice relaxed fit look. I rolled the waistband down and think it looks super cute that way. These pictures were taken right after I took the pants out of the bag so they're a tiny bit wrinkled but that should be fixed after a wash. Love these pants!

Good fit and comfort I am very pleased with my purchase. I am 5'3" (160cm) and 105 lb. (47.7kg). The small size fits very well. The material is soft, but also durable cotton. It has a nice relaxed fit without being overly baggy and not fitted like skinny jeans. The length fits good. The cuffs come all the way to the ankles. I mostly wear my pants on my hips so the crotch tends to sag a bit, but when I pull them up onto my waist, the crotch seam fits as pants should with just enough room. I have a long waistline. I have NOT washed them in hot water and line dried only due to the high cotton count. I have not put heat to them so I don't know how much they will shrink. Some pants, when you bend you knees, they creep up at the ankles, but these have enough play and stretch in them that they stay where they are, even flexing the joints.As for any cons, the pockets are a bit bulky, but I can deal with a little bulk if it means I get pockets! I am using these for work. The zippers are a nice feature, but I can see the small plastic grips may be problematic. I read some reviews where they fell off in the wash. They have been through one cycle already. So far, so good!I included pictures for reference.

These straight leg sweatpants from the new airy collection are so cute and cozy.I feel like you could dress these up with a different style top and shoes. They could almost pass for dress pants depending on what you pair them with. Also very comfortable and relaxed fit. I am wearing the color splash white size small. The length on these seam to be a bit on the shorter side. For reference I am 5’4” 135lbs. They hit right at my ankle.

Loved them…until I realized they ARRIVED W A HOLE IN THE BUTT OF THE PANTS I tried these pants on as soon as they arrived & instantly fell in love w them so I decided to keep them on & wear them over to my friends house. Our plans included relaxing while we lounged around binge watching a show on Netflix & I had a less than 2 min walk to her house, so these were perfect. Unfortunately, less then 10 minutes after I put these on I arrived at my friends house, bent down slightly to take my shoes off & as soon as I did, my friend piped up & said “there’s a hole in your pants”. I wasn’t even halfway bent over when she said it. The hole is not in an easy spot to cover either. I plan on sewing it up bc I really do love these pants & they’re not only cute & comfortable, but they’re also the perfect weight for the weather this time of year (early fall). W/o the hole these would have definitely been 5 star pants, easy. The fit is absolutely perfect on me although they do run a bit large bc the small fits me amazingly & I’m typically a medium in pants. The measurements in the size chart seem to be accurate though so I absolutely recommend using those to choose your size. I decided to order a small opposed to a medium using the size chart measurements. I’m short waisted so I often have to roll the waistband of pants like this. I was so excited when I rolled this waistband & saw that the string used to tighten the waist was now on the part of the waistband that’s visible once it was rolled. It’s like the waistband was meant to be rolled. The pockets are still easily accessible w/ the waistband rolled as well. Not to mention that they lay flat w the waistband rolled or unrolled unlike so many other pants this style. I often find that the pockets bulge out at each side making you look bigger than you are. The fit is perfect imo…loose w/o being too wide legged & the length wasn’t so long that I’d end up stepping on them either. With sneakers on the length looked just right. The material used is also insanely soft, almost silky, breathable & just super comfortable. I was a bit worried about the feeling of the inside of these pants bc it’s described as a “a comfy looped interior…” & the close up image of the interior showed the looped design opposed to the soft fleece I usually try to get. I decided to take a chance after reading the glowing reviews stating that the interior was super comfortable. I was pleasantly surprised to find that I thought the interior was just as comfortable, if not more so, than I’d hoped based on the reviews I’d read. It has an almost silky like feeling against the skin that actually feels quite nice. In the past I’ve gotten pants & tops w a similar looking fabric that in some cases has caused irritations from rubbing against my skin it really is a shame bc these pants are absolutely amazing & I fell in love with them the moment I put them on, but I can’t in good conscience give them more than 3 stars bc they arrived damaged. W/o the hole in them, these are 5 star pants all day. For reference I’m 5’2, between 135lbs &140lbs w/ my weight fairly evenly distributed & a 29.5” waist. Also, I’m typically a medium in both tops & bottom & the small in these fits me perfectly.

Quality Material and Comfort These sweatpants are certainly well above average in their quality, and have a nice fit. I ordered small and I'm honestly not sure if an XS may be a better fit? I like the fit from the front, but as pictured from the side, these are definitely a baggier loose fit-which isn't bad, but just not sure if I could have gone with XS? For reference, I am only 5'1", a heavy118lbs today, and 28.5 waist. These are only slightly long for me but do work best with at least an inch platform underfoot at my 5'1“ height (or lack thereof). These photos are taken right after their first washing. I through them in on "warm" wash temp and dried them and don't really believe they shrink much, so don't count on that. If you like that looser baggier fit I'd recommend you order yournormal size. My 83 year old mom has also worn these, and while she weighs the same, her waist is smaller and hips slightly bigger (why didn't I get that shape?!) Interestingly, they fit my mom similarly to myself. Stated another way, I think the extra room in the seat/upper thigh area of this cut will accommodate both the larger waist/smaller hip and more shapely figures about the same. The blue color on these was great and they are very comfortable. Very good quality. Thought they looked like a nice cut to them in the advertising and they actually are as pictured with great quality fabric/weight to boot. Recommend!

These photos are taken right after their first washing. I through them in on "warm" wash temp and dried them and don't really believe they shrink much, so don't count on that. If you like that looser baggier fit I'd recommend you order yournormal size. My 83 year old mom has also worn these, and while she weighs the same, her waist is smaller and hips slightly bigger (why didn't I get that shape?!) Interestingly, they fit my mom similarly to myself. Stated another way, I think the extra room in the seat/upper thigh area of this cut will accommodate both the larger waist/smaller hip and more shapely figures about the same. The blue color on these was great and they are very comfortable. Very good quality. Thought they looked like a nice cut to them in the advertising and they actually are as pictured with great quality fabric/weight to boot. Recommend!

LOVE LOVE LOVE THESE PANTS! (This is the only pic I have of me wearing them but I’ll tell you that bending down in them is effortless!)

True to size.

Decent fit but colors ran horrifically in the wash and even stained later loads These fit nicely, but first I want to make sure anyone reading this is aware of what an ordeal it was to wash and clean up after these pants, as the dye ran worse than anything I have ever seen in my life. Full disclosure: I am not 100% sure who is the culprit, as I washed both this pair of pants and a new pair of the Rafaella lightweight satin twill ankle dress pants (also in black) at the same time. I wouldn't have noticed how horrifically they color ran due to the them both being black, except that I had also tossed a brand new light pink bra in a white mesh delicates bag in at the same time - that one's on me, I was just thinking "wash the new stuff before wearing" and not about color bleeding. I used Woolite detergent, and when I pulled the previously white delicates bag out, it was the blue/purple color of ballpoint pen ink (fortunately the bra inside was just a slightly more mauve-y dusty pink, not completely dyed). I soaked the bag and bra, and have gotten some of the dye out, and I washed the pants through two more cycles. Out of an abundance of caution, I ran the washer again in case dye was left in the machine, and put in an old white cleaning rag as a test. It came out purple. I ran it multiple more times with a new white cleaning rag each time, and they eventually got lighter and lighter purple. I ran a load of Tide Laundry Machine cleaner through, and then bought a box of Shout Color Catcher sheets to run through, and after maybe a dozen wash cycles across two days, I'm finally confident I've gotten all the residual dye out and have run a load of regular laundry through with no problems. I'm nervous to wash either pair of pants again, as I'm not sure if I'll have to go through this whole process again or if I've gotten out all the dye that's going to bleed. I've included pictures of my purple rag progression (the mesh bag is also in the photo, but that's after soaking and running it through the wash again), as well as the tags from both pairs of pants where you can see that they are stained purple as well (although it has lightened significantly from the first wash). I'm actually rather glad I had the bra/mesh bag in that same load; even though they got stained, I would have otherwise just tossed a load of regular clothes in next and ruined nearly my entire wardrobe. I left this same warning and photos on a review for the other pants' page as well. But let's talk about the fit of the actual pants! These are very nice, but not perfect pants. I really struggle to find pants that fit my hips and thighs but don't have a horrible gap at the waist - I usually end up cinching it in paper bag style with a belt. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and ordered a size 14 curvy (sized up from the suggested sizing chart, which would have put me in a 12: 32 waist, 43 hips). These pants fit and flattered my legs nicely, and the waist is a bit loose but manageable. I'll probably move the buttons/hooks over a bit to help. They are quite long on me and will need hemmed, even to wear with heels, but I expected that. I wish the material was a bit thicker - I would describe it as almost midway between dress pant material and tee shirt material. In black, this isn't as much of an issue, but in a lighter color I think it would be somewhat clingy and probably showing off bumps and dimples in ways that I wouldn't like. Overall, I'm satisfied with the pants, but I don't think they were worth the laundry debacle.

I’m 5’6, 156# with the menopause belly & big butt. These pants fit well. I haven’t washed them yet, but I think they will be fine. With the buttons, you can move them over tighter or looser! I showed you the unflattering pics so you could see the buttons, how the waist fits & the belt I added to hold the navy ones closed. Review of: Rammus Women's Fleece Lined Dress Pants with Pockets For fitting reference: I am 5’4”, long torso, 28” around the mid rise waist band (a few inches below belly button and natural waist), 36.5” around my widest section/hips. I am wearing a size small in the black color. The inseam of the pants measured slightly over 27”. Pros: + Waist band is comfortable and stretchy + Belt loops may be handy should I loose weight + Minimal gapping at the back of the waist band with squatting down/ bending + Size chart was accurate for my size + Fleece lining does provide some warmth, but not enough to enough to make you sweaty indoors + Pockets hold my iPhone 12 mini + Washed and dried well with no signs of wear after first couple of times through the laundry + Enjoyed wearing at least 5 times in the last 2 weeks of early November in the Midwest (considering getting another pair) + Fabric does seem durable and not easy to snag + Appears to be sewn well / seams look well constructed + Easy to move in Cons - Fabric is a bit shinier than appears in the model photos - Fabric makes some noise when walking if one leg brushes against the other - They look more snug around my upper thighs than desired, but I am pear shaped and that is where I carry my weight. I do tend to wear these with a longer tunic style top to cover the extra snugness in the same way that I would with leggings. To clarify, they do not feel overly snug or constraining in any way. I want to say they are a 4.5 star pant for me. Since half stars are not an option, I rounded up because I honestly keep reaching for them. Functional pockets + comfort = worth it!

Well…now I need them all…. Bend, sit, stand, they don’t need adjusted while wearing them. Buttery soft and smooth and WARM. Not too warm, just right. Stretch that snaps back and doesn’t lose its shape. And not bulky in the slightest. I was very comfortable in the office set at 72* and also had to walk .6 miles to drop off my car for a tire repair. It was 36* and sunny out. I was still comfortable. 5’3 and they hit me at the ankle (see pic) The waist band is smooth and flat. Sits just over my hip bones and is very comfortable. I would consider these mid-rise. They have a pretty sheen in the sunlight. Get these. Gift these! You won’t be sorry.

Unfortunately the two first ones that I purchased were slightly too big (I ordered size M for both) and they kept falling down when I was running, which was super frustrating. BUT I just love how comfortable these are and I also love the pockets (where I keep my phone while running and also a taser, cause you know, just in case) so I decided to give them another chance and got this purple pair in size S and these definitely fit A LOT better. When I tried on the S, it was a little hard to get them on because I kind of have a big booty (which is initially why I ordered the M), but once they were on they fit SO much better than the M sized ones. I guess I'll have to put these to the test the next time I go for a run, the M ones I think I'll just have for yoga and lounging around the house :) For reference: I'm 5'3, weigh about 125lb, my waist is about 26" and my hips 38" So for everyone who's gonna order these, DEFINITELY MEASURE YOUR WAIST!

Thick, comfortable, and POCKETS. I love these leggings. I bought a size Small, I'm 5'4 and about 120 lbs. The pockets are great, can fit phone, keys, etc and will not slide down!
