excellent fit on my tall, super plus sized frame; very comfortable I went with the size XXL because the size chart in the pictures states that XXL is size 20/22. I'm pretty much a size 20. I took my chance on this because sometimes things just don't fit right or feel right. I am SO glad that I got these- the sizing is perfect. I can tell it can stretch more (but hopefully wont need to). When they arrived, my initial thought was that they won't fit. But I put them on with ease. The polyester spandex blend is perfect. It is snug without being constricting. It is compressing without feeling like being encased like a sausage. My lower belly apron (flab) appreciates the additional support. High waist keeps the capris in place. I find that I do not need to hike them up. I can sit, bend, run around, and they aren't falling off. This is important because I am tall (5'10", 300lbs), and sometimes the way clothes are made are meant for shorter frames, or so called high waists roll, etc. So this pair of capris is rather perfect for me. Gusseted crotch lends to extra comfort, ease of movement, and not as much strain on the seams. The pockets are functional yet aren't noticeable when not in use. They lay flush. Surprisingly deep. The color gray, depending on lighting has more of a purple undertone. I like it a lot, even more so than the dull light gray I often see. I really like it paired with a black shirt. The material is thin enough to feel light and breathable, yet has plenty of coverage. I could make out my underwear line, but not my underwear. The mesh is not revealing; it looks more like a pattern. I find these to be very comfortable, and am pleased with the overall look, fit, and feel. As a plus sized person, it means a lot to find something that I feel good in. I am highly considering another pair or two in different colors. Again, very happy to have tried this, and now I have another brand I can count on.

Comfy, Thick, and Flattering! I've been wearing leggings non-stop since working from home; figure these RAYPOSE leggings will double as lounge-wear for now and I'll have them for when I finally start working out again as well, ha! The fit is comfy and not too tight: I'm 5'1", 105lbs and got a small (I'm usually an XS-S). The material is suuuuper soft and very thick; definitely squat-proof! Happy that these are actually high-waisted as well and come above my belly button. The cut is flattering, and would be more so if I was bothering to work out 🙈 Color is exactly as it appears on my screen: the dark gray is more of a taupe-y color. My only concern are the little holes in the fabric on the sides that allow for greater air flow and breathability. I'm just not sure if they will end up unraveling or tearing eventually, as they are just cut into the fabric and not finished on any way. So far they are holding up though, so I'm very happy with these super comfortable leggings! Would highly recommend! BTW- I got another pair for my mom: a medium, and she is 5'-1", 150 lbs and she says they fit great. They are snug, but this proves there is some flexibility in the sizing chart, since based on her weight she should wear a large. She said she thinks a large would fit, but they just wouldn't be as snug.

Pretty, not at all itchy I was hesitant to order these yoga pants because I've gotten several pairs recently that have large, itchy seams and itchy material. However, these looked soft and I liked the color so I decided to risk it, and I'm glad I did! They are very soft, almost like brushed fabric, and there are no large seams inside to dig into me. They're very comfortable! I like the mesh... it's cute but not too distracting or revealing. The pockets are deep and fairly vertical, so my Galaxy S10 fits (and stays put). I was also excited by the length-- they're almost long enough to come to the top of my sock (not quite, but close enough, and better than most on my 33.5" inseam). They fit well. I ordered an XL because I assumed they're Asian sizing, but they're not super tight and they have room in the waist (but I wouldn't size down because I have a booty and they might get see-through if I did). They're not see-through in this size and they feel great. They come to my bellybutton, so no muffin top but also not so high-waisted that they are up to my ribs (I hate that). These fit me pretty close to perfectly for yoga pants, and the color is really pretty. I washed them in cold and hung them to dry--they did fine. I wouldn't pay $50 for these, but as far as less expensive yoga pants go these are pretty great (no price is listed yet, so I can't factor value into my rating). I would definitely order another pair. For reference, I'm 5'10", 185 lbs, 36dd, 32 waist, inverted triangle/apple shape, wearing the XL.

Dark grey is more of a purple grey The color description for these leggings was dark grey, but in person they are a very purple-grey/dusty lavender color. My pictures I added with this review show the true color. I actually really love the color because it is very unique and I don't own any leggings this color! They fit true to size (I pot my normal size: medium and my measurements are hips: 40" and waist: 28"). The mesh material detailing on the sides adds interest and added breathability. The quality is excellent. The material is very smooth and lightweight but not see-through at all! So great for working out in. There is a good amount of stretch that make them comfortable and there is a good amount of support in the leggings so they hold you in well. The only downside to non-black colored leggings is that they tend to show any leg dimples, but don't let that defer you! Your legs are perfect and will look cute in these adorable purple leggings no matter what! If underwear lines bother you though be sure to wear the no-show kind to avoid any visible lines.

Excellent I ordered these since yoga pants are typically what I wear most days. I'm 5'4 115 lbs, and I ordered the small. They fit me perfectly, and I could probably even go smaller. They are a really smooth material, and I feel they are very comfortable. I love the mesh areas that go all around which helps make them more breathable. I have a lot of tummy support on top which I appreciate. They have nice big pockets that will hold a bigger phone. These are probably some of the softest yoga pants that I have. They are well made, and I like the look of the darker seams going around. I have no complaints, and they are very flattering on my body and I do recommend them.

I bought these for wife 5 ft 4inch and weight 140 lb and medium size was a good fit. These are comfortable to use while outdoor or exercise. Mesh on the side is a good idea. It fits well and stretchable material keeps it tight. Easy to wash as well. Overall quality of material is good and comfortable to use.

I love these Raypose High Waist Tummy Control Capri Yoga Pants. They are so soft and the material is luxurious and soft and stretchy. I got a size large and am happy with the fit. I am 5'9", 210lbs with a 32" waist and 42" hips. I highly recommend these awesome pants.

I’m 5 ft. 5 inches tall, 130 lbs. waist 27 inches. I got size small. The quality is nice and they are the right size, but it’s not very flattering (see last two photos compared to my burgundy designer leggings.) These Gray ones almost give me a muffin Top and kinda make my butt look elongated :(. They do just cut into me at the waist ever so slightly, just enough to change my shape in a not-so-good way.

These are really nice quality and I love the color, but they don’t fit my proportions.. I am 5’5, 105 and got a small but I should have gotten an x-small because they aren’t as snug as I’d like. The only thing I didn’t like is they are about 2 inches too short. The ankle part is too loose and not snug around my ankles. If I got the x-small they might have been even shorter. I do like that they are high waisted and go up to my belly button. Very flattering.

A little baggy These joggers have the softest, stretchiest material, they feel very high quality. I do think they run pretty big though, I don’t think they’re meant to be super fitted but they were baggy on me. I’m usually a size medium, ordered a medium and they fit like a large. I’m 5’8”, 160ish. They still fit somewhat similar to the way they fit on the model, but a little larger on me. If you want a more fitted style, I’d say size down one size. Other than that, they’re sooooo comfy.

Not quite as pictured The waist sits higher which I do like. The legs are fuller than shown on the model. I am slim, 5'5" 113lbs. The ties at the calf don't have a cinch, I had to use tweezers to untie. The cargo pocket is tiny and useless. I will return.

Better than expected I have 5'7" and 195 and at first purchased the XL. If you like a baggier fit then sizing up one would work but I exchanged for a L (which I am wearing in the picture) as they still fit very well and weren't so baggy. They're great for working out and are very comfy and have a little stretch to them. I've gotten multiple compliments and have bought another pair. I never leave reviews but these are worth it.

So comfy! Great material and great fit. I don’t like working out in skin tight leggings. These are lightweight and roomy. I’m 5’3” and 140# and a medium is a great fit. RBX is a great brand. I wish they came in more colors!

Very baggy but comfy Ok I am 5'9" 180 long torso 53 year old woman trying to not look like a balloon because I hide my menopausal stomach Well I did not succeed with these capri joggers I ordered xl shouldve went with my instinct and ordered L. The xl are very baggy. Being I am only heavy in the middle I look lost. And when they slide down my belly (and they will ) the capri is now an ankle. Hope this helps!!

Super cute & comfy Loooove these pants! I now own 3 pairs. I’m 5’3” and about 135-140 pounds.... the medium fits perfect. The Capri hits right at my mid calf. These are so light and comfy for summer!

I love these pants!! Fit/Stretch/Durable I bought these pants last minute the night before we left to go hiking because I discovered that my favorite pair of prana convertible pants was missing one bottom half of the zip off leg! Ugh. Cue overnight delivery options and scurrying to pick one. Well I am very pleased!! I am 5' 5 "1/2 and approximately 128 lbs I was unsure about buying the small or the medium but I like for my hiking pants to be a little bit snug because as they stretch out during the hike they slip down. And with the seat of the pant hanging low it actually puts more stress on your thigh (quad) leg muscles to 'fight' against the garment you're wearing if you can move freely. (True!!) So I went for the small because if the medium was too big and baggy I would've had to forgo wearing them. But the small fit great with elastic waist AND they actually make me look like I have a butt which is awesome because most snug ish pants just flatten my butt!! SORRY for pics , hard to take body selfies without a mirror. HaThe material is thin, that's ok because I can put fleece leggings underneath, but be able to use them for warm weather camping too. The quality feel is nice, the pockets are great and the stretch is amazing for climbing and squatting. Haven't tested the waterproofing yet, but as long as they repell some water I'll be happy. The leg opening is just big enough to go over top of my hiking boots. If I could change anything I'd make them 1 inch longer but they are NOT too short. Happy camper here. Hope it helps.Update**** wore them 4 days in a row in Feb, north Georgia mountain weather camping and hiking. Don't look worn or dirty, no damage. They are wind resistant to a comfortable temp and in colder conditions I just wore thin leggings underneath. Great budget pant. Look good enough to wear in to town too. Comfortable, stretchy for climbing. I'm going back for more and to hopefully get a convertible pair from this company!

I loved these pants! They’re lightweight and comfortable with so many pockets. They are very highwaisted and looser than expected in the stomach area but rolling them over allows them to fit better. I’m 5’4’’ and 145 pounds and got a medium for reference.
Great Pants I am 5'4" and the pants sit around my belly button which I prefer. If you are between sizes, definitely size up. The Large fits me perfect and the stretchy waist band is very comfortable.

The perfect pant exists! Wow, I’m so pleased with these lounge pants! My body type is short and stout with lots of curves and I didn’t know whether to get 2X or 3X. I got the 2X and the fit is perfect. I think they may be supposed to be capri length because I’m only 5’1” (but somehow I’m 260 lbs 🤷🏾‍♀️) and the legs fit me like they were tailored 😊 The waist is snug and stretchy with so much give that it kind of molds to your waist like a gentle hug. Not constricting at all, but definitely stays put so no riding up or falling down. The material is soft and ultra light without being flimsy. The pockets are wonderful. The solid color pants can easily be paired with a tunic, nice sweater, or long blouse and heels or flats to have the most comfy business casual work outfit ever. Also they are so soft, stretchy and lightweight, you can do yoga and Pilates in them. And of course they are great for lounging and sleeping. Great, no regret purchase. I will be ordering more in other colors.

C amel toe I like the value, but for me it fits weird in the front, I am 5 ft 115-120 got in small. The front bunches up very odd, no matter how high I pull the it looks like, that see pic :( Fabric ok, light weight, but the look of these leaves me self conscious about wear it

Super mega soft!! These joggers are by far the softest pants/joggers EVER!!! I couldn’t believe when I was trying them. I am wearing medium (135 lbs, 5’5) and they fit perfect. They have elastic waist and drawstring too. The elastic is not at all tight. Super comfortables. Very stretchy. 2 side pockets. And elastic on the ankles. The colors are nice and beautiful. But if you don’t have cellulite, or better yet, you don’t care about it, these are a MUST HAVE.

Perfect Pants for Curvy Girls/Ladies! These pants fit incredibly well and boy do they accentuate your curves. I now own 6 pairs of these pants you can see most of them in my other reviews. Here I am wearing the gray ones in size 22- I could have gone smaller but I have HIPS and since these are for work, I didn’t want to go tighter. These pants have a lot of stretch to them which is nice and they fit like zippered pants! In the picture you can see how they fit with and without a 3.5 inch heel so they are long - I’m 5’7.5 and they fit me perfectly with at 3.5 inch heel. If you are shorther than 5.7 then you may need to have the pants taken up at the cleaners. I also bout this broth on Amazon and it is the perfect compliment for these pants (they have 4 belt loops) which I need with the classic coke bottle shape, small waist and larger hips and thighs. Order your regular size for dressy occasions and to impress that special someone in your life (or soon to be). Order a size up for work and get the belt ($8.00) and adjustable to like size 40 I think, and you will be as happy as I am! Oh and I included a picture of the pants laid out after I washed them in cold water. So I recommend you have them dry cleaned instead as one black pair lost their sheen after washing (on the left) and the Calais color (far left) looked a little more brown than burgundy after washing, sadly. But that doesn’t stop me from wearing them; it’s just something I noticed and want others to be aware of. I also own the dark green pair; they each fit the same and look the same. The material of the navy blue pair doesn’t seem to be the same weight as all the others - they are lighter and thinner for some reason. So there you have it! These are the perfect pants for girls and ladies with real curves!

Thicker Material, good quality Update: The color drains tremendously, but the dark brown oatmeal color now looks more traditionally khaki. I’ve washed them twice a week, for over a month now and they do not shrink! I admit that I ordered the wrong size and that’s an error on my part. The legs are still too long and they get really lose in the waist due to the wearing in of the elasticity. Buy them a size or 2 too smaller; I will order them in a 22 regular (not wide) petite. I will be ordering more because they are very comfortable dress slacks and the material is strong and not flawed. I wear them for work in an office and I’m sitting standing, climbing stairs and they are very flexible. I purchased the Oatmeal color because Khaki was lighter than I like to wear in pants, I’m 5’4, 300 lbs and ordered 24W. I wish I would have ordered a size down and not the wide because they come under my bust. I have a large bottom and the seat is very large in these pants. I read some reviews that they shrink after wash so I’m hoping on it. The legs were long. I don’t plan to wear these with heels, only the non-slip sneakers pictured here. I didn’t purchase the petite so the 3 inches longer length is dragging the floor. I plan to wear a belt with these and I can hem the bottom. I ordered 3 but I think I’ll return them for a smaller size.

Comfy while looking professional I bought these pants based off reviews. I really really like these pants as they are super comfy. I normally wear 16, but I'm super short 4'11. So it's hard for me to find pants... but the 16 shorts fit perfectly. The only cons on these pants are 1. Looks like it has pockets but doesn't 2. Shows panty lines. And the reason I did not give it 5 stars is cause I bought 2 of these pants one in the grey and one in black both 16 short... for whatever reason the sent the grey ones short and the black ones regular, I did not return them however because I did not want to go through that hassle, I'm just going to have them hemmed. But if your looking for comfort as well as professional here ya go.

I’m upset that I spent $30 on a pair of pants I could only wear one time.

LOVE (size 24, measurements are 55/45/55) I ordered a size 24W (USA). My measurements are 55/45/54, I'm about 5'5-5'6, I wear a size 22 pants usually, around 260ish pounds (give or take like 15 depending on the day/week). I love these a whole lot. There are real pockets but they're sewn shut, a quick snip and they're functional. Thick, quality, crunchy material, pretty color, cozy fit (maybe a tiny bit big), perfect length for me. I was super pleasantly surprised that they worked well with the vest and dark charcoal jacket I bought. Wearing them to my abuser's trial, thanks for being as advertised.

Really comfy, great fit I'm 5'7" 250lbs usually wear a 20w-22w. Bought 22w. Fit great. A little long... bit totally workable. Better than too short... I hate ankle pants. These are comfortable, im able to work and maneuver around or sit comfortably. Easy to wear

I love these so much! I'm short (5'0 ft) and plump (200+) so it's always a struggle to find pants that I don't have to hem, look good on me and are comfortable. These pants are hitting all the marks. As soon as I'm done typing this review, I'm ordering more.

Fell in love I didn’t like them much when I first tried them on. I felt they were way too long. I’m in the over 50 age range, I’m 5’1” I think my inseam is 27 (or 28”) and about 200 lbs. I bought a 16 Short. They seemed to have snugged up after the cold water wash. I did put them in the dryer with the other laundry. I lack the desire (read “patience”, lol) to hang up clothes to dry. I probably should have went up a size, but I’ve lost 30lbs and am working still working on it. I also dislike it when pants become baggy/saggy during the day; and these did not. I did feel like I had to pull them up here and there through out the day, so I may wear a belt next time. The tummy control could be better, but I could also stop eating cake, so there’s that. Overall, I’m ordering another pair as we speak because I really do like how comfortable they are. I may try an 18 and return them if they’re too big. Hahaha, you see that? I made a joke! I’m not gonna stinking return anything (said in my ‘Granny’ voice) lol. I’ll just have to eat more cake so they’ll fit better.

Silver looks like Green!! I’m 5’7 145lbs and I order a size 10. They fit as expected, they are soft and look good always. I have about 4 colors and they are consistent in size and length. I took a star off because in person the silver looks like a light olive kahki green. It doesn’t look silver. I posted a picture with my leg next to a silver bowl so you can get some sense but the picture doesn’t do it justice. These pants are more comfortable than jammies, I prefer wearing them over jeans. This same company puts out another non tummy control version of these that are exactly the same except they are actually boot cut as opposed to straight leg. After ordering about 4 pairs of each, I realized this was the only difference between them. But they don’t tell you in the description or give you an option to buy straight leg vs boot cut. The boot cut is a wider leg, but the straight leg makes me legs look more slender all the way down and fit over the top of my heels too. I just wish they had an actual very light grey in size 10. They need an option to wait list until those colors come in again. The pretty light blue is also not available in my size. But otherwise I lovse these pants!

Cute, comfortable, perfect fit! I just received these and they fit perfect! I usually wear DressBarn secret agent straight leg pants in 2 petite. They haven’t had khaki since last year when I was still losing weight and I was in petite 4. They only have black, charcoal, and navy in the similar straight leg DressBarn petite pants. I was on a mission to find comparable pants and I searched every where! I found these and I was so excited! I waited a while and I finally decided to purchase. I could probably fit a 2 but they are for work and I don’t want them skin tight. These have a nice stretch and nice thickish fabric. I ordered these in 4 short! For reference I’m 5’0 and weigh 120 lbs. I’m worried I may put them in the dryer by mistake but I’m taking the risk! I already can’t wait to order them in more colors!! EDIT: I bought these in April 2019, its now August and I've been wearing every week and washing and drying along with all of my other clothes and have not had any issues with the seams at all!! *knock on wood* I would definitely recommend these to anyone looking for comfortable pants that work with a business casual dress code.

Fits true to size I got the silver in size 4. I am 5' 3.5" 124# with a mom pooch (4 kids) and I love these pants!!! They are stretchy and have tummy control top type thing going on in the top part. I own 3 pairs already. Black, navy and silver, and I plan on getting more!!!!😍
These are the BEST work pants EVER. I have 2 pairs now and I am ordering all the colors. They are SO comfortable and flattering. I got a size 2. I’m 5’5..125lbs.

Ordered a size 4...Overall A- I'm 5'5" and weigh between 115-120. I usually wear a size 2, and in some brands a size 4. Size usually 4 fits ok but gets saggy in the derriere after a few wears. With all my other pants, this is easily remedied by washing/heat drying. I'm a mommy who sits at a desk all day long and prefers eating ice cream to working out. My tiny Asian mother blessed me with a thin build but I still have all the imperfections that come with parenthood and it being the hiking off season aka the dreaded winter of the PNW. These pants definitely do a great job of hiding all flaws...self-consciousness be gone! However, the size 2 wasn't available so I thought... what the heck, perhaps the size 4 will shrink a little after drying. After reading so many reviews, I'm going to try a low heat setting then air dry as I don't want to destroy the fabric by overheating. When I first put them on, the shape was weird around my waist. They're a thick, stretchy fabric with a very light sheen, and they don't feel at all like those ultra soft jeggings (which is precisely what I was looking for). Perfect for wearing to work and true to color (really dark charcoal gray). They pull on with no button/zipper, yet have two full sized pockets up front. Without a shirt tucked in, the shape at the waist is baggy... Seems they are shaped to accommodate working up and around larger hips. Kinda bugged me at first, but I got over it. With a shirt tucked in, looks very professional and cute. The length at the ankle is a bit longer than I'd like, but I can live with it. Will definitely order the shorter pants next. These sit just halfway through my belly button and hips... SUPER COMFY! No plumber's butt (I'm not 21 anymore) and not overly high waisted. Doesn't ride up in unwanted places. I did notice a weird part in the stitching on the rear though... It's a bit shinier there and almost looks like it's coming apart. I did a minor stretch test and the threads are holding tight... Just SHINY in that spot. Even with my little complaints (borderline neurotic/OCD when it comes to clothing) I do like these pants and will try other colors, but definitely going with my usual size 2 next time around. Hope this helps!

Best Work Pants Ever!! I’ve been buying these pants for about 3 years. They run small, so I always buy 1 size up- I’m a size 4, so a 6 is perfect. They have a little stretch to them, so even if you gain a little weight, they’re fine. They hold their shape really well. When you wash them, DO NOT PUT IN DRYER, because they will shrink in the length and sometimes fray at the seam. As long as you hang dry, they’ll be fine. They look so professional and I always get compliments on my outfit when I wear these. I’m 5’6’”, 131 lbs and they are a great length, they don’t ride up on my ankles. I have these in several colors and I will continue to buy them as long as they remain $45. I’ve looked at Loft & Ann Taylor for similar styles, but the fit and look of these are superior, especially for the price.

Fit Looser than Expected Bought a size 2, I feel like a size 0 would fit my legs better but be too tight in the waist. I was hoping they would fit more like a ponte pant but they do not. Other than that they are good basic work pants. I got the olive color and they pretty much look like in the photo. I’m only 5’1” so I had to hem about three inches off so that they were actually ankle length. I might like to have these in oatmeal too.

Awesome I first bought these in a 16R, those were too long on me & after having lost weight I needed new ones anyways. Got these in a 14Short, I love the fit! I’m 5”6 and wear a size 14. The regular length would be perfect for taller ladies. I’ve worn them everyday for work with no issues. I love how good they make my booty look and having usable pockets!

Get these! I love these pants! They are perfectly stretchy and fitted. The quality is surprisingly good. Pulling them over my hips does take a little effort but once they are up the fit is perfect. I ordered a size 2. I’m 5’5”, 120lbs and I normally wear a size 2.

Get these! I love these pants! They are perfectly stretchy and fitted. The quality is surprisingly good. Pulling them over my hips does take a little effort but once they are up the fit is perfect. I ordered a size 2. I’m 5’5”, 120lbs and I normally wear a size 2.
Amazinf fit for self-love! I am buying a pair in every color, they make me love my body again. I’m 5’4 165lbs and size 14 just perfect wow. I feel lighter and shapely in all the right places!

Amazing Pants! Holy smokes! I love these! They were everything I was looking for. The fabric is stretchy, comfy but not too thin that you don't feel comfortable wearing to work. I have a booty and a small waist and am forever struggling to find something that fits well. I am 5'7" 135 lbs and very athletic. I got a size 2 and while I had to struggle to get the waist up around the booty, when I did, it was a perfect fit. No gaps, no pants slipping down and no expensive alterations. I am thrilled that I found such beautifully fitting pants at such a reasonable price. I cannot wait to get some more colors and styles.
Yes yes and yes. I am 5'6 and 160. I ordered two size 12's and they fit GREAT. They are the perfect solution for someone like me who needs to look professional but doesn't like the flare or boot cut.

Love this fit! Was a little hesitant to buy dress pants on amazon but these are AMAZING! I absolutely love the fit and the length. 5'9 and perfect length Will be buying in multiple colors for work!

Great fit! Material is nice enough for work and the pants are very comfortable and super flattering! Perfect for tucking in a blouse. I'm 5'6", weigh around 123-125 lbs and ordered size 6 and they fit perfectly.

DIFFERENT COLORS DIFFERENT SIZES First, I just came upon this brand and I absolutely love it! However I must caution you that your size may depend on the color you choose! I bought a red and black pair, both in size four. The red one "fit" but was likely too snug based on this color. The black were way too big but could be tolerated. As you can see they are "off" about 1 inch in the waist. So I ordered a size 2 in black and a size 6 in red. I am hoping the sizing difference is consistent within that color. Other than that I LOVE these pants. And for perspective I'm 5' 7" with a straight body type (which this brand is FANTASTIC with!) My measurements are about 27" inch waist and 34" hips. I clock in around 118 lbs. I've also been warned about shrinkage and wrinkles. But I've had a similar material with the same issues and I'm good with the fabric care. Well I hope so!

The perfect pair of pants DOES exist. I love these. I am 5’5” and 125 lbs. The photo is me wearing a 6 as everyone said to size up. The six fit great with a heel on as far as length but the waist was big on me. So, I bought some 2’s (and by some, I mean 6 pairs…) and they fit great, just a hair too snug for my preferred look for work as I pair these with blouses and blazers. (The 2s are great for all the non-work stuff). So, the 4s are the winner. And I will be buying at least 22 million more pairs, give or take. They’re not a super thin material which I love. They look and feel like a $90 pair of pants. They don’t wrinkle too easily and I’ve never had an issue with threads or holes or what not like some of the other reviews mentioned. If you’re debating buying these? Don’t just buy them, buy 22 million of them.

My go to brand for good fitting, quality pants. I have at least five pairs of this brand pants and they never disappoint. I can buy the same size in any style and they will fit. The pull on jeans are my single favorite pair of pants in my wardrobe. They run small so size up. I am 5’6” and 180 pounds. I wear 14 in most brands but a 16 in this brand.

Waist doesn't have a lot of give, but they fit like a glove! I ordered these pants in a 4 Short after reading reviews about the length. I am 5'2" and typically wear shoes with at least 3" heels. The length of these pans is perfect for me, as I don't like the bottom of my pants rubbing the ground. I could still wear these with flats without worrying about the length.Be warned that there is not a lot of give in the waistband, so if you have any type of butt/hips, you may have to shimmy them up and over. Once they're up all the way though, they are super comfortable.The material of these is lovely, and they look professional with the comfort of yoga pants. I will likely look to see what other colors/patterns I can order from this brand!

Skimped on the sizing since last time I bought these... 60 years old, 5’3” and 200 lbs, square shaped. So I own 11 pairs of Rekucci pants, size 18, boot cut. Purchased them over a period of months back in 2016-2017. I machine wash, air dry, just like the instructions say. The colored ones have all aged fabulously, no pilling or fraying at the seams, no loss of stretchiness. The white, ivory and silver Rekuccis all wore out about a year ago (frayed at the crotch and bubbling in the fabric where the stretch gave out). Since I had other pants, I didn’t replace them...until now. I ordered the Silver, in the same boot cut size 18. As you can see from the photo, these pants are cut just a wee bit narrower...and for us full-figured ladies, that extra inch in the waist and thighs makes a BIG difference in look, fit an comfort. Feels like the manufacturer is skimping compared to the ones I purchased two years ago. So - sorry—no more 5 stars. And no more Rekuccis for me. Bummer.

Pleasantly surprised Ordered my usual size, US 4, I'm just shy of 5'8" and about 140lbs. They fit nicely however I was expecting them to be longer. I would not mind another 1/2" or so in length. Fabric is stretchy and looks professional enough to wear to work. It is not soft or remotely similar to a cotton blend yoga pant. I am skeptical on the seams and how durable they will be after several washes. I paid $29.99 with Prime delivery and do like them but feel like the price is high for the product. If they hold up for a year I will boost the rating to 5 stars.

Amazing, breathable, and chic. I LOVE these pants. I have bought them in several different patterns and colors because they are fantastic work pants. They are surprisingly breathable and lightweight. They make your butt look a-maz-ing! The downside is that these pants are slightly long. I can not wear them without heels because they are long on their own and bunch up on the bottom. The photos show 5 inch heels. I am 5 feet 4 inches and 115 pounds. The pants also sit a little higher than I would like. They come up midway between the hips and the belly button. Overall great classy pants. I could hem one set a bit so they could be worn with flats.

Comfortable and long I ordered size 4 tall. I'm 5'6, 120lbs. My legs are long and skinny, I have no butt, but I have a belly. I ALWAYS need the long/tall option but these pants are actually TOO LONG! I will attempt to hem these myself and I'm ordering more colors in 4 regular.

Love!! Ladies - you were so right! I’m 5’3” 128lbs and ordered the 4P in deep plum. I love these pants. I’ve been on the hunt for something comfortable, but still stylish to wear on weekends. Waist is perfect - tight enough not to fall, but not restrictive.

Cute, stretchy and comfy! The bootcut leg on these stretchy slacks is flattering and great for boots, flats or heels. I’m 5’3” and they are a little long, so I need to wear heels with them, but that’s fine with me. I love these pants and will buy more! Join me on FB at Just Shop With Jackie!

Worky and still sexy 5'3" 118lbs. Got the short length and it was good. Flattering to the booty. Fit is sexy (returned a different pair that was tooo worky and frumpy). Material seems good, thick enough, stretchy, flattens the belly for my extra donuts days. I'm keeping these.

Comfortable and very good thick material Love how these pants look and how comfortable they are. Love the thickness of the material and how well made they are. I bought them in 4 colors and planning to buy more. The length is perfect; bought size 8 and I'm 5'6 weighing 152 lbs

So comfy!! These pants are wonderful! I am 5’6” 155lbs with 30” waist and 43” hip. I have got a sizeable caboose. The pants are stretchy so they’re comfortable but they also look professional (they fooled my husband). Definitely recommend!

Amazing fit!! These pants are amazing! I'm 5 feet tall and 116 pounds, usually wear a 2 and bought these in a 2. They come up pretty high on the waist and hold everything in! If you have a big butt and small waist you'll have a hard time pulling them up and over since there's no zipper, but once they're on the fit is great. I honestly like the fit of these better than the expensive Betabrand pants online, and the fabric is nearly identical. Wish there were even more colors and patterns! I tried the houndstooth because I thought it was a burgundy color print, but it turned out to be brown so I returned those.

Great for fuller lower body-no waist gap Ordered a size 10...very true to fit. Great for a fuller lower half...nice contour to my back and lite hold at my midsection...hits a good place...1-1.5 inches above my bellybutton. I wear a size 12 jeans...I’m 5’4...had to turn the botttoms under once but pants still have a clean line down....these are worth getting tailored when I get the chance. Very comfortable when sitting...the material is soft and thick can’t wait to get another pair.
I'm so impressed! Best office pants ever! High quality, definitely a 5 star product. I'm 124 pounds and 5' 6". Ordered size 2 and they fit perfect! Will order another color soon.
I'm so impressed! Best office pants ever! High quality, definitely a 5 star product. I'm 124 pounds and 5' 6". Ordered size 2 and they fit perfect! Will order another color soon.

I bought these pants hoping for quality professional pants for work. I bought the burgundy pair and the black pair. I was going to buy a size up due to some reviews but decided to stick with my size. I'm glad I did. They fit perfect. There is a review someone has left regarding sizes, cut, colour, and fit and their review was SUPER . They were correct to mention the secret figure pants do taper back in at the waist band. This could bug some people. I used the size chart and I was 1 inch over the measurement for waist, prompting me to think I needed an 8 but the 6 still fits perfect and an 8 would have been too big. I'm 5'3, 150lbs, and the length of the regular was perfect. Definitely going to be ordering many more colours.

Spanx vs these....I choose these Hesitated. But I have spanx and these now.....dont notice a difference. I wasn't sure if I wanted to try these due to some of the reviews saying that they received thin leggings But I had a completely different experience I was nervous I opened the package it was very nice packaging pull them out they were thicker not too shiny the same day I also received my spanx that I had ordered and honestly I cannot tell the difference. I was going to return these until I tried them on and was like these are the same there is no reason so I'm now going to keep these and telling you at least try if you receive a bad pair obviously return them but I had a good experience and these are great option if you are looking for something less than half the price of spanx. I Am 5 5 about 135 pounds in I ordered a medium they fit perfect I can't complain about anything I love the length it's not too much but it's just right. I had an option to wear these or spanx today or these and I chose these. I did not receive a free pair but I'd love to ;) I just wanted to review being I was so nervous. Hopefully it helps the next

Spanx Dup! Very similar to the Spanx faux leather leggings. They are definitely longer in length compared to the Spanx petite and the Spanx material is just slightly silkier than the Gongs. The Gongs are a little less constricting as well. I really thought I would like the real deal better but I ended up returning the Spanx! In all my pictures, the Spanx are on the left and Gongs on the right. The seams are the same too. I ordered the Gongs in medium which are true to size and the Spanx in medium petite. I don’t love the long length of the Gongs nor the price but still cheaper than Spanx. I’m 5’3 and 145lbs. Happy with my purchase overall :)

Pleasantly Surprised These faux leather leggings are a diamond in the rough!!! They are the most comfortable pair you'll ever find. To me, they are a million times more flattering than Spanx & are Way more comfortable. They are extremely soft on the inside with warm fabric which is great bc the leather doesn't stick to your skin at all. They aren't overly shiny; instead they have the perfect amount of shine to keep them classy & fashionable without being childish or costume like. I LOVE these! I will 100% be buying them in every color. Also, they are thick enough to where they are not see through whatsoever, & they Do Not show your panty lines either!!!! Seriously a 30/10. Everyone needs these! I'm 4'10" & 115 lbs with a 26 inch wait & a small fits me like a glove. There is still stretch to them too, so they aren't skin tight by any means. They are around 3-4 inches too long, but I just tuck them into my boots & you can't tell. Even if I were to wear them with sandals though it doesn't look bad at all to have them a little bunched up around my ankles. If that bothers you, just fold them inwards & they'll look like the perfect length!!

Love these!! EDIT: I came back to buy a second pair at the price I paid for them two years ago ($40), only to see that they’ve since DOUBLED in price!! I thought they were pricey but worth it at $40, over my dead body will I pay $80 for a pair of stretchy pants. No thanks. May as well buy real spanx! Took away a star from my initial review because they aren’t THAT amazing! Go get some designer pants for that price!! I’m 5’3” at 130lbs.. I went with a Medium based on reviews (I usually go for a small) and I love them! A little long but it doesn’t look bad (posted an up-close pic of the extra length at my ankles). They aren’t overly shiny, more subtle, which is what I was going for. Super comfy! Definitely a true dupe for other leggings I’ve been eyeing!! Hopefully they hold up with washing.

Perfect Spanx Dupe - Obsessed! LOVE THESE! I’m 5’7, 132 pounds, and I ordered these in black in a size Small. They fit so well and are flattering in allllllll the right places. They are just a little long so I do fold them under so they sit right above my ankle. Such a staple for your closet! You could wear them with fall/winter outfits as I have styled in my photo, or you could even do an oversized t-shirt that is knotted in the front with some white sneakers! So for me, it was hard to dress them up too much because of the waist band being so thick (although the thick waistband is nice to hold in the tummy area), they felt a little more casual in that sense, versus a pair of black leather pants that have a zipper and a button. Either way, highly recommend these, they look just like the Spanx, but half the price! Also, they have washed up very well and look just as they did when I first purchased them :)

Feels like butter! So comfy. Update to 5 stars. I LOVE these leggings. I wear them for going out, not exercise. I typically wear them with a big chunky sweater or a cute shirt. I recently wore them for a tattoo session and they were perfect. I have chunky ankles so I tend to wear them like dancer's pants around my heel. I like the look but it may be strange to others. Don't care. I love them so much!! I plan to buy more. Original review... These leggings are CRAZY comfortable! I got a Medium because that's my go-to size with leggings and though these fit like a dream once they were on, I did snap a thread on a side seam in the process of getting them on my body. For reference, I'm 5'6" and about 157lbs. Bigger lower half but I wear Medium stretchy pants and size 6 jeans. I would have given these 5 stars if not for the weak thread. (Over the last several months and multiple wears, that broken thread hasn't been an issue or unraveled, thus increasing my review to 5 stars.) I tried a few other pairs and these by far were the best. The front is oddly flat but did not bunch up or look odd like other leggings of this style have done. If you are on the fence, try them!

Best Spanx leggings dupe! I bought these after hearing wonderful things from friends and reading the reviews. JUST WOW! I was not expecting to LOVE these! I did go up 1 size (medium) from my regular size (small) to make room for my booty after reading they run small in the reviews. But I love the look, fit, feel, and especially appreciate a NO FRONT SEAM! Perfect shade of black, and I like the slight shine to them for the faux leather look! A spot on dupe for Spanx leggings! I not only enjoy wearing them for a more dressy look, but also find myself wearing them around the house to lounge in because they are THAT comfy! Perfect length to them which is a tough find for me (being 5’7) but these hit perfectly right at or just below my ankle depending on how I distribute the fabric. The high waisted band hits just right; a couple of inches above my belly button. Tall girls like me will love these. I'd size up!! I'm 5'9 and 150 lb. I wear a size 10 in jeans, but a 6/8 in dresses. Small on top. I'm mostly legs, and straight shaped, not hourglass. I like them a lot! I wanted a nice loose fit around the waist, but to be able to wear them with a bodysuit and still be cute. they have regular pockets, and then on the left they have a little hidden zipper pocket that could fit a key fob. The material is great for summer. I agree that it's similar to a t-shirt material, but my cellulite does not show through. So it's kind of the best of both worlds. I'll definitely be keeping these. I also got a size large in black. they feel great around the waist, not too tight. And they are a little slimmer around the thighs. I would say the large would be a true fit for me. I'm keeping them too :-) I put up pictures of both. Update: they bag out around the bum and get really saggy after a couple hours, and they don't regain their shape! Returned.

Not 100% Cotton. Otherwise, Finally Long Enough. I am returning these because although they are advertised as 100% cotton, they are not (see picture). The body is 100% cotton, but the lining is 80% cotton/20% spandex. Sellers need to be accurate with fabric descriptions. Some people (me!) are sensitive to spandex/elastic fabrics, and an otherwise deliciously nice cotton feel becomes a constant irritation. Now the fit: I am 5'8", 150 lbs. with long legs, and the 34" inseam was actually too long, and bunched at the ankle. The waist was a tiny bit snug, but OK; and the leg width runs slim, not quite leggings-like but close. Additionally, a label describes the material as UPF 50+ sun protective.

If you're tall, these are for you! These are AWESOME. These are a LIGHTWEIGHT sweatpant material - not a light, thin cotton and not a fleecy-lining that you'd think "sweatshirt" with. They're thick enough to be warm and comfortable and not see thru! Not only do they have POCKETS!! they actually have a little extra zippered pocket! Please excuse my swollen belly from surgery - it's making the waist area a bit more snug. I was suggested an XL, but the 34" inseam was only available in a L, so I figured I'd risk it, and I'm glad I did. Size up for a roomier fit, go with your normal size for a more trendy fit. It fit my hips just fine and the material feels good for the price, unfortunately it just didn’t fit in the other areas.
Good quality pants These pants make me look like the androgynous professional that I was meant to be. 5’2.5” 125lbs size 4 fits great.

Almost perfect These pants are a near dupe of the old navy mid rise pixie full length pant. Great length (I'm 5'7), and four REAL (albiet small) POCKETS! I say nearly perfect because there are some minor flaws. They are not quite as stretchy and comfortable as I had hoped. They are slightly too high in the front which makes them bunch in an unflattering way when seated. They are slightly too low in the back so when you sit there is definite whale tale potential if you're not careful (might not be an issue for those with smaller butts). That said, they are better than 90% of dress pants I have tried, and cheaper than nearly 100%. If they don't fade or wear out, I'll probably buy more colors.

Better than I expected I was pleasantly impressed with these pants. I am 5'6", 29" waist, 40" (39") hips, 136lbs. These feel a bit like parachute pants. They are 17% nylon after all. But they do have a good amount of stretch to them. I wish they were a little more supportive in the rear and that might have been achievable maybe one size down if I thought they were really stretchy enough for that sort of feature but that's just wishful thinking for a forty-something y/o whose assets aren't where they used to be. The waistband is very comfortable, the length is just where I want it, and I think they look really good on my body. As for my measurements, I am 5'6" but I have short-ish legs and a long torso - specifically my "rise". Like, high-rise pants fit me more like mid-rise because there is over eleven inches between my crotch and my bely button. I mention this because these pants are a little tight in the crotch for me and have a little camel toe effect but the waist is so forgiving, I can adjust and pull them down whenever I feel them creeping up in there;) Plus they are black and it's not noticeable. I don't think that would be the case with lighter colors though which is a bummer because I like these pants so much I'm tempted to buy them in all the colors. Anyway, lots of reviews complaining about how they don't hold up to washing but it clearly states that these pants are best dry cleaned. I never dry clean anything so I'll probably wash on cold in the gentle or hand wash cycle and hang to dry. I included front (heeled and flat footed), back, and side pics because I know that even someone with the same measurements as me may have a different shape. Hope this info helps.

Not petite If you were shorter than 5 foot five or built with the petite frame. These may be too long for you I have no issues with it, but I am more curvy. A lot of scrubs are joggers. Have a petite option. That way it’ll come up above the ankle a little. These do not. I ordered a large and I typically wear large in all scrub pants. Size 10 in regular jeans. I probably could’ve gotten a medium! However, these will be very comfortable for work! Good quality material not too thick! I appreciate all the pockets !

Comfortable Flowy Pants After ordering several pairs of this style pant and returning for various reasons, I wasn’t getting my hopes up but these were perfect! I ordered the small and the fit is perfect (5’4” 120ish) the waistband is not tight, I’ve got a little fluff right now and you can see from my pics they hit right above my ankle, I bought these to take to Greece in the hot weather and they were perfect (some places your legs must be covered, no shorts) the fabric is not sheer like a few pairs I tried, it’s a nice weight, definitely more cotton than linen and they hang loose like a skirt, pockets are nice and roomy. These seem very well made, they do need to be steamed after laundering, I laundered in cold water wash and hung outside to dry so I didn’t find that they shrank but again I didn’t put them in the dryer. I would definitely buy these again and I’m sure I will in some of the other colors, you can style with flip flops, sandals or tennis shoes and all look cute.

Comfortable, thin and long Okay I am short, only 5'3". I need to wear platforms with these! Fabric is soft, stretchy and comfortable but a little on the thin side. You can see underware lines easily. Really great stretch and deep pockets on each side. Good for the price. I got olive and it truly is olive but the photos make it look grey.

Wonderfully comfy, attractively cut, pleasantly priced I ordered these to wear as comfortable pants to wear around the house and to wear out with tunic tops. They are super comfortable and stretchy. I can see already that I’m going to be living in these leggings. They aren’t compression leggings, but they are a reasonable thickness, neither thin nor thick. I find boot cut pants more flattering on me than straight legged. These have a distinct, but not weirdly huge flare. I used the size chart, figuring that if it said a small was a 26” waist, then my 28-29” waist would do fine. It does—perfectly, without being snug at all. I’m only 5’ 1” and so wasn’t surprised that I’m going to need to hem them up at least 2 ½ inches. So, I’m guessing the size small is an ideal length for someone who is 5’ 4”. I’m not sure why these leggings were advertised as “tummy control.” They aren’t, although the wide stomach band (which you can see in the ad’s photos and video) is made of a double, rather than single, layer of fabric; so maybe that’s what the manufacturer had in mind by “tummy control.” The fabric is not slick, so if you have cats, don’t be surprised if you find cat hair sticking to it. I’m really happy with these leggings, which are so comfortable I don’t want to change out of them!

Soft and comfy First, I have never owned golf pants so I am unsure of the material typically used or how they would feel So these are mega comfy. The length was perfect considering I am 5’2”. I did roll the waistband as that is my preference. I believe that the legs may taper a bit. I am not a fan of that. I have bigger calf muscles so I avoid any pants that taper if it’s not leggings. However, I state “think” because I could tell if it tapers or if I just need to wash them first. They are in the laundry pile now so I will update my review if I find that they just needed to be washed These pants have plenty of stretch to them. I feel like they cling, but again, I am not used to golf pants so find a review with a woman who golfs. I only got these because my husband wants to take me out sometime to play. I may as well try to look nice as it will be a disaster otherwise!

Stretchy; Great for Lounging and Athletics My wife is 5’ 3” and weighs around 100 pounds. She went with the small instead of extra small because she was concerned about the fit, but she’s happy with the pants at this size. Instead of them being ankle length, they fit like any other pair of pants. So if you want a normal fit, size up. The pants are super stretchy and fit well. They’re great for working out, a round of golf, or just lounging around the house.

Thin Material - See-Through for Lighter Colors -UPDATED I was excited to try on these pants since I live in Southern Texas and it gets really hot and I thought these linen pants would be a great addition to my wardrobe.They came well packaged and the stitching on them is really nice. The problem is when you put them on you can see right through them due to how thin the material is. It's unfortunate since they are cute pants (a little tight around the waist/butt area and perfect fit around the legs) and comfortable. Too bad I can't wear these out in public.Update:The company reached out to me immediately and offered to send me a pair of the darker-colored pants to help resolve the transparency issue. A pair of black pants arrived the next day and they are perfect. The fit is nice (they are a little long but I am just shy of 5' tall so that's expected), the fabric is thin and breathable so it's great in hotter weather. With the darker color there was no transparency at all in the fabric. I would highly recommend the darker colors of these pants for anyone looking for some comfortable and cute pants to wear in the summer months.

I chose these trousers in an S white. Not surprisingly, they are a bit transparent. Not a deal breaker. For reference, yours truly is 5’1”; thus, the inseam is long. This can be remedied by 1 1/2 to two-inch heels or wedges.I love the wide waistband. It lends a polished look when tucking in your top. These pants are scratchier than 100% linen but the relaxed style makes up for it. Overall, a nice addition for Summer/Spring.

Linen Pants I am 5'3" tall about 110# I ordered a size Medium. They fit very well in the hips and waist. The length is a bit long but that is remedied by folding over the waistband. The pants are really comfortable, like sweatpants comfortable. They are also NOT see thru. The pocket are deep and usable. As you can see in pics they do absolutely need to be ironed. They would be comfortable for traveling and hot days. If your office was casual they would be appropriate. I like them to just pull on to look nice but be very comfortable..... When it is hot outside and chilly inside. The makeup of the material is 80% cotton and 20% Flax in the color black.

NVGTN dupes at less than half the cost! These pants are amazing! I absolutely LOVE them. I have a couple NVGTN leggings with the smile lines and these are a comparable dupe at less than half the price. I own the dark blue pair (which is actually a light blue, not sure if this was a mistake or the colors are listed incorrectly on the ad but I was hoping for the dark blue ones) and I also have the dark grey pair. The leggings have a fairly tight compressive stomach, but the material on the glutes and legs are much stretchier and fits curves well. They are not see-through, thank god! I hate that. They hold up on my heavy leg days and I feel confident and beautiful in them. They definitely accentuate the curves, the smile lines you need to line up under your cheeks for the best effect, but once you have them where you want them they don't move around too much. I am 5'10 tall, 160 lbs, 40-27-42 and I got a size large. I usually wear a medium in leggings, but I got the medium in the purple and rust orange colour and they were not the same material as the grey and blue ones. The material was much thinner and the orange were completely see-through, as well as the color of them in the ad are a beautiful rust red-orange, but the actual leggings are neon orange! Like a highlighter. I hated the color, I wanted them to be rust orange because that is so unique and gorgeous. Oh well. I returned those. I would definitely recommend these to anyone who has been eyeing the NVGTN leggings but don't want to pay $50 for one pair of leggings. 10/10

TALL GIRL FRIENDLY, SQUAT FRIENDLY I have the all black pair and I LOVE them! I have 3 pairs and plan on purchasing more. They’re not see through at all. They run slightly small (which I prefer) and they snatch my waist! Im 5’10, 180lbs and they go all the way down to my ankles (honestly they go past that but I wear long socks so no need to pull them down further) I can do a HIIT workout + weight train and dont even have to pull these leggings up! Lunges, sprints, and squats are no match for these durable leggings! I highly recommend purchasing. Im back almost 2 years later and I just wanted to say BUYER BEWARE- YOU WILL GET ATTENTION WEARING THESE lmao. Theres no scrunch on the butt (which I like!!) so if you’ve been blessed below the belt, these will emphasize what you’ve been gifted lol. Still no complaints with these leggings. I’ve probably owned 10 pair over the past 2 years and 3 of them ripped on the inner thigh (normal for thick thighed ppl so not a bad thing tbh..) and they ripped after MONTHS of wear + washing and drying with high heat (also they dont shrink!) My least favorite place to wear them at is the airport cause they’re not the thickest pair of leggings so I would still be freezing even though my legs were fully covered. In summary, great pair of leggings! I still wear mine to this day :)

Good sweats for tall gals. These are soooo overpriced for what they are. It’s just regular sweats that most people can buy for $20-$25 but of course since we are tall we have no choice but to pay the “tall tax”. They are good, comfortable sweats. Very soft, not itchy. They have nice pockets with zippers. Hopefully they hold up in the wash. At first, I ordered the charcoal color, but it was not true to color. They were much darker in person. The heather gray ones are true to color. My only other complaint aside from price is that these would be a lot better if the waistband was an inch or two lower. High-rise looks great on jeans that pull everything in. However on sweats it does not look good. Of course I can roll the waistband over, but it just flips right back up with movement. I’m mid size. 5’10” and have 43” hips

Totally different from the first pair I ordered I LOVE these joggers. LOOOOOVE. But make that lovED, past tense, because I just ordered a new pair (since my original ones were getting worn out with so much wear) and they are NOTHING like the first ones I received nine months ago. I’m 5’8, 115 lbs with a 25-inch waist and I first ordered these in navy blue and light grey in size XS with a 32-inch inseam. They were sooooo soft and comfy-cozy (they looked and felt like cotton, per the description and label), fit PERFECTLY, and were ultra flattering. This new pair, on the other hand, feels super stiff and plasticky by comparison (not cottony, at all), is way thinner, and has a freaking huge waist. (The waist is so huge I checked the label to see if maybe I’d been sent a small instead of XS, and I even ordered yet ANOTHER pair in the hope that maybe it was just a fluke.) But no, the pair that followed was just as big, synthetic-feeling, and generally disappointing as the pair that preceded it. I am massively bummed because these are (were) my All Time Absolute Favorite Joggers Ever, but now it appears the vendor has replaced what was once a high-quality product with a cheaply made substitute. I don’t know if all sizes and colors have been replaced with inferior materials, inconsistent sizing, and misleading labeling as regards fabric content, but it sure appears to me that this is the case with the navy blue version. Sigh.

I just wish I didn't have to wait so long to get them only to return them again. It's a shame... If I knew they'd stay this way then I wouldn't mind fixing up the holes.

Almost perfect If you’re looking for sweats for tall people, you probably found these. Here is my detailed review of these sweatpants. I am in between 5’8”-5’9” for my height. I followed the instructions they gave to measure your favorite pair of pants that fit well for the inseam measurement, my pants gave me a 31” measurement. Since the guide that was posted in the product listing says for heights in between 5’6”-5’9” should order 32” inseam that was what I ordered. I was hesitant to do this because the jeans I measured for the 31” inseam can bunch at my ankles, but I don’t mind that and it allows for full coverage when I bend my knees, so I figured I would trust the seller. They ended up being too long, which was a pleasant surprise since every other sweat stops at least 4 inches above my ankle. I would personally exchange them for a 30” inseam, if I liked the quality of the sweats. Let me talk about the quality of the sweats. The seams of these sweats make me want to take them off immediately. I may just be picky with my seams, but this is my review after all. The seams, specifically on the inside of my leg are just itchy, not unbearably itchy, but enough to notice the itch and notice that you are wearing pants. I love my sweats to be soft and cozy, not itchy. The material they use for the sweats is also just kind of thin. Not thin enough to were you can see my underwear, it just feels cheaply thin. The ends of the drawstring on the waistband is also just a cheap plastic cover. I also did not notice online that the back pocket is so small and that there is only one pocket in the back. I personally only use front pockets in my sweats, I see no point for a back pocket, especially one that is so small as this one is. It doesn’t even fit my whole hand and I have small hands for someone that is 5’8”/5’9”. I think these pants would be better if they just removed the pocket in the back. I also want to point out that the sweat just kind or gathers weird on me near my crotch and you can see that in the photos that I attached. Also why include a front seam? It’s just grounds for camel toe. What are some positives of these sweats?These sweats look extremely flattering on, I just wish they were a nicer quality because I am not going to wear a sweat that itches my legs. I personally love a thicker waistband and I would describe this waistband as perfection, it’s not itchy, it’s comfortable, and it's nice and thick. The zipper for the pockets also seems to be really nice, not the nicest zipper, but good enough. The front pockets are also nice and deep. Are these the worst pants I have ever tried? No. Am I going to keep these pants? No. I personally would rather have a sweat that feels nicer on my leg than one that covers my whole leg.

Disappointing I read the reviews about this product prior to ordering. I ordered the tan, black, and white pack in size XL. I normally take a size 12. According to the size charts, a large is 12-14, and an XL is 16-18...so I took the other reviewers' advice and sized up a full size. When I received them, they looked huge and way too long. However, as the other reviewers mentioned, I tossed them in the washer and dryer on hot water and high heat. They definitely came out shorter. I took them out as soon as they were done. They are soft like a jersey t-shirt. I expected them to be flimsy. Actually, they are a little heavier weight than I expected...definitely not see-through. I wanted to use these as a combination of indoor/outdoor pants, lounge pants, and pajama bottoms while hanging out at home during the pandemic. I am 5'6" and 160 pounds. Unfortunately, these pants have no spandex or anything to help hold their shape. So, when you wear them, they stretch out on you...get baggy in the knees and wider everywhere. It is not an attractive look. I now would recommend buying your regular size if you choose to buy these and wash/dry them on LOW. They might end up being a little long, but they can always be shortened. I would wash/dry them at least a couple times before I altered them though. If you get your regular size, they will fit better and not "grow" on you as much as they stretch out. Actually, I'm sorry I purchased these pants. I am just using them for pajama bottoms.

I wear them constantly though! So I buy a new pair when that happens and have no regrets! I would be so sad if they ever stopped selling these.

Serviceable but Don't Love It I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

Cute and comfy corduroys! This pair of corduroy pants is very comfortable. They're lined on the inside and feel breathable. I really like that they come with front pockets and belt loops. Nice for summer. As someone who practically lives in jeans, I’m always on the lookout for something lightweight to wear when it’s warm. These cropped, gauzy pants looked like a great option, so I selected the neutral khaki pair. Here’s a list of what I liked and what I didn’t.What I liked:• The pants are a cotton/linen blend which makes a soft, lightweight fabric that keeps you cool.• They are washable on a gentle cycle in cold water. I hung them to dry, and they looked ready to wear afterwards.• The color didn’t fade.• The length was great and ended right above my ankles.What I didn’t like:• According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart and the Amazon recommendation, I selected a size large (my usual size). On me (5’6”, 185 pounds, size 12), these pants seemed to run a little small. The waist was comfortable, but they fit slightly small through the hips. As a result, I didn’t get the casual, flowy look that I was wanting.• As much as I love pockets, I don’t like the ones on these pants. They are positioned on the outermost sides of your hips. These pants probably would have looked and fit better without them.I tried these pants with both a cropped t-shirt and a long t-shirt. Because of the fit through the hips, I didn’t care for the look on me when I tried them with the cropped shirt. I preferred the look of the longer shirt over the pants. It created more of the casual, flowy look that I was hoping for. I wore these pants for a day of errands and was delighted with how comfortable they were. I was secretly hoping that the fabric would stretch out through the hip area, but it didn’t (maybe a tiny bit).Overall, these are a nice, lightweight summer pant. I might consider sizing up if you want them looser through the hip. Additionally, I’d probably remove the drawstring, so you don’t see the bulky bow underneath a longer shirt. I’m giving these pants four stars because of the minor sizing issue and the unattractive pockets.

Great color & comfort ~ Size small = fairly big + high rise Just got a compliment on these from someone in my house & she is glad to take these off my hands as they're pretty massive on me ~ Hip = 45" / Front rise 13.5" / Back Rise 18.5" I am 5'4 and pulling up to my belly button I still have 2' left hanging down in the crotch (and also must use the drawstring even to get them to stay as the elastic is also made for someone a bit larger). So it's not a fix to make them look tighter/more fitted just pulling them up unless you are taller with a wider hip. I range from xs to 4/6 all depends but these to me feel like a medium or like one of those very generous vanity sizing smalls. Also just realized the knees stretch so I get the kangaroo look like I'm bending my knees when I'm standing straight. The front panels of fabric down the legs are for some reason narrower than the back, so essentially you can see both inseams & outward seams from the front & not sure what that's doing to the silouette & wear. The wide hip takes away from the wide leg because it's almost seems like it's tapering, even if it's the same width.. there's just a whole lot more upper fabric + rise for me than I'm personally used to in a pant. Nice saturated teal just as in the ad, very drapey and linen-y to some extent (like linen washed a lot and blended with something else) There is no materials tag in the pant, only a brand tag with size & care tag on reverse of it. If you got a lot of hip, are preg or looking for a not-too restrictive short pant with a wide leg & high rise, give em a try! :)

Super comfortable and look like real pants! ***edited a year postpartum and these are some of my favorite work pants. I'm around 170lbs** The product page is unavailable at time of review, so I can't attest to the monetary value of this product, but I LOVE these pants!!!!! They actually look and feel like a pair of maternity jeggings I have. They have real pockets on the back, so they look like legit pants and not yoga pants (I'm going to try to get away with wearing these suckers to work). The material is a little on the thicker side (not running legging material, thicker than that), but not too sweltering or uncomfortable. I like the boot cut style as well. I feel like this style is more flattering and slimming if you have wider hips (like I do) than the typical "skinny" style that is so popular. These remind me a lot of an active/lounge pair of pants I have from old navy. I like the high rise wait to help tuck in the mom tummy. These are going to be perfect for postpartum wear! The length is great on my as well, not high waters, but not dragging the ground when I have tennis shoes on. For size reference (ignore the preggo belly in the pics...I pulled the waist up so you can see where it's supposed to sit, but not comfy on the bump...will be GREAT once baby comes out for some tummy support): I'm 5'5", currently 185 pounds at 36 weeks pregnant (expect to be around 170 for awhile after baby comes out). I typically wear a Large in work out leggings and these are an XL. I would say these fit true to size. They fit really well on my pregnancy body, which is about 40 pounds heavier than my non preggo body. These will fit great for the first few months postpartum, but then I'd most likely need to size down to a Large. Would absolutely recommend!

If you are an owner of an animal with hair, be prepared to have a lint roller.
