I can't stand the saggy crotch I bought these in an XL last year when I had gained the "Covid 20". At 5'6" and 180 that seemed right. They were very flattering, but the crotch wouldn't stay up. I was constantly tugging to pull them up. I thought maybe the legs were tighter than they should have been and that was pulling them down. I put them away for another day. Now, I'm 163, hips 42. Same problem. Still flattering, but awfully uncomfortable when the crotch sags. The whole pant slides down. Now I'm wondering if the pant is too big or the rise is too short. Either way, I have two of these and will put them in the Goodwill box. Don't know if the pics will show the difference between when first pulled up and after sag happens.

It has 4 pockets! Very versatile, thick, stretchy and comfy! I like these pants. They are a thick Lycra blend and stretchy, great for working out. I like to buy my pants a size larger so I can wear them as lounge wear around the house. These pants are XL and I’m 5’5” and 165. I like pants that don’t grab or grip making me look extra sausage-ey, so I bought this in XL even though a L would have been more comfortable for actually working out. I was surprised to find that these have 4 pockets, 1 on each side and 2 in the back. These make a great pair of stay home everyday pant, soft and comfy and very versatile.

Comfortable and flattering! I am in love with these pants! I originally bought black and navy but I love them so much I bought a few more colors! They are so comfortable and flattering and a nice upgrade from my old yoga pants. Great for lounging, running errands, or work from home. I’m 5’4 and about 150 lbs and the M fits perfectly.

Runs large Im 5'1" and 130 pounds and these can easily be rolled 4 inches. For the price, I'm having them tailored so I can wear them and have them look nice. The pic is with them rolled in. They run loose in the tummy and more fitted thru the legs but I'm thicker legged so maybe it is me. The material is good, the pants are flattering, and I would recommend for a tall curvy body.

Perfect Hiking Pant! First of all - I have a difficult time finding pants that truly fit me. I am 5'2", with a small waist and a bit full in the hips. Usually, I wear size 6, sometimes size 8. These come in XS - 2XL. I took my measurements and according to the size chart, I should fit into a size S. So, I took a chance and ordered them, and I'm glad I did - they fit me perfectly! I love these pants because: the price is right, the overall size is perfect, (even the length) they are well made with material that is water-repellent, is both lightweight and sturdy enough for hiking, the elastic waistband is thick but comfortable and easily adjustable with drawstrings, there are multiple pockets and the pants are convertible to shorts with good quality zippers, and the length of the short is at the perfect height above the knee. I ordered black because I can pair them with any color and I'm glad I ordered the brown because it is a beautiful, deep mocha brown - very pretty! I will be hiking in the Alps next July and I think 2 pair will be enough for 8 days, but I'm already looking for an excuse to buy a third pair! I highly recommend them!!

Plenty of pockets I'm a 5'1" curvy girl who doesn't like tight pants, therefore I'm very picky about hiking pants since most have no stretch and are way too long. I was pleasantly surprised that these pants are a relaxed fit that have room without being too large. They have a hint of stretch, but are not what I'd call actually stretchy. As expected, they have a zip off leg to turn them into shorts. The positive, it's far enough down the leg that you can comfortably hike or climb without worrying about modesty, but it's not so long as to be clear down at your knee. These pants aren't have nicely appointed pockets, with the side pockets being large enough for plus sized cell phones. All pockets have either a strong Velcro closure or a zip closure so items will stay secure. An added bonus for a short girl that I seldom find: a zipper and elasticized cuff at the ankle (yay me!) The zipper makes it easier to put a hiking boot on while the elastic keeps these from being so long that I'm walking on them.

Comfortable and Practical Hiking Pants I ordered these hiking pants, and they've quickly become my go-to for outdoor activities. The material is lightweight and quick-drying, which makes them perfect for camping, hiking, or even just casual wear. The convertible zip-off feature is a nice touch, allowing you to easily switch from pants to shorts when the weather changes. The pants are super comfortable, though I probably could have gone with a size medium instead of large since they’re a bit baggy in the butt area for my height and weight (I'm 5'2" and 148 lbs). Even with the slightly loose fit, they’re still great for moving around and staying active. Overall, these pants are a solid choice for anyone who needs versatile and comfortable gear for the outdoors.

Yes! Low rise sweats! So excited to find these. They’re a little pricey for sweats and the hem on the bottom is unfinished, but I love them. It’s about time we had low rise flare sweats come back! I love that the bottom can be altered to my 5’4 height if needed.

Great Work Pants! Im 5'2" 200 lbs. Most of my weight is on my belly. I got XLarge. These pants are GREAT! I'm a social worker so I'm running around all day. These were so comfortable. They fit right at the largest part of my waist and came right above my sneakers. Best part is 3 USABLE pockets, each can HOLD a large phone. If you're in retail and standing, walking, bending all day these are for you.

On the fence with this pair of pants I was looking for some inexpensive lightweight joggers to wear hiking or while cleaning the house or yard as I've always been afraid to wear my ridiculously expensive Athleta joggers doing either. I received this pair yesterday and here are my thoughts: Pro: 1. Color options are virtuously infinite and price point is excellent. I ordered the khaki, but was going to consider other colors if the fit was right. 2. Fabric is lightweight, but opaque, doesn't show panty lines, very stretchy, nice drape and soft to the touch, just like the Athleta joggers. 3. Lots of pockets: slant (not bulky) pockets in the front, one zippered pocket over the lumbar spine (for keys or a very tiny pet maybe? too shallow for a phone). 4. Length works for me. I am 5'2", the bottom sags some which is totally fine with me, even gives it a bit of a cool vibe. This pant should easily work for average height women, lengthwise. (Only one) Con: 1. The waist is a tad tight. I ordered a medium which is what Amazon recommended and, while the fabric is overall very stretchy, the very top of the pants where it falls on the waist has a double seam on the inside which gives that area much less give. Picture of waist unstretched is attached. I don't think I'd exchange it for a large, that would probably be too big. In all, I've decided to keep these and see if the waist thing becomes a non-issue later. For reference, my natural waist is 30 inches and I wear a size 6 in Athleta joggers.

Soft, but…….. I ordered two pairs of these buttery soft joggers. They are great because they come in different lengths. I opted for the petites which was perfect for my 5’3” body. However, the attention to detail just isn't there. Both pairs of pants are full of bunches of threads both inside and outside of the pants. The one pair I received had very clearly been opened and returned. They were wrinkled beyond words. If you don’t mind purchasing something and then trimming the mounds of threads left behind by the manufacturer, buy these pants otherwise, move on.

Fits perfectly, light weight These joggers are soft on your body and fit true to size. . Good sized pockets. They are a good spring or summer pant. You can wear them casually or dress them up. They are not see through even though they are lightweight. I wore them on a chilly day and discovered they are for warmer weather days. Great quality. Looks nice on. Length is good without being to long. I'm 5’5 and they fit nicely in length. I got the black color and it’s a really rich looking pant.

Fits perfectly. Nice material. Waist is 27" Hips 34.5" and i'm 5'4" tall and the X-Small fits me just right esp on the hips and crotch as I didn't want a it to be too fit or stretchy. Length is just right for my height. Nice material too though its not thin and can be a bit warm in the summer.

Fits perfectly. Nice material. Waist is 27" Hips 34.5" and i'm 5'4" tall and the X-Small fits me just right esp on the hips and crotch as I didn't want a it to be too fit or stretchy. Length is just right for my height. Nice material too though its not thin and can be a bit warm in the summer.

Fit perfectly, not to tight or to loose. Perfect for the gym. I bought these joggers for the gym. I am 5 3” and 200lbs and purchased an XL. The material was like tights, not like sweats and was perfect. I did leg day with them and loved them. The pocket for the phone fits my iPhone 12 Pro with a thin cover. Will not fit a bigger phone. I purchase 2 and glad that I did.

Better than Athleta?! I was so delightfully surprised after trying on the joggers! I normally wear $100+ Athleta joggers and these are pretty great for the price! I like that they are a little tighter at the tummy and have the pockets as well without making the pant look baggy. I’m 5’5’, so even small a are usually a little long on me, but these were the perfect crop ankle. The print is great and even the stitching is quality. I would definitely purchase another pair!

Great, buttery soft leggings! I am 5’5” and 112#, and ordered a small- they were too loose in the crotch and belly. I exchanged for an extra small, and they fit perfectly! A great cross between leggings and joggers! I love them!

Minus one star for the front wedgie and slight frumpiness due to the length and loose legs. I know I have an odd shape so I’m not too upset as this was the best I could hope for.

Best work pants! I’m 5’ 4” and 170 lbs with wider hips and the medium is perfect for me. I work in a hospital and split time between the kitchen and the units. These are the best work pants I’ve found. They’re stretchy & thin enough to be comfortable doing physical tasks, but not so stretchy or casual that they look inappropriate or unprofessional. They are also virtually impossible to get visibly dirty, even working around food all day. I even wore them hiking in a trip and they were great for that too! Moisture wicking, and the ankles are not skin tight, so it’s easier to stay cool. Plus front and back pockets.

Nice but pills The fit is perfect, I’m 5 ft 130 lbs and medium fits perfectly, but it’s pilling like crazy. Only on the first wash, there’s so much pilling and thread balls all over it! It’s very nice when you first receive it but when you wash it one time, it starts to break down. I wish the quality was better.

They're okay.... I'm 5'3, 150lbs and ordered a L. They are comfy and are fitted. If you'd like a baggier fit size up. The pants are soft and would be great for the winter time.I've washed them twice and the fabric has pills all over it so can't really wear them out.

No regrets! I love it! I loooove the fabric it's so soft and its comfort. I do recommend this it's great for raining days or just to chill at home. I've ordered size M, I'm 141 pounds and my high is 1.75 m I hope it helps!

These aren’t really anything like I expected. Theses aren’t really anything like I expected. I could never wear these in public. The color is lighter than the picture and way see-through. The fit is okay but usually mediums are loose on me, and these are pretty tight. (5’4”, 140)

Thought I'd give it a try.. I've been looking for joggers that are actually made out of sweatpants material. This one was but it was a little too tight (I wanted something more relaxed) and I've come to the realization that my body is just not made for joggers. I've got weird big thighs that do not allow some bottoms to sit properly in... that area. But if you don't have that issue then for the price it would be worth a try. Material is pretty thick and seems like it should hold up. I'm 5'4" about 200 lbs and I ordered XXL.

The BEST sweatpants! I live in New England and after a serious search for sweatpants that are open on the bottom... these the the bomb! They are thick enough to not let the breeze through. They are SUPER soft on the inside. The pockets are deep enough to hold an iPhone 13 Plus and it won't fall out. AND THEY DON'T SHRINK IF YOU FOLLOW THE WASHING INSTRUCTIONS! These are to be hung dry. They are not supposed to be put in the dryer. I let them hang dry for a few hours and the put them in the dryer for an air fluff and they came out perfect. No shrinking! (See photos) I also got an XL. I am 5'7" 170 lbs. I wanted them slightly bagging because its more cozy and I have enough leggings that are tight on me. Most of my weight is carried in my thighs, butt and a little bit of a mom pouch (two children later). I normally wear a large and have been losing weight so my tops are some mediums but pants are a bit tricky. I would definitely size up if your looking for a bit of a relaxed look as shown in the photos. But they are long enough, are SO COMFORTABLE, and very well made! I will be purchasing more when they are back in stock! Thank you Southpole! You are my savior!

Super-Soft Well-Designed Womens Leggings In my personal (and unsponsored) search for the perfect yoga legging life pant, I purchased both sized XS and SMALL Sp3lops women's legging/pants, in both the waist-pocket style and the side-leg pocket style in Black, Teal, Navy and Maroon.COLOR: First of all, the colors are attractive but off from names IMO. Maroon is a nice berry red, navy has strong purple undertones (see pic with navy top above the row of these pants to see color difference), teal is a lovely soft teal (more blue than green, although it looks gray in some of my pics, it's not...it's just not vibrant bold teal) and black is a more true dark black than the fleece-lined Dragon Fit brand leggings (which I also tested which are pictured on far left of first pic).STYLE: I really liked both styles, thought they were comfortable and will wear both. I am more likely to wear the side-pocket style at home for easy phone reach, but less attractive because of the phone in the pocket look, while I'm more likely to use the waist pocket style at the gym.SIZING: I'm about 5'4" and 130lbs, and both sizes were stretchy enough to fit, however the SMALL was more comfortable on me. The ankles are fitted and both were long enough for me, however the Small ended about two inches above my ankle, while the Small size was about an inch longer.Overall, these are what they claim to be, buttery-soft and very comfortable leggings. Love the pocket options and the gusseted stitching on the crotch. Fabric and make feel high quality and I love the high waist on these as it helped my shirts lay nicely. Overall, I think the size chart is good guidance, but colors need renaming.. I'd highly recommend to anyone looking for very comfortable and durable leggings.

Tight waisted comfortable sweatpants The fit in the hips and legs are great, and the elastic around the ankles is comfortably loose. However the waist band elastic is very tight. Not only does it create an indent in my hips, it feels very tight. For the fit of the pants, I feel it is too tight. The length of the pants is typical pant length. They are a bit too long for my 5’2” height, but not long enough they look strange. They could definitely fit someone a few inches taller than me. They are wearable and will get used, but they aren’t the favorite sweatpants in my drawers.

Great quality I was surprised to find these pants thicker than I expected. They're not heavy, but are definitely soft and warm. I prefer loose fitted sweats and the Large is perfect for that type of fit on me. I'm 5'1, 130 lbs for reference. I love that they're cuffed at the bottom and the sleek fabric on the outside gives a great look. Overall a great pair of sweatpants.

Between leggings and tights. This was basically meant to be a capri-like legging to go under longer shirts (like in the picture), but I wanted an athletic type quality (like a thicker spandex) that I thought Spalding would provide. I couldve just got regular capri leggings. These fit tight, I'm about 5'7 127lbs, ordered a medium, and once I got them up my thighs it felt like I was putting on tights. Suffice to say, once they are on, while they are thin, they are still somewhat comfortable, and you will probably want to cover your behind with a longer shirt.

True fit good quality! I am 5'7" 135lb and these fit perfect for me. The material is a tad bit thinner then my last pair of Spalding Capri pants. Overall good quality I have had for a few months now and wash a few times a week. I bought the grey pair too which I like more.

Pretty good! Overall they are pretty comfy. They are cotton leggings! I am 5'2 and 125 pounds and the medium fit great. The length is perfect! They are not squat proof, it is not awful but they are a tad see through. These leggings are pretty nice for the price! I do not know if I would buy again, but I will be wearing them!
So comfortable! These workout pants are so soft and comfortable. They are heavy material so they are not see through. Highly recommend. I ordered an XL. I am 5’7” and weigh 165. Perfect fit.

Great Fit Good quality I took a chance on these with so few reviews plus I never buy pants online because the never fit. These are perfect! Not too thin and not too thick. I would be comfortable wearing these in Florida where it’s always hot. I’m 5’9 and 177 lbs. and a large fits perfect. They are very well made. Too long usually. The fit is so perfect I’m going to buy another pair.

One of the best cotton knit yoga capris I've ever worn. These are a fantastic casual mostly cotton Capri. I am 5'7 tall and I have a long torso and short legs. Most of the time capris look more like ankle pants on me but these actually are somewhere in between which is nice. They come down to about mid calf. These are not thick sweatpants but a nice thicker cotton. I would still feel comfortable wearing these in spring summer and fall without feeling like I was going to sweat to death. They have a waistband with a drawstring to fit you comfortably. I weigh 189 lb and got the extra large and they fit perfectly. The pockets are nice and deep and there was a surprise pocket on my right hip on the inside. I'm not exactly sure what I would use this for but it's nice to know that there's one there. Maybe that's where I keep my quarter for the grocery cart at Aldi's? Did I mention how comfortable these things are? I would love another pair or two in other colors to toss into my everyday mommy wardrobe. They washed well with a little shrinkage but nothing extraordinary. I think these are a good value and will absolutely buy them again.

Soft, decent leg length for taller people The first time I tried these on (unwashed) they were super soft and very tight. No seams on the outside of legs which was nice. I went for a 2 1/2 mile run and the longer I wore them, the more comfortable they became, until they just felt like a second skin. Nice. However, I would not wear these to the gym on leg day because they felt like the rear would split open. The rear was not see-through but under garment is absolutely noticeable, leaving clear lines. I did not feel secure squatting down or doing leg curls but fine for upper body workouts and running. Took them off and washed them then hung to dry... not so soft any longer. Didn't want to put them in dryer out of fear they might shrink and now they feel kind of rough. Color is unique, price point is a bit high. I'm 5'9" and they reach my ankles. High waist is comfortable to wear for a decent length workout.

I did not feel secure squatting down or doing leg curls but fine for upper body workouts and running. Took them off and washed them then hung to dry... not so soft any longer. Didn't want to put them in dryer out of fear they might shrink and now they feel kind of rough. Color is unique, price point is a bit high. I'm 5'9" and they reach my ankles. High waist is comfortable to wear for a decent length workout.

😮 THESE ARE AMAZING! When these arrived, I didn’t think there was any way possible I’d get them on my body. They looked SO TINY! SO PERFECT!! I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Its regular high waist not too high and the crossover makes them show more stomach than regular straight waist pants which is so flattering. The waist isn’t too tight. I probably could’ve fit the small. But I’m happy with the XS and will definitely order more in a different color. I’ll probably gift these for my sister around the holidays as well. I have an event coming up that I needed these for and ordered them later than I should’ve however they came in a day earlier than expected which was awesome. There are no holes, weird spots or crooked stitching. I could definitely wear these to bed they’re so comfortable. I really am so so happy with them and can’t wait to wear them. I already know I will get so many wears out of these. I would say they fit true to size and are nice and stretchy even though they fit snug. If your questioning weather to get them or not I would say go for it! They are PERFECT!

Solid leggings, but unflattering on me My measurements: waist 24", hips 38". I'm 5'8" and have a long torso (my rise is long but I don't have much space under my chest, so I need a high rise but it can easily overwhelm me). I've got hip dips and not a lot of upper glutes. I was searching for a pair of flare leggings that had no front seam, flattering glute seams, a long inseam, and that aren't $70; these are the ONLY ones I've found so far. I ordered the XS 34" inseam. The good things: I like how high rise they are! Not in love with the cross waist, but it's not bad. The bottom of the V comes down to just above my belly button. They changed the gusset design so now it's a small triangle. They're a great length. The fabric is a performance material that's cool to the touch, super stretchy, smooth and kind of soft. No loose seams. It's got some weight to it, so I would size down if you're in between sizes just to avoid any sliding around. No show underwear would be best with it but the fabric isn't super thin, so you could get away with regular underwear depending on the thickness. I'm not wearing no-show underwear in the pics. Now onto the bad... I really don't think these are very flattering on me. The design of the crotch gusset means that my butt just eats up the lower part of the back seam, in a way that doesn't look intentional, and I don't have enough upper glutes to do the same to the top part so it ends up looking weird and kinda making my butt look smaller?? Now I like me some scrunch leggings, I even like regular leggings to fit my glutes, but for some reason these just look a little off. I'm returning this color. I'm debating whether I'll keep the black pair and just wear oversized stuff over it. If I could rate this 3.5 stars I would, I don't want to give it 3 stars but I don't like it enough for 4 stars personally. If I keep the black and end up changing my mind I'll come back and update this review.Overall good leggings, everything except the back is fine, just not for me.

SO PERFECT!! I usually don’t write reviews just ratings. This is actually the first one I’ve written. I had to write this because I was skeptical of these pants and they ended up being so perfect. I’m 5’8.5 and 120 lbs. I ordered XS & 34”. It’s usually hard for me to find pants that fit well. As a taller girly it’s not easy to find pants small enough that are also long enough. I read so many reviews on these pants. Mostly good but some weird and questionable. First of all the crotch is perfectly fine. No camel toe and no large or messed up stitching. I went back and fourth between S & XS but I knew the pants were supposed to be stretchy so I went with the XS. I’m glad I did because they fit perfectly. I will wear them with my chunky sneakers. When I have my shoes on they cover the tops of the sneakers which I love. When I have no shoes on they sit on the ground which I also love. The length is PERFECT. The flare is bigger than I thought it would be but it’s very flattering. When I initially took the pants out of the bag I was worried the flare would be too much but it’s not at all. It’s very nice. No sheer or see through but I also have the color black so it might not be like that for lighter colors. The texture is nice and soft. It’s thin but not as thin as I thought it would be. Just your basic work out material really. I won’t wear these to work out in but they would definitely be fine for that. They’re breathable, comfortable and flexible. If you’re looking for something for cold weather these aren’t it but I’m not. The crossover waist is SOOO CUTE. My waist is snatched in and it doesn’t roll over or anything. Its regular high waist not too high and the crossover makes them show more stomach than regular straight waist pants which is so flattering. The waist isn’t too tight. I probably could’ve fit the small. But I’m happy with the XS and will definitely order more in a different color. I’ll probably gift these for my sister around the holidays as well. I have an event coming up that I needed these for and ordered them later than I should’ve however they came in a day earlier than expected which was awesome. There are no holes, weird spots or crooked stitching. I could definitely wear these to bed they’re so comfortable. I really am so so happy with them and can’t wait to wear them. I already know I will get so many wears out of these. I would say they fit true to size and are nice and stretchy even though they fit snug. If your questioning weather to get them or not I would say go for it! They are PERFECT!

Solid leggings, but unflattering on me My measurements: waist 24", hips 38". I'm 5'8" and have a long torso (my rise is long but I don't have much space under my chest, so I need a high rise but it can easily overwhelm me). I've got hip dips and not a lot of upper glutes. I was searching for a pair of flare leggings that had no front seam, flattering glute seams, a long inseam, and that aren't $70; these are the ONLY ones I've found so far. I ordered the XS 34" inseam. The good things: I like how high rise they are! Not in love with the cross waist, but it's not bad. The bottom of the V comes down to just above my belly button. They changed the gusset design so now it's a small triangle. They're a great length. The fabric is a performance material that's cool to the touch, super stretchy, smooth and kind of soft. No loose seams. It's got some weight to it, so I would size down if you're in between sizes just to avoid any sliding around. No show underwear would be best with it but the fabric isn't super thin, so you could get away with regular underwear depending on the thickness. I'm not wearing no-show underwear in the pics. Now onto the bad... I really don't think these are very flattering on me. The design of the crotch gusset means that my butt just eats up the lower part of the back seam, in a way that doesn't look intentional, and I don't have enough upper glutes to do the same to the top part so it ends up looking weird and kinda making my butt look smaller?? Now I like me some scrunch leggings, I even like regular leggings to fit my glutes, but for some reason these just look a little off. I'm returning this color. I'm debating whether I'll keep the black pair and just wear oversized stuff over it. If I could rate this 3.5 stars I would, I don't want to give it 3 stars but I don't like it enough for 4 stars personally. If I keep the black and end up changing my mind I'll come back and update this review.Overall good leggings, everything except the back is fine, just not for me.

The good, the bad and the rest Actually these are a 4.5 star. For reference I'm over 50 years old. Height 5'6", weight 170lbs measurements Bust: 43 Waist: 34 Hips: 43. I went for the XLarge with 30" inseam. I really like these leggings. They are very smooth and soft, fitting nicely. These have been through the washer and dryer a couple times with no issues. The seams all seem well sewn, but I'm not a fan of the shiny thread that goes down the outer leg. It kinda feels like it cheapens them and keeps me from being able to wear in a say a work environment or an evening out with the hubs. Otherwise, they are very comfortable on. And I really liked that they offer different inseam options! Why do more womens pants not do this??? For that alone it bumped the star back up. The flare at the ankle is a bit much, but I can easily cut it down to my tastes. And that's all that is, my tastes, not a complaint on the pants. But just wanting to state that the fabric and cut otherwise is worth altering. The waist band has no extra elastic or pull cord in it, just relying on the compression on the fabric to hold them up. So far it hasn't been and issue as the elasticity of the fabric is good. The pockets are great and can easily hold my camera. Overall the only detriment is the shiny thread, but if you only want to work out in them they would be fine. I can comfortably recommend. Peace and soft pants

These flare leggings have a clean design. The material is soft and smooth, lightweight and have a slinky feel. The leggings are made of 80% nylon and 20% spandex with some stretch. They are medium in thickness and feel sturdy. I like that it is not see-through. The quality is very good, and did not change after washing. I do like the v-shaped waist and it is double layered for extra support. It provides a flattering look. The two pockets are a nice addition and are pretty deep. However, you can't really put anything bulky in them as the items will bulge out due to how tight fitting the leggings are. I am 5’1” and on the petite slender side and I ordered the Small size. I typically wear X-Small, but I've ordered from Sunzel before and their sizing is made smaller and tighter, so Small was a slightly better fit for me though still a tad tight. I recommend going a size or two up from your normal size. The length on these is a bit long on me even with shoes. The bottom of the pants is more bell shaped and pretty wide rather than bootcut flare. Overall, these are nice leggings with a clean design but do have a tight fit. If this is your style then these are worth consideration but I recommend going up a size or two. At the time of review, this is $35.99.

Best stretchy pants I've tried in a while Black XL - 32" inseam. These pants come in a nice medium to thick weight fabric that's very soft to the touch. The stretch is amazing, and the high waist ensures that you won't be uncomfortable with binding at the hips or waist. The pant legs are in a single layer of fabric, while the waistband is folded into a double layer. The seams are strong with multi-needle stitching. I love the feature of an elongated gusset insert running at the inseam and crotch - I think it looks sporty and probably adds to the comfortable fit and flexibility of the garment. The flare at the hemline looks cute, and makes a nice silouette. I was so glad to see that they offered different pant lengths. The 32" inseam was perfect for me at 5ft - 7 1/2in, as you can see the length just hits the floor with flat sandals. If you want to wear these with platforms or thick sneakers, think about getting a longer inseam. I was able to pull the pants up higher and lower to adjust the length though. I really appreciate a brand that caters to their customers by offering options to get a perfect fit. I would defintiely recommend these. Love them!

Got these leggings to wear casually, and they are soooo comfortable. I already own a pair of the Aerie crossover flared leggings and the material on these pants feel notably higher quality. The waist on these leggings is seamless which is a must for for me. The fabric is very smooth and soft. I see some complaints about the thinness of the fabric but personally it's not a detractor for me; these leggings aren't really meant to be heavy compression, they're more like the Lululemon Nulu fabric used in the Align leggings. In fact, I would still consider them to be thicker than Align leggings and the amount of hold feels just right. They're also totally squat proof. I’m generally size 6/5ft5, I got the Medium 30” inseam as I have relatively short legs for my height. The fit is great! Though if you have slightly longer legs I think you would need a longer inseam. These are super flattering and the quality is amazing, definitely recommend!

The leggings came very quickly (2 days). The size was spot on. They do feel like quality leggings. Squat proof 100% and the stay snug to your waist. Will be buying more colours once they are available in 28” inseam

I like how the fabric feels a little more substantial in the Sunzel Mini Flare Leggings than other more opaque nulu fabric leggings. They are still lightweight and comfortable for both the gym or running errands as well as walking my dog. I appreciate the gentle flare that looks stylish without being too wide, so no hindrance when using elliptical or bike. I found the fit to be true to size and the waistband stretchy and smooth hitting high on my waist without rolling or sliding down when doing exercises or yoga poses. I am 5’4″ and typically wear a 30″ inseam and these fit great with my sneakers. The cost is very reasonable for the quality and durability of these leggings which wash and wear very well. I found they don’t pill, shrink or stretch too much after wearing. The fabric feels buttery soft and fits nicely.

These are really very lightweight leggings. They stretch and feel very comfy. I am 5’8 and these are the 32 inch inseam and they are a perfect length for me. This is a size XL . These are well made and I will get a lot of wear out of these.

I loved these leggings! Honestly I buy Sunzel flared leggings, biker shorts, I buy it all! Such great quality, price, true to size, and non see through! Definitely recommend. I am 5’1 & these were a large.

Good quality Compression is definitely there but not uncomfortably, these are not see through so I guess they’re squat proof, they’re not obnoxious in the scrunch butt area like they are more modest then a lot of the scrunch bottoms out there. I like these so much that I’ll try them out in another color and see what happens. Army black and grey gets my vote. Also I’m 155 lbs, athletic build, I ordered a medium and they fit perfect.

Quality and stretch are great! This is my third time buying Sunzel branded leggings and I love the feel and stretch that the pants give. I ordered a Large and I am 5'2", 150lbs. I could have gotten away with a medium but because im shorter and have wider hips/ thicker thighs I like that the large has enough tummy control and it doesn't fold at the top like a lot of brands do. Its a comfortable fit and I don't feel the need to constantly adjust them. My only qualm about it is that the crotch area looks a bit awkward but I think that's probably the case with most leggings. I would recommend and would buy again!

Good material, fit was a bit large, dye marks on front & back My measurements: waist 24", hips 38". I'm 5'8", have a very high rise, and usually need longer inseams for leggings. I ordered an XS. The material is smooth, very stretchy, and not irritating, but they were looser than I would expect from an XS (I do not like any looseness in the waist). I would size down if you're in between or on the lower end of your size range. No loose seams. There were large dye lines on both the front and back of the leggings, not creases from being folded and packaged, actual dark dye marks. You can see them in the picture, but they actually looked worse in real life. There was a big line of dye from the top of the waistband to the crotch on the front as well. I'd order like 20 pairs if they fixed the slightly loose fit and the quality control, but I'm returning these.

Do yourself a favor and buy like 10 of these The pictures don't do these leggings justice. These leggings were the first time I finally felt confident again after 2-3 years of feeling disgusted by my body. When I first took them out of the package, I thought they looked like a child's size, that there was no way they were going to fit. I maybe could've gone up one size, but honestly they fit great. I risked a size large when I'm usually at least an xl-5xl (depending on the brand's sizing), and I'm glad I did. They're so stretchy, yet so solid. They're holding & shaping in all the right places, and super comfortable as far as tight pants go. Great on men / masc-presenting folx too, bc I was at Costco the other day, and some random lady complimented my legs / butt while I was wearing these lol. I've also noticed that these hardly hold any dog fur hostage, unlike some of my other leggings, which are constantly covered in dog fur. 11/10 would recommend. Definitely buying more of these. Hip measurement: ~45" Waist measurement: ~35"

Super comfortable I already owned the regular flare cross waist leggings and the mini flared leggings. So when I saw this brand posting a giveaway on IG for the mini flare WITH POCKETS I jumped all over that. In the past I’ve sized down in Sunzel products because they’re so stretchy and I love compression. However I feel they are TTS. Sunzel is my go to Amazon legging! I ordered my normal size in these but was nervous because one time I ordered pocket leggings in my normal size and they were too small. But I feel they have made changes and they’re TTS now. I love how deep the pockets are and can fit my iPhone 13 Pro Max. I love that the seams are flat locked. I have never had an issue with them not being squat proof either. My only issue is my fault. I ordered them in a 30” inseam and I needed them a little longer. It’s not THAT noticeable imo. Especially when standing still. But I noticed when I walked, and my thighs rubbed together, they did come up higher on the ankle. But it does have good vertical stretch, so I was able to make them work. If they come out with a longer inseam I will 100% be ordering them. Don’t get me wrong, I’m still going to wear these. But just be aware they’re a 30” inseam.

LOUNGEWEAR - Not lulu but great quality for the price I’ve purchased these in black and wine colors last year for $30, and saw the hot pink ($14) and forest green ($19) go on sale so I tried them and I’m keeping them. The forest green is nothing like the pictures as other reviews said. More of a teal/green (see pics), and significantly lighter of a shade than the photo. Pink is spot on neon pink you’d expect. For the price, they’re both perfect to lounge around the house in. At the end of the day lulu aligns are $98 and for loungewear the no one will see, these are a great alternative. Comparing these to lulu aligns (used sell their clothes so very familiar with the brand) - similar buttery soft feeling, they equally pick up just as much lint and dog hair, and they both pill in the inner thighs (maybe if you have a thigh gap that won’t happen in either brand) but lulu aligns long term also just pill all over from washing (to be fair, some pairs I’ve had 10+ years and you can trade them in to the store for credit towards a new pairs). I haven’t owned Sunzel long enough to say the same, but I imagine they will too based on the fabric. Lulu leggings that are NOT aligns don’t pill like this - for anyone interested in taking the leap. They are different in the crotch and waistband though - comparing a M Sunzel to a 6 lulu (5’10” 145~150 lbs for reference) - the Sunzel has noticeably more material in the crotch. It doesn’t stand out as much in the black, but in the colored leggings, especially when you sit, you’ll see extra fabric bunched up down there vs. lulu I’ve never noticed significant “bunching” in any color. And while there’s extra crotch material, the waist band is tighter in Sunzel. Positives are you could probably do a high intensity work out in these (lulu aligns are aimed for lounging and yoga, not running or high impact) but these stay up better. The negative being you might want to size up if you’re using these for lounging. I’m between a 4/6 in jeans and typically buy a S/M in non-numbered clothing. I carry more weight in my legs than stomach so for me they’re comfy. If you’re more of a 6/8 or are slightly more top heavy with thin legs or evenly spread, I’d honestly recommend trying the large just to have that comfort in the waist band for lounging. If you use these for working out, but them in your normal size. I’d definitely need a small if I wore these to the gym or for a run.

No issues rolling down. Stayed in place. I knocked off one star because it does catch lint very easily, so I air-dry them only and wash them inside out. I had to buy a second set, I really like them. UPDATE- I loved these so much previously, so I decided to buy some more. They CHANGED! This is NOT the same material or design. The XL now feels like a 2X, and they are so thin they feel like pantyhose or leggings that go under a dress. I no longer recommend and I’m SO disappointed. I now have to find my gym pants elsewhere. Ugh.

Good leggings I looked through several reviews before I bought these...so I wanted to make sure that I wrote an honest and accurate review to help any other looking to buy. I am 5'7 and roughly 160lbs after having 4 children...I bought a size large and am pleased with the fit. These are not miracle working pants, you won't look like the woman in the pictures unless you have the same body as her lol...with that being said, they are more flattering than your regular every day leggings. They give your booty a nice curve and the angled waistline helps give you the appearance of a slightly slimmer waist. I have thick thighs and child bearing hips and definitely could not fit into a medium. The large fits well with minimum belly bulge, I might be inclined to try an Xtra large if I order again. The material is thick enough to not be see-through and stretchy enough to be comfortable. Overall, a good purchase.

LONG SWEATPANTS These are super comfy sweatpants and I am surprised how long they are! I’m 6’ tall and these come to the floor! They are plenty roomy, pretty wide legs actually. But they’re a great pair of sweatpants and plenty warm, I might get some more! They fit true to size.

XXL not very long, or wide, but SOFT I REALLY wanted to love these, as they are pretty thick, super soft, and Borahae purple. But they are getting returned for a variety of reasons. I'm 5'8" and 195 lbs. The pants weren't tight in the butt (and mine is big), but they weren't loose enough either. Most importantly, they are made lowrise ONLY IN THE BACK, so if you have any butt at all, it IMMEDIATELY starts to slide down and give you peek-a-crack. My backside photo was taken less than one minute after I pulled the waistline up as high as I can without hurting my lady parts. The front of the pants folds under my belly instantly, but I'll take the blame for that one. The other part that was a no for me is, they are BARELY long enough for me, and I'm not particularly tall. If I were 5'10" or taller, they'd be highwaters on me. The lack of length is already unflattering, and I'm only 5'8". For XXL, I would expect longer in the legs (and butt) unless it's labeled as XXL Petite. Notes that didn't impact me returning these: The only label on or in them is on the inside side seam near the pocket. I posted a photo. And the waistband seam is only ok, folded over, but not double folded. So you can see the fabric cut, and I fear the seam could come loose after a few washes. The band is soft and bunchy, though, and the drawstrings seem quality. Cheers and good luck in looking for pants, especially if you need purple ones!

Def wouldn’t call these baggy, but they’re nice I’ve been wearing these sweats in my rotation at home since August, and they’re not my top pick, but they are pretty nice. To be fair, I like a sweatpant with a really loose fit, especially in the waist. I’m pretty average sized (5’4”, 150lbs, athletic, longer torso and shorter legs), and I would not call these baggy by any means. The waist is definitely tight enough to where you can feel that it’s there; these are more the type of sweatpants that I wouldn’t feel at all uncomfortable wearing out of the house. They’ve got a cinched ankle, a cute tie in the waistband, and good pockets. If you prefer some sweats that hug your bum and won’t slide down at all, or if you are a waif queen, these will definitely be a nice option for you! They are quite soft and comfy in terms of material. As a side note, I know the selling page says to hand wash, but I’ve been tossing these in the laundry with everything else and haven’t had a problem. Like with all my drawstring pants, I tie a little bunny knot with the strings so they don’t get lost in the waistband. The price is right, and for some, these might be the perfect sweaties!

They are decent These are workable sweats. For a teen, they are exactly the style everyone is wearing. For an adult (like me, wanting cozy), I wouldn't probably get these again. The inside is fairly comfortable but the outside is rougher than expected. The drawstring doesn't really do anything so size yourself appropriately because there's no cinching. I got a medium, and wear a 8/10 typically and these fit perfectly, a bit baggy where they need to be and waistband is just right. They come to my ankles, I'm 5'8". My biggest gripe apart from fabric is the ankles are tightttt. Like on my mind constantly because it's annoying. Leaves dents in my skin. This might be the great divide between teens and adults, if ankles are somehow smaller on younger kids wearing the same waist size as me. Otherwise stitches etc are good, but the overall product is noticeably not on the same level as sporty name brands.

Some fit better than others. I purchased medium pairs of the long rainbow striped pants and the short double white striped pants. I love how the rainbow pair fits while the other pair fits more like leggings. I am 5'6 and 135-140 LBS, I have wide hips and a small waist and these sweats are very flattering to a more hour glass figure. However, if my waist was just an inch wider the waistband would be too tight (which could be problematic for square or apple shaped figures). In general, I suggest sizing up, especially if you want a baggy fit or if you carry more weight around your tummy rather than your butt.

Take me back to Y2K!! I purchased these to wear to a concert: Tori Kelly's "Purple Skies" tour. The album screamed Y2K vibes and... hello, PURPLE!! The fit: The pants fit a little bit snug around my waist. I ordered a Medium, as I'm typically a medium in leggings and sweatpants. Even though they fit snug, I don't know that I would have sized up because everywhere else, the fit was just right.. any baggier I wouldn't have loved the look as much. They come just above my bellybutton, so they'd look nice with a cropped top as well. I'm 5'7" and they hit right above my ankle; however, depending on the shoe you wear, it won't look like you're about to wade through a flash flood. You can also loosen or tighten the draw string at the ankles. The color: These pants are VERY purple. Less of a royal purple color, more like a deep lilac; which I quite like (see the second pic for the true color). So, keep that in mind :) The quality: Without a doubt, these are some pretty well made pants for an amazon find. It's always hit or miss for me when shopping for clothing.. sometimes things are so thin and very see through; but, the fabric used for these pants are on par with any decently priced cargo's from a traditional retailer. I've washed and dried them with no shrinkage or color bleeding and very minimal wrinkles. All of the pockets are functional!

Disappointed - Not worth the price I'm 5'5", 140lbs, little thicker in the belly/hip area and these pants accentuated all the wrong parts!! I got a Medium. There is a little stretch in the waist which is nice, but the waist does run pretty tight compared to the legs. Not a 'skinny leg' fit like the picture. Plus, the material is pretty thin and cheap looking. I paid about $35 for these pants and I would expect this kind of quality from pants half the price or less. I don't return items from Amazon often but I will be returning these. For the price I paid, I could get similar pants with a much better quality and fit. If you're on the chubbier side and looking for high waisted jegging-style pants for comfort and lower belly concealment, these are NOT for you!!

Thick thighs size up These pants are super cute & comfortable, but if you're an hourglass or pear shape, you'll likely experience some back gap. I'm 5'6" 165lbs and am normally a size 10-12 pant. Amazing quality — lovely soft and thick fabric. Perfect fit! These are such lovely trousers! I ordered a size small and they fit like they were tailored for me. The fabric is so nice and thick and soft. The quality is excellent and the detail around the pocket is a really attractive touch. I’m 5’11, 115-120lbs and the small is perfect! I usually wear a size 26 in jeans and these are ideal. The rise is nice and high but not too high —almost exactly at my belly button, maybe a half centimeter below. The length is just as I expected—definitely crop length. I’ve never had anything made of this artificial wool before but I really like it. I live in Southern California but I would definitely recommend these to women living in colder climates. I would have killed for these living in Minneapolis! Perfect length to show off a skinny bootie or shoe. Brown color is gorgeous in person. All around probably favorite purchase to date on Amazon. Need the other color now! Arrived in 3 days and in perfect condition.

Good but I don't wear heels so they are a lil longer leg length wise Was concerned about buying because of the lack of reviews n photos and the not so ones but the Lord told me to so I did. Which kinda made me sad I have keys I have to carry around and a cell phone. So I need somewhere to put it and putting it in the breast pocket of my long sleeve work shirt makes it sag cuz the weight lol. Anyway very good very comfy and they are stretchy material I'd say about inch maybe inch and a half of an extra stretch for me for the waist band.. They are baggy as you seen in my photos.. I havent tried it with my work shirt but this blue one is tucked in and its still baggy. Which would be okay but it looks kinda weird so I think I might have to take it in. I mean I have a tummy but not that drastic and not in the crotch lmfao. However, the baggy part helps when you sit down so they aren't all going up your lady area uncomfortably especially for hours on end.. It may look weird standing but makes wonders for when you sit. They are kinda long but that is for heels. As I tried to show in the photo. My heel was showing like hers was. Bout maybe half an inch give or take. The heels I used are 3" to be exact. I do however don't wear heels for work. I work as an assistant manager at a movie theatre so there is no need for them considering I'd be running up and down stairs for the projectors and cleaning theatres😅 Also the height chart is correct I am 5ft exactly and they fit accordingly. Hope this helped... for reference I am 5 ft, 160 to 170, 23 years old, bust is 46", waist is 36", hip is 42", and body shape is inverted triangle. Stay blessed and beautiful you!! 🥰😋

Comfortable Fashionable Palazzo Pants Great choice for a pair of palazzo pants. I'm usually a size small but my 5'9" frame requires longer pant legs and this medium pair worked fine. Love at 1st try, but snagged and started pilling day 1 When I 1st tried these on, I was had over heels in love with them. They are extremely soft and super stretchy, (but not in the tight yoga plant type easy). I love that they have a drawstring waist and pockets. They feel absolutely amazing. I was about to order several more pairs, but decided to wait a couple days. Within just a couple hours, they had at least 2 snags on them. By day 2 of wearing them, they felt bumpy from pilling. I still love them , but can only imagine how bumpy rough they will be after I've worn them more than 2 times. If there was a way to prevent them from pilling or snagging, these pants would be perfect.(I'm 5'11, around 200lbs pear shape with a large bootie and thighs and the x- large fit me perfectly. With how stretchy these are and good elastic in the waist, I wouldn't be surprised if the large would work as well)

Pretty Color; Not Amazing I had higher hopes for this one. I’m 192 pounds and 5’6. I got a size large. What I like about it: the color is beautiful. The fabric is almost silky and super soft. It has pockets. What I don’t love about it: the fit was a bit weird. Because of the waist band, I expected it to hold in your tummy a bit but it doesn’t. The pants are not see through but the fabric was thin enough that if I pulled up the waist band to be flattering on my waist, you could see my cellulite on my thighs. If you’re smaller and don’t have cellulite, these would probably work great for you! The problem is if I would have sized down one, the waist band probably would have been more flattering but then the pants would have been too tight for my liking on my thighs. All together: for me personally: they’re okay, but not super flattering. I probably won’t be wearing them out of the house, but they’re super comfortable for at home, they’re loose on the legs and it’s a silkyish stretchy material.

Two sizes tried, like them both I’ve chosen boyfriends with less effort and time then it’s taken to make a decision on these pants… I’ve gone back and forth for three days trying these on to see which size I like best. This is my fourth revision of my review. And, honestly, the jury is still out, because both sizes I tried fit great, albeit different. My stats: age 45, 5’5”, 170lbs, 43-36-43, 36F/38E, flat butt with chub on the flanks (back area of hips just below the waist), normal size bought on amazon L/XL. What I ordered: Small (in white) and medium (in black). The deets: Fabric is a great weight; feels thick but not heavy; says 70% rayon/30% linen on tag. White is on the sheer side, but no more so than other white linen pants would be. The medium fit like a true baggy harem pant. Lots of material around the hip/legs/butt area. The waist was a little loose before tying the drawstring. The abundance of fabric did add some bulk or perceived girth to my hips, but I found the same bagginess to be flattering to the hip/butt area since it wasn’t hugging the spots I am self conscious about. I do find that I am constantly adjusting the tie depending on whether I am sitting or standing. The small fit was perfect at the waist; the elastic still had plenty of stretch and fit just right at rest. (At rest, the waist was about two inches smaller than the medium.) There was significantly less fabric around the hip and butt area. This lended itself to a more tailored, less frumpy look. However, I noticed that because the pant has less of a dropped crotch and less fabric in the booty, I felt like my cheeks were eating the material; I noticed the square shape of my backside more, particularly the chunk at the back of hip. Length is a little shorter on white. Conclusion: I think these are a great FP-inspired pant. The closest of all the brands on here to the original inspiration. The problem for me is that I still can’t decide which size I like more. The difference in amount of material from one size to another really does change the whole look of the pant, and not necessarily in a bad way. I think the looser fitting medium is great for lounging or running errands, paired with a crop tank or even oversized top; much like the look of the FP version. The more tailored through the hip/tummy/butt look of the small makes the style a little more work-appropriate, and still comfy enough for lounging, etc. It really DOES depend on what look you're going for. Would I be crazy to keep both sizes?!

Perfect summer pants I love these pants! They are drawstring and elastic wait. The fabric is a linen blend- super light and flowy. They are perfect beach pant for a bathing suit cover up or just to wear around when it’s hot- they feel like wearing air! They feel like a great dupe for a popular boho brand which costs 3x more. They have washed well. I ordered several different versions and colors of this pant from different makers, and for me these were the best! I love the river blue color- so summery. They have a nice pocket and pleated pocket detail on the leg. They do have a dropped crotch kind of harem style, which I love, and is probably what makes them so comfortable. I’m usually between a medium and a large, and I’m wearing the medium. The large was huge, and I maybe could have taken a small. I am also tall, 5’10’ and they are still not super cropped on me. So depending on what look you want, you may want to size down. I also tried the army green color but it was a little bit too light for me- I loved this blue. If you are looking for super relaxed, flow, beach vibe- give them a go!

Good set for lounging at home I am an apple shaped, mid-sized curvy girl, 5'5" with waist 38, Hips 46, band 35, bust 42, weight about 195lbs, Bra size 38DDD. They are pretty long on me, so I think they could work even for some of the taller girls. Overall I think this is a good set for the price.

Don't love this version due to the scrunch, but great customer service. I bought these after seeing lots of girls in our Fit Finds FB group gush about them. I was never going to spend the money on the WS x GS collection (if I could even snag a pair before they sold out) but loved the look of them, especially the scrunch and laser detail on the back, so I was pretty excited to see this dupe show up and get great reviews. To be transparent, I absolutely adore leggings and have tons of pairs from Amazon, AliExpress, and many other more expensive brands. I wear them every chance I get because I think they are really flattering and comfortable as hell. Aligns or the Amazon Hawthorne/HeyNutz ones that are very similar are my daily go-tos, and I like Wunder Unders or similar slick things for the gym, so that will give you an idea of what I prefer. I'm pretty open to any brand or price, as long as they're great for the gym and/or comfortable and flattering, or just a good lounging pair. I'm normally a black leggings kind of girl but decided to branch out and try this gorgeous GREEN, and then later DANDELION BROWN, and both are super cute. I'd say the colors are true to the picture. They aren't the most cellulite friendly, but I expect that with pretty much any color that isn't black or patterned. I got a MEDIUM in the GREEN pair and a LARGE in the DANDELION BROWN pair. I have pretty consistently been a size medium in leggings for years, but I've recently gained some more weight, so I'm more on the true large side now. I'm 5'3" and I think my measurements are about 43-33-43 (bust-waist-hip) right now, unfortunately, and I'm a 10 or 12 in AE jeans, depending on the pair. That being said, both the medium and large fit me great and about the same, so take that into consideration when trying to determine your size. They are super stretchy. While I think these have potential, these two versions (aka the original with laser detail and newer without it) are just not my thing. Both pairs fit me great, but the scrunch completely stretched out and made me look like I had a slightly wrinkly butt-crack, which is not really the look I was going for. It's hard to tell in my pictures, but I promise, that's what it looked like. I think the scrunch is just too weak and a big butt stretches it out. I've been told that Tom Tiger plans on fixing it, so I have high hopes for future versions! They do help give a little glute separation, so some may love that it's way more subtle than something like the Alphalete Amplifies with their very intense scrunch. I guess I just wanted something a little more in-between. Also, for those of you that hate WAISTBAND SEAMS in leggings, like me, you should note that both versions do have a seam there, but NO ELASTIC BAND! It's just a thin sewn seam around the top of the waistband. I would definitely prefer they find a way to just make it a folded material waistband and do away with the seam, but it doesn't bother me too much, just cuts into my fat a little bit. Due to my short torso, I also have issues with leggings rolling a lot on my waist, and these definitely rolled a bit. I did enjoy the cute laser cutout on the back and the general shapes of the seams, but I found the lack of a front seam, and the higher seams of the front waistband, made my lower tummy area look pooched out. I unfortunately hold some weight there right now so just didn't feel confident in them. If you are lean in your tummy area, that wouldn't be an issue for you, obviously. I wish that waist seam sat a bit lower to give me some compression, but I don't know how that would look. The other thing I didn't love about these is the material. I'm not great at describing this sort of thing, but these are like a super thin but slick material, and they honestly feel a bit cheap. They aren't rough, just not my favorite. I also had some of those line dye marks on the butt and a few on the front and legs of both pairs, as you may be able to spy in the photo of the Green pair. I come across this issue with random leggings on occasion. I assume it has something to do with how they are stored, maybe the dye process, and/or them being folded for extended periods of time in different temperatures so the dye settles in those folds. We'll see if they go away with a wash. Despite the things I didn't love, I do still recommend everyone try these for themselves, and like I said, I have high hopes for future versions. I really like the company, as they are very interested in customer feedback and actually listen to critique. I highly recommend anyone that loves leggings add Tom Tiger (and their sister company, Aurolas) on Instagram and Facebook so you can interact with them. They are very responsive and open to suggestions. TLDR: I got a Medium in Green, Large in Dandelion Brown; both fit about the same. These have a sewn seam without an elastic band around the top of the waistband. The butt-scrunch doesn't really do much but help with some glute separation. The material is thin and kind of slick, feels a bit cheap; not my favorite. They are not cellulite friendly in these two colors, but who cares. While not my fav, I still recommend people try them to see how they feel! We all like different things. I also really like the company.

These have so much potential Edit: After a few washes the lines came out and they still haven’t ripped so I’ve added a star. My measurements are 25” waist and 36” hips and I bought a size small. These leggings could be so amazing but I was disappointed. They arrived covered in lines, which kind of look like the dye had run where the leggings creased. I exchanged them and the new pair have the same lines but not as bad so I kept them. They’re really thin, which personally I prefer, but I’m scared they’re going to rip every time I put them on. They’re soft and very comfortable to wear. The butt scrunch is invisible when you’re wearing them which is great! Not 100% sweat proof but held up pretty well. They’re already piling from hip thrusts so I don’t know how long they’ll last. These might not be suitable for any workout that includes lower body contact. Like I said, these have great potential but just didn’t pull through for me. Regardless, they’d make perfect lounge leggings.

Versatile.. wear to Gym or to Dinner These pants remind me of some athletic pants I had years ago and I really loved them. But no, I didn’t order these for yoga or pilates, I’ll simply be wearing these in place of other slacks for fairly casual activities. I selected the speckled “charcoal” color which has a tweedy look. I can wear these with any number of sweaters and shirts, and will even be wearing them to dinners.The fabric is stretch and slick, and are quite fitted. These flare out to a bootcut style and the fabric flows with movement and is very soft. This is made with a wide panel at the waist which can be drawn up into puckers by a fabric cord on the left side, or can be left smooth. I’m not crazy about having a bow tied at the side with this long cord, so probably will either tuck the excess cord under the waist panel or into the waistband.I followed the size guide and ordered my typical size, XL, and they fit perfectly. When first opening the package, I was concerned that they may be too long. I have a 32” inseam, and these will be a perfect length with the 1” heels I plan to wear. With sneakers, these will be a shade long, but acceptable. For folks with shorter legs, these probably will be too long.These will be very nice for travel since they are very comfortable and can be packed with NO wrinkles! Under $15? Five Stars!!I hope you found this review to be . Thanks for reading!!

Love the pockets! Initially I got these to wear as pajama pants now that the weather is getting colder. I try to buy cotton as much as possible because for me it's more comfortable and these are 95% cotton 5% spandex which is perfect because the spandex helps them keep their shape. If they were 100% cotton they would most likely get baggy and shapeless over time without the spandex, so thats a plus. Anyway, when I tried them on ( I am 5'2", 155lbs, usually a med/large in bottoms ) I was very happy with the fit. I ordered a large according to the size chart because since they're cotton I will have a little room if they shrink a bit. I carry most of my weight up top and have 41" hips and a fairly flat booty. So they fit fine with a little room to spare, I am hoping they will shrink a tiny bit but its so close I'll be happy either way. As far as quality I am impressed. The material is thicker than a high quality T-Shirt but not as thick as your average sweat pants. For me thats perfect for fall and spring weather. It has a ribbed texture that gives it some charm. the waist band is not to wide with excellent stiching and a nice elastic but not to tight. The ties are functional and can be used to tighten the waist if needed or just for looks if not. The seams are well done, straight and even. The hems are good, equal size and well stiched. Last but not least the pockets are deep enough to carry a phone or keys etc. with no worries about falling out. I might even wear these for a grocery run or my grandsons soccer game. I would reccomend these to a friend.

Like them, but not as long as the look Pros - Nice ribbed materiel. Comfortable to sleep in. Nicer looking than most pajama pants, I feel like I could leave my house in these to get mail or something and be fine. Good drawstring Cons - I keep looking for PJs that will be long enough for me (5'9 woman). These are not them, but I'll keep looking. Shrunk a little in the wash too. The stitching can be kind of wonky in areas. Also, I washed them once and one of the pockets has a long hole in them now. Overall, I don't mind them, and for pajamas, they do just fine. Don't think I'd recommend them, however.

Love, Love, Love!!! These pants are so cute and comfortable that I immediately ordered another pair in a different color! I am 5’5” 185lbs and ordered the XL, and the fit was perfect! The fabric was thicker than I expected, which I was really happy about. I felt totally comfortable wearing these outside the house. These pants would be wonderful to travel in! I love the loose, comfortable fit, along with draw string waist, and pockets! If I find something I like, I want to share my experience, so others can buy with confidence. Great price on a quality product! These are a 10 out of 10 in my book!

Fit well. Material is thin, summer weight. I got the X-Large in black. I guess because of the stripes it makes it more of a dark gray color, to me. That is okay with me. I've included a photo that is sort of a close up of what the color and material looks like. The material surprised me with how thin it is though. Great summer weight pants due to the thinness. It was pretty warm (upper 70's) when I tried them on and they felt cool and soft on my skin. A plus for summer! I would not want to wear them in the winter without some thermals underneath as I do live in a state where it can get pretty cold in the winter. The fit is good. The only stretch they have in them is the waist band. The material has no stretch at all. With so many items of clothing having stretch to some degree in them, it is rare to find one that doesn't have it. The length is not too extra long and I will be able to wear them with a shoe that has a slightly thicker sole or even a low heel will be just fine. Being 5' 4" I often run into things being too long in the leg by the time I get it to fit my apple shaped torso. The legs are not as flowy on the thigh part as I thought they would be, but from the knees down, they're flowing nicely, lol. They look to be of decent quality. I did find one dangling thread along a seam on the lower leg, but a quick snip fixed that. Did not see any negative affects from getting rid of that. I will recommend these. Especially for summer or warmer climates. They do look very nice and can be dressed up or down, whatever the mood and occasion is. I did try these on a second time while writing this review and found that I liked them better than when I had tried them on earlier in the day. I am pleased with these pants and the options I have for wearing them.

SWEATPANTS... BUT FASHIONABLE! I ordered these Trendy Queen Wide Leg Sweatpants in Gray in a size medium. I looked at the size chart and decided to size down because I am on a weight loss journey. These sweatpants are truly comfortable! They are the usual fleece lined, Russel-style sweatpants that you can find at the big box store. The criss cross waistline is visually interesting and stretchy. The pleat down the front of the wide leg makes them appear more elevated than your usual ol' slouchy sweatpants. While I do wish that they were a couple of inches longer-- but I can totally deal with it. I plan on wearing these sweatpants with a black high neck cropped tank and athletic shoes. I wore them out to run errands wearing this today... even my doctor commented on how cozy and cute they were! If they were a little less spendy ($30 at time of ordering) and a tiny bit longer, I would have definitely given them five stars.

Cute sweets but pilling already Con: I have worn these for about 4 hours around the house doing house work. They are already pilling in the crotch area/between my legs. Pro: they’re super cute, long enough for my 5’6 frame and a very comfortable and not see thru. I’d buy them again if it wasn’t for the pilling.

Surprisingly heavy I never thought I would use the word "heavy" to describe sweatpants, but that's exactly what these are. Taking them out of the package my first thought was that they feel heavier than a pair of jeans. I was curious so I actually weighed them and they are 1.4 lbs. That may not seem like much, but I also weighed a pair of normal lounge pants and they were under 0.5lb. That means these pants are almost 3 times heavier than normal lounge pants. The material is thick (obviously) but somehow they aren't warm at all and are actually cool to the touch. The waist fit ok, but the legs were gigantic and very boxy. The length was good, but I prefer my pants on the longer side. For reference, I'm 5'4 ad 135lb and ordered size medium. The material seems like decent quality, but I just didn't like how they felt while wearing them.

👠 Light Weight, Smooth & Cool Fabric with a 27.4” inseam For Reference I am ( 36 C *** 5’4” *** 162 lbs ) I received the size Large in the Taupe color . The color is neutral and sides a bit on the tan spectrum. The Fabric is comprised of 75% Nylon and 25% Spandex so it is very stretchy and flexible, which makes these joggers perfect for working out, yoga or in my case they performed very well at pilates class. The versatility of these joggers makes them perfect for lounging or for casual wear also. I paired them with a flannel shirt and some platform uggs for a quick trip to the market. The smoothness of the material offers comfortability, while the coolness of the fabric helps makes them perfect for activities and various seasons, tho I would not be wearing these during the winter months. I like that they are light weight , but unfortunately not so breathable. The Inseam on the main listing page is stated to be 25” which is what drew me to them , except they are also listed on the lower description portion as 27.4” . I decided to measure them and they come in at 27.4” inseam, making them a bit long on me for my height. A great feature of these joggers is the waistband which is not too wide or high and fits true to size. It offers a working drawstring, 2 generously sided pockets and a cuffed hem They would make a comfy travel companion and pack easily but do have the tendency to gather at the crotch area. They are machine washable in cold cycle only, they will shrink in warm to hot water. There are an abundance of different neutral matte colors offered, the brown being another nice option for me, especially if they offer a petite inseam.

Not quite what I as expecting.. These are definitely athletic type material. Or scrub like? It will not keep you warm .. the color is pretty, but does look like scrubs. Very high waisted which isn't my favorite but it'll do. They are not thick. Thin and slick feeling, hence why you will not stay warm in these. The pockets are also crazy deep! Might Need to Size Down if You're Short I like these joggers for the most part. They are light weight. I like that they are slim fitting and not baggy. The pockets are a huge plus. On the models the pants came below the bellybutton. I'm 5'2" and on me they came up quite a bit higher. I got a small. I should have gotten an extra small. Also the lace is really long so there is a lot of extra material after tying it. I wish it was shorter. Overall I think they are nice. Also if it looks like they have stains, in my photos that's my mirror not the pants. They came nicely packaged in a box too.

Attn Plus Size Gals: The XXL's fit great! I'm 5'6" and the length is perfect! I love these joggers! Couple of things to note: 1. I wear a size 20-22 in pants and the XXL's fit me perfectly! 2. I'm 5'6" and the length is perfect! Comes down to the top of my sneakers where joggers should. 3. Pics make them look matte like cotton, but they're nylon and spandex so they have a bit of a sheen to them. 4. Drawstring is nice and long 5. Tops of pockets stick out a little on me, but I'm not wearing a crop top with these, so that will always be hidden underneath a longer top. If I pull these pants up as high as they will go, that pocket issue does smooth out. 6. Well made - Good stitching 7. Very comfortable! Waistband does not constrict or roll over. 8. 100% opaque 8. These pants arrived in a box...you heard that right. A. BOX. I've never received a garment from Amazon in anything other than a bag, but I guess there's a first time for everything! Note: There was a very bright ring light used when I photographed these joggers which made them look like they were charcoal gray. These are actually a very dark, pure black.

I'm obsessed - maybe size down? Wow! I have a lot of joggers, including several higher-end pairs like Vuori, but I reach for these in my drawer! The fabric is incredible; it's hard to describe: sort of silky (not t-shirt material like Vuori), super stretchy, and thin but somehow not in a cheap way. It's sort of like the fabric used for Lulu Align leggings but in jogger form. I will say that the taupe color washes me out, but that's a ME problem, not an issue with the joggers. The stitching is excellent, the pockets are plenty deep enough for a phone, and the waistband is super generous and comfortable with a true elastic drawstring. If you are between sizes, I would size DOWN. I am 5'6" and 130-140 with an athletic build (read: built like a rectangle with zero waist) so I usually have to size up to not be squished in the waist, but these are almost too loose on me. I got a medium and definitely could have gotten away with a small, but this larger size just has a nice, comfy lounge vibe, and that's fine, too. The ankles are tapered but again, a bit loose on me. Vuori brags that you can work out in their joggers but I actually WOULD work out in *these* joggers, and I could also sleep in them, travel in them (perfect airplane pants!), and feel put together enough to run errands in them. Given the quality, this is an amaaaaazing price for these. I'll be ordering more!
