user_comment: Exactly what I was wanting except 1 pair Love them. Exactly what I was looking for. I think these fit as expected. If you want them real baggy order a size up. Otherwise order your regular size. For reference I'm 130# 5'6" I ordered small. Waist for jeans 25" Edit: so I ordered 4 pairs of these sweats. One pair I do not have yet but of the three that I have received, my above comment applies to two of the pairs. The light blue once in the picture and the maroon ones with white lettering Unfortunately we're not able to rate each pair individually and I don't think it's fair to down rate based on one bad pair. Overall I'm happy with my order. However I am warning you guys to beware of the pinkish ones with white lettering. You can tell right away when clicking on the different colors that this one was pictured differently. This one I feel pretty safe to say it's not legit. The tags and everything appear to be but you can tell immediately that the lettering down the left leg is different than the other pairs. It's clearly an iron on. It's smooth surface and the edges are going to peel. Where as my other pairs are more textured and seem to be an actual part of the product. These also were made in a different country than the other ones. Also the color is very misrepresenting. It's more of a pumpkin or a dull Coral coloring. Unfortunately these were the most expensive out of the four pairs I ordered and my gut was telling me something wasn't right with these just off the picture but based off the reviews I also thought it would be a good little research project to order multiple colors and see what happened. The reviews seem to one extreme or the other. I've added pictures of these as well. Simply for the color to be noted

Comfortable, quality pants Sometimes size guides can be tricky. I'm 5'5, 118 lbs, and decided to go with an XS. I don't like baggy pants in general, even with sweatpants. These fit well for me, and I do have decent size hips, and they still fit fine; not tight, and not baggy. My inseam is short (about 27"),so I normally purchase pants from the juniors department. These pants are a pinch long, but the closed bottom hem does a good job of preventing the material from spilling onto the floor while I walk, without being too tight at the bottom either. I use these mainly as lounge pants and they have washed well. The pockets fit my Samsung Note 10+, although it does slightly hang out the side at the top, but keys, slim wallets, etc fit deep inside without a problem. I bought these in a few colors and I think the price makes it a good deal. Edit: I bought the Blue Yonder Heather one and although the size label was what I ordered, the fit was way off.. closer to a medium or large, when I ordered a XS. The print was different too. The other colors I ordered were fine though. Second edit: Have bought a few more of these, most recently the royal blue color. I personally think these pants run big as I ordered the XS and the size chart says I should get a small. Wanted to add that my hips are 38" and my waist is 27". I currently have the Samsung z fold 3 and it fits fine in the pocket when the screen is folded. I recommend.

Nice lightweight sweatpants but… I’ll write the positives of these sweatpants before my “but.” The fitting was accurate as stated. I ordered a large & I’m 32” waist & 42” hip. I weigh 180lbs with my weight distributed evenly between my stomach & hip. These are lightweight. The terry cloth on the inside is soft to the skin. Pants to die for The heathered colors have a wonderful soft brushed feel but the solid colors are a different fabric entirely: soft but slick & shiny so the drape is very different & just feels cheap to me. Right length for my legs (I'm 5' 2"). Two deep pockets. Perfect for running errands & decent enuff for almost everything else. Pregnancy approved! So being pregnant and a tight skinned whale I hate all bottoms right now. Everything is uncomfortable to wear. I wanted to find a pair of pants like these that I could wear to the hospital and back home after giving birth! Or at-least for postpartum use if they were too small or tight on my tummy. My usual weight is in the 130s lbs but with baby number two I’m currently at 170 lbs at 8 months. I wasn’t sure what size to get so I just got my usual pre-pregnancy medium for the after birth. But surprisingly they fit my lower pregnant tummy just fine! No tightness or discomfort spandex pants give me. And they fit great under my tummy too so I know they’ll fit great once baby’s out. I’m just worried if my belly continues to grow these last 2 months it’ll stretch the waist elasticity so I’m not gonna wear it too much to preserve it’s youth lol. But definitely recommend for pregnant people! If you’re less weight or roundness than me medium should fit fine! Bigger than me I would say medium -large just so you don’t suffer from the compression if it gets too tight.

They can stay I like these pants. They are so light and silky and have pockets. The shorter inseam is perfect for us shorter gals. I like the slit at the bottom aesthetically. Can't speak to longevity but first wash (regular cycle) and dry (low) they came out great. I am concerned with the tightness of the waistband though, hence 4 stars. I saw reviews of people over 200lbs saying the Large was comfortable but I'm just not sure how. I feel maxed out in the Large at 160lbs. I think these are great for yoga, sleeping, lifting, walking, beach, and shopping. One more minor gripe that can be easily hidden or may not even be noticable on all pairs, I feel like the way the seam on the crotch is done it makes it look like camel toe. I wear long shirts and I will probably keep these home anyway but just a thought.

I must preface with my height of 5’1.” So, I did expect these pajama bottoms to be long. But, no, they are ridiculously long. Regardless of my choice in shoewear (i.e. chunky sneakers, flat slides), these pants must be hemmed about two feet long, yes? Haha.To its credit, this pair of pajama pants is mostly cotton. With angled pockets and adjustable drawstring, these bottoms are great for lounging especially come Fall.

True petite and comfortable For reference, I'm 5"3, 28 waist, 35 hips, 118 lbs and got the brand's size 4 in black (since I'm normally size small) and they fit like a glove. These area are thin but not sheer. Keep in mind I got the color black and I also wear black underwear underneath. I can't speak for other color options. At least, the ones I got are not see through. I wore them to play pickup in relatively high humidity and sweat buckets. They stayed on pretty well and remained their integrity and comfortable the whole time. Sure I pulled them up and/or adjusted from time to time but that's normal for leggings. Even though black doesn't really show sweat marks, with how much I sweat and how wet the leggings got, I would certainly assume other lighter color options would show very visible sweat marks. If you don't wear them to do things that will cause you to sweat like crazy, other colors would be fine. (or if you don't care much about visible sweat marks on your leggings) Because these are petite, they're actually 7/8 length on me. Yay. Fabric is soft, stretchy, and comfortable. They stretch quite a bit with no bad squeeze. I would not call these compression leggings, and depend on your body build, they may or may not offer tummy control. Compared to my other leggings, though, I personally wouldn't call these tummy control or would not get these for their tummy control feature. But that's me. One thing I noted during my high intensity pickup was I heard a slight, low ripping sound a couple of times when I adjusted the leggings. They did not rip but the sound was not reassuring. I can say I'm not rough with my leggings when I adjust them. Hopefully, these will last a good amount of time.

Perfect Dupe! If you are looking for comfy leggings these are them. They are a total dupe for Lulu even down to the tag inside, it's that long skinny tag if you know you know. Nonetheless, these feel like butter, so soft and smooth. These aren't the most supportive style in that they are not crazy tight in an uncomfortable way. These are the weightless, non-see trough stretchy, quick-drying magical leggings that I cannot recommend highly enough. They will be my new go-to leggings for fall. Also, did I mention the length, I'm a shorty 5'3" and these are not too long like many leggings are on me. Honestly, these are so good I dont want to take them off.

OBSESSED Love this sweatpants set!!! I got it in the black-lilac-gray color and size M. I’m a size 6 or 28/29 in bottoms and they fit perfectly on me, super comfy and doesn’t pinch or squeeze in the waist at all. If I wanted it more form fitting I could have gone down a size but I like my sweatpants comfy. I’m also 5’3 and usually have to get a short inseam, and these go all the way to the floor on me but aren’t too long and don’t bunch at the bottom. EDIT: I’m taking off 1 star because after wearing them for a few weeks I noticed a bunch of loose threads and a bit of pilling in between the thighs. Sooo soft, thick, feels like premium sweatpants from a high end brand but much much cheaper. Could def wear in winter - great colors. The lilac is cute but not too bright, a nice muted pastel pink/purple The only thing to note is that each color fits slightly different, but they all fit great on me in general. The black is the roomiest and the lilac is slightly lower waisted than the others (see pics). Very very slight differences though. Would highly recommend!!!

MY FAVORITE PANTS I LOVE THESE! I'm 5'2" so buying pants online is always a little bit of a chance because usually they're too long but these are perfect! I'm also curvy so I usually don't wear straight leg "mom jean" type pants because they can make me look frumpy, but these are super flattering! I got them in September 2023 (6 months ago) and they are still in perfect condition, I've worn them ice skating, sledding in the snow and they're still in perfect condition. They're not very thick and feel thin so I was worried they'd rip when I totally failed at ice skating but they didn't. Because they're thin they don't get too hot or feel too thick, and I wore leggings under them in the snow and they worked great and I didn't get cold. They also have a lining on the inside so it feels like I'm wearing swishy pants. I wear them at work a lot with a blouse so they work for a business setting, and they're my go to coffee shop pants so they can be dressed down and up. I'm literally going to wear these until they die then buy another pair.

SUPER comfy, black pair tighter though GREY PAIR- received in 2 days. I weigh about 275 & bought an XL. These are very thin but beyond comfy. On me, they're not see thru, they are stretchy, kind of "tight" fit bit definitely not tight fitting; they're super comfy. The ankle part is like a jogging pant bottom (cinched in) but the regular material kinda hides that by laying over it. It's like a cotton feel with a slightly silky feel. I reallyyyy love these && will definitely be getting more colors. I'm 5'7. BIG thighs & calves. BLACK PAIR - bought few weeks later, took 6 days to deliver. really didn't think there'd be a difference but these are a bit more snug than the the Grey pair for some reason. Same size (XL) & you CAN see thru these. I definitely can't wear these out in public & that sucks but still love them for the house.

I sized up and they fit comfortably, very soft fabric, the color doesn't quite match the pics though I'm 5'5"/120 lbs and usually a medium but I got a large as I like to size up for loungewear to ensure it's comfortable. I'm pleased with the way these fit. They are loose enough to be comfy but they still look nice and the waist isn't tight at all though it still stays up on its own. They would probably be too long for me if it wasn't for how they're made to bunch up at the ankles but as is, they are fine. The material is very soft and it's lightweight enough to be nice for hotter weather but not so thin as to be see-through or anything. They do advise hanging these to dry so I wash them on delicate and hang them up. The pockets are nice and roomy but you can see the outline of them while you wear them because of that as it bunches up. There are a couple loose stitches here and there but they seem pretty nicely made overall. The elastic seems sturdy for the waistband and is pretty thick. The drawstring is the only part that seems a little cheap. It's knotted a couple inches down kind of haphazardly and it's very thin and looks a little off. My other small complaint is that the color isn't quite what I expected. In the pictures, they look like a deep purple but to me they look more like a navy blue in person which was a little disappointing but in general I'm happy with this purchase.

Love these! I loved these pants when I saw the picture because they reminded me of my favorite cargo scrub pants. Those were the most comfortable pants, so I had high hopes. I was a little worried when I saw pants in S, M, L, etc., but I hoped that meant there was some leeway in sizing. I wasn't disappointed! I'm 5' 3" and 142 lbs and I bought the MEDIUM. I have long legs for a short woman. I didn't measure the inseam in these, but I'm going to guess 32" or 33". I left my shoes off in the photo, so you could see where the length hit me. I normally wear 32" or 34" inseam depending on the kind of shoes I'm wearing, so these are exactly as long as I was hoping. These snow pants are high waisted, which means no snow falling down the back of your pants. The fit of these is flattering, but they are roomy enough that I could wear another pair of pants underneath. In the photos, I'm just wearing the pants and you can see where I have some room, without there being too much room. I'm looking forward to wearing my favorite Cuddle Duds leggings under these when we get super cold. Our temps are often in the teens during winter with some negative temps sprinkled in. The back waist is elastic and it also has belt loops. The pants have 2 zippered side pockets (one on each side), one zippered back pocket, and one zippered leg pocket. The back and leg pocket are both on the right side. All pockets can easily be zipped and unzipped with one hand. The zipper pulls have an easy-grip fob, so there's no fumbling to get into pockets, even with gloves. Additionally, all pockets are big enough to hold my iPhone XR without any magic tricks, so these pockets are generous. When I read these pants were water resistant, I worried they might make a swishing sound when I walk — like some track pants do — but they don't. They are totally quiet when I walk. The pants have a smooth satiny feel. They are soft, inside and out. The inside of the pants are not fluffy and bulky — think of the lining as being sueded and you'll have the best idea of what the warm lining is like. The fabric is thick without being bulky. I live in a really cold climate and I've been wearing these whenever I leave the house. I've comfortably worn them down to 10 degrees, so far. Normally my legs are my weakest area in my cold weather gear, but these pants have changed that since they are very warm and I can wear leggings (or even regular pants) under them. These snow pants are super comfortable — even for wear around the house. They zip up and snap. I love the snap and it has a little velcro tab to help keep things smooth and together. They're well made and look really nice. You can't tell they are snow pants. The just look like cargo scrubs or tactical pants. They have these cool seams in the knees (like my favorite cargo scrub pants did) that make it so you can bend down without the pants binding in the knees. I don't have a single complaint about these pants! They've upped my winter game. I'd love it if they had colors like dark plum purple and wine. I'd definitely purchase at least one more pair if they had colors like that, but I may even be back for another pair of the black ones, so I can wear a pair while the other is in the wash. I know some people are very concerned about fabrics with odors, but I didn't notice any odor at all when I took these out of the package. I've washed them and they came out just like they went in, but softer. All in all, these are great pants and I'm happy I found them!

Great quality and true to size! I am a security account manager and work in a cold warehouse. We have a uniform that is issued, but they do not provide warm pants. Before buying these pants I had to wear leggins under my uniform pants just to keep warm, but it was uncomfortable. I am 5'6" and weigh 135 so I ordered a medium. They look professional, are well made and keep me warm without the extra layer of leggins! These pants fit well and are comfortable to wear, the length is long enough to go over my boots and the zip pockets give extra protection from loosing anything you may have in them. I will be buying another pair to get me through Michigans cold winter!

Quiet & comfortable I am 5'8", a standard size 16 with a 31/32" inseam. I carry my weight a little bit in my belly, but am proportionally filled out throughout the bum and thigh. The XXL was a smidge large- but not enough for me to want to size down - to allow for base layers if needed. I would guess these would be a 16/18.Pros: Quiet- very important to me that they didn't sound like snowpants. Soft fleece interior with satiny pockets. The closure has both a snap and hook-n-loop tab and there are belt loops well placed around the waist. These are what I would consider mid to high waisted and didn't feel like I would expose my crack when bending over.Cons: Pockets! Can womens pants please start having the same size pockets as mens! There is only one back pocket and the thigh "cargo" pocket is just a straight-flat pocket ( the pleat is just for looks - I'm going to remove the one seam to allow it to be a real cargo pocket) AND both of these pockets are on the right side so if you have stuff in both, your pants will be very heavy on the right side. Also, the front comes up a little high for my tastes and is a little floofy in the pooch area. I feel they could drop the front at least an inch and they would almost fit perfectly.I've now tried 6 different, highly rated pairs of fleece lined pants - even high-end name brand pairs and these are the ones I'll be using on my upcoming hike. For the price, they seem to be of good quality and have been the most comfortable so far. If anything changes after the hike, I will update my review.

Cozy but a bit odd I’m 5’1” and weigh 183 lb. I got the 2x which at first felt a bit tight in the crotch and thighs but seemed to loosen up within an hour or so of wearing. I think I’ll be able to wear a thin base layer underneath. The pants are comfortable and the lining feels very cozy though the cut seems a little odd to me. The crotch is higher in the front than the back and there is some puckering along parts of the seam. I can’t speak to how weatherproof they are, but hoping for the best. The outer material is soft yet substantial. I like that the waist is elastic in the back with a zip front. The pockets are a good size.

Stay cozy and dry in these light-weight dogwalkers/hiking/camping pants Thoughtful details like a deep side leg pocket to slide your phone in and out of. They have elastic around half the waist for a snug fit (but not tight). Side zip pockets. One Pocket in rear. A snap closer with some added velcro also. I am 5'9 and 147lbs and the large fits perfectly but I like them a bit loose in the back ( they are NOT form fitting). If you want a sleeker look- (well. These aren't the pants for you, if you want a sleeker look! They are cargo pants with soft , light fleece lining), I would go a size down. Otherwise I usually wear a size 8 or 10. Mostly a 10. And the large fits perfectly.

Great value!

Well made, slim fit These have a nice fleece lining and are slim fitting so more flattering than a lot of snow pants. The leg is rather narrow so my Sorel snow boots would have be worn with the trousers tucked in. All my other boots and hiking boots will be fine. The zipper enclosures are well made and easy to open and I really liked the Velcro fastening. I would say they’re true to size but if in doubt or you’re between sizes then size up. For reference I’m 5’6, athletic build and 129lbs. I have enough room in these to wear leggings or Thermals underneath. There is some give in the waistband with it being elastic. They’re more flattering than the usual snow pants I’ve worn and will be easy to move around in if I’m hiking or shovelling snow.

Exactly what I wanted! These pants are exactly what I wanted for winter hiking and playing in the snow. They are incredibly comfortable and warm. The outside is water resistant. The water rolls right off. The inside is lined with fleece. I think that they look great on me. I was worried that they would be fluffy like traditional snow pants, they are not. They look and fit more like hiking cargo pants but they are durable and warm enough to wear as snow pants, great functionality. There are plenty of pockets and they are all zippered. That will be nice for sledding, I won't need to worry about my car keys getting lost in the snow. I am 5'6, 153 pounds and the large fits me perfectly. I am able to wear a thin pair of leggings under these pants. They're tight but it's do able.

Another pair going back Updated Review: I decided to try a size small. I am shocked they fit so much better I might just keep them. The small is the same length as the medium (weird) and they are still big enough for me to fit thermals underneath. The front of the pants pulls a little but it's not a total deal breaker for pants that I'll only wear in the outdoors.---- Original Review -----These pants felt nice and they seem like they would be warm. I am 5'4" tall and weigh 140 lbs. I am a little curvy and somewhat muscular. I have an hour glass figure and very little stomach poof. Also I have a low rise (high rise pants come up well over my navel). I like my outdoor pants to be baggy so I ordered a medium. The fit was just all kinds of wrong for my physique. As you can see from the photos the front of the pants are cut higher than the back. All of my women's pants are cut lower in the front than the back which leads me to think that these are guys pants with elastic in the back. The front of the pants are OK. The back of the pants are huge. Maybe I need a small but then I fear they won't be baggy. I think these are just short men's pants with elastic in the waist. If I were a guy these would fit so much better because then I'd have the stuff in the front to take up the space that makes the waist so high. I like my clothes to actually fit me properly and totally dislike when they don't. This is not something I sacrifice on. That being said, if you're OK with a not so great fit then these pants are nice and have deep pockets and just might be for you. Otherwise keep shopping.

These are ladies trousers so this review is by my partner: Lightweight fabric with a cosy fleece type lining. Plenty of zip pockets and a part elasticated waist for a better fit and comfort when moving around. I'm a size 16 and a pear shape do I tend to have difficulty finding trousers that aren't tight on my thighs and hips. The size XXL were a perfect fit with still room on my legs. The only issue is they are very long in the leg for my 5ft4 stature but I simply rolled the bottoms up. Good trousers and will buy more as this is the only pair of water resistant trousers I have which are great for walking and gardening in (as well as skiing!)

Recommend if you’re gonna be pregnant in the winter! I absolutely loved these pants while I was pregnant! I’m normally someone who wears a small but I did gain 50lbs during pregnancy so I ordered a medium. I’ve put them on since I’ve given birth and they are big but still fit. They are super stretchy and a soft athletic type material. I’m 5’2” and normally 120, most pants fit me long but these weren’t too bad. Overall, I highly recommend

Amazing Such a great look and fit, buying another color soon! They are thin but still feel great quality, perfect high rise, hidden drawstring, fashionable design. Just love them! I am 5’7” 155 pounds and got a M

Amazing pants, love the material and I was so excited that everything fit well.. except for the length. There’s two sizing charts that both have different inseam lengths than what I received. I’m 5’6 and got the XS, 31.9” would have been a perfect length but they came as 30” long which makes them look too short. I would size up for the length but sadly the waist would be too big.

Comfortable & nice quality..LONG My photo is a bit difficult to see (and I apologize for the dirty mirror - the white spots are NOT the pants!). The pants are comfortable, love the pockets and the feel of the fabric. I'm 5'3" and have one of the pant legs rolled up and pinned and ready to take to the tailor for hemming (cutting off about 5"). These are a great weight for fall or spring. I don't think they'll be warm enough for winter in the north, but if you are in the south, they should be year-round pants.

These pants are very flattering and well made. They really show my figure in a good way. The pockets sit flat and they are roomy, and the overall construction is excellent. The fabric is pleasant and somehow warm, the pants are made of a linen and cotton blend. To note, there is absolutely no stretch in the fabric, aside of course from the elastic band at the waist. Fit wise, the pants fit as expected, maybe a little bit large. I take L or XL , I ordered Xl and they were fitting me perfect this morning. As an observation, the fabric is such that it gets a little bit larger and larger as the day goes by, something that both linen and cotton jeans tend to do most often. There is an adjustment tie at the waist which is most welcome nice the pants fit me large. The length is quite short on me, a few inches above the ankle. When I sit the length goes up to mid calf on me (I am 5’7”) The pants are very comfortable and ideal for spring and fall, I think they might be too warm for the summer. They look great with clogs or sandals, since they are too short to wear them with socks. Recommended! Thank you

According to their size chart, I wear a XXL, so that's what I ordered, and I was pleased to fit perfectly in the joggers. I'm also 5'10, and the length is perfect—not too short. The only thing is that the fabric leaves very little to the imagination. There's no cotton (80% nylon, 20% spandex), so it's super stretchy but very shiny, and it's not flattering. The fabric shows your underwear demarcation and everything else—cellulitis, you name it. I'll still wear them at work since they’re comfortable and breathable, but they're definitely not my favorite joggers, and I suggest you wear them with a tunic shirt.

Size is a huge plus for me as not clothes are made for small women.

So comfy! These are great! I got 4 different colors and love them. They fit wonderfully and are very flattering. The only con I can say if they aren’t *super* warm. They are fleece lined, so it’s great for a fall day. I did try them for a walk on a windy day that was around 37F-40F and my legs froze. My thighs were solid red when I got home and freezing to the touch.

Cold weather exercising I ordered two sizes (S/M) for comparison and plan to use these for cross country skiing and hiking in 25-35 degree weather when a wind layer isn’t necessary. 5’4” and 130 lbs. I tried both the small and medium. Both fit. The medium was a little loose in the waste and a little longer but I went with the medium in case they shrink in the wash. This is the same material and fit as the tights listed as “Thermal Winter Yoga Pants Warm Running Tights with Pockets”. The difference is that this product has a horizontal zipper pocket on the back of the waste band large enough to fit an iphone (photo). Nothing about them seem water resistant so I don’t expect them to be.
Weird knees They’re warm, but the knees are super weird, both while legs are bent and straight, super weird.. I’m 5’3” 103 lbs

I live in Canada and was looking for some nice fleece pants. I ordered 4 different colors in XL. Three pairs showed up in XL and the purple pair was a L. I was a bit disappointed but tried them all on anyway. I usually wear a large in my clothing but based on reviews, I ordered the XL. It turns out both the L and XL for me well. The L is a bit tighter, but still fits. The XL pants fit perfect with a bot more room in the butt and upper thighs. I am going to keep all 4 pairs. They feel like really good quality and wash nicely. I let them hang to dry in case they shrink. The legs are a bit long and bunch at the ankles but it doesn’t bother me. I am 5ft 5 with an athletic build and a 30 inch inseam. I have a bit of a belly but not huge. My waist is about 34 inches and my thighs measure about 42inches. I love the fit and would recommend buying them to anyone. I purchased a medium pair for my niece as well and I am excited to give them to her for her Christmas present. Update-November 12, 2023- I have been wearing these leggings for almost a year now during cooler days. I have washed them in the washing machine and dried them in a dryer and they are still holding up. I love them. They haven’t shrunk or lost their shape and haven’t ripped or pilled. I still recommend these pants to anyone who asks. I am looking at purchasing the green pair, just so I have one in each color.

Comfortable and cute These weren’t what I expected at first, but after I put them on, I really liked them. They were very comfortable and looked ok for office pants. The buttons on the side class these up a bit. I thought they would be taper in at the ankle more, but they were more of a straight leg opening. The front pockets are deep and the back pockets are functional, just not very deep, my phone fell out when I bent over. The material is slightly thick, but these wash and dry well. They did shrink a tad after the first wash, but nothing too drastic and actually fit a bit better. These don’t have a belt loop, so the elastic waist is what you get. I ordered a medium and have been wearing size 8 lately. The medium fit fine and wasn’t too long or too short, just slightly loose around the waist at first, but better after wash. For reference, I am 5’4”, 141lbs, 30” waist, 28” inseam.

Accurate photos and description These are a stretchy polyester like thicker leggings would be. I was expecting something a little bit softer, but they are still comfortable and look fine. The style makes them look like regular dress pants. The front and back pockets are (somewhat) functional, and the waist detailing is cute (although I don’t usually tuck my shirts in so it won’t be seen). I ordered my usual Medium, as the size chart indicated I fell right in the middle of Medium measurements. I like the fit overall and they are comfortable. The waistband seems fine so far, but some belt loops would have been nice just in case they loosen up over time. Often pants labeled as “cropped” are a perfect fit on my short legs, but these are ankle pants on me, which I don’t think is the most flattering look. The 27” inseam is accurate on my Medium size pair. I would feel more comfortable if they were an inch longer or shorter, but that’s just me and my 29” inseam. They have straight slim legs (but not tight), so I think they will work tucked into boots, which solves the length problem for me. They seem like a nice quality, and I found the size chart to be accurate as well as the photos and description, so I feel I should give them the full 5 stars. I’ve included photos with a tape measure for inseam and waistband on the size Medium.

High-quality material and construction; sizing may be a problem We ordered these for my wife because she was looking for a warm pair of sweatpants to wear around the house in the winter. We looked at the sizing chart and ordered a size Small based on her measurements. She is 5’0” tall and weighs just under 100 pounds. The Small was too big around the waist and the hips, but the biggest problem was the length: it was way too long. The Small is what she is wearing in the two photos. We then ordered the Extra Small, thinking that would solve the problem. The Extra Small fit better in the waist and hips, but it was still at least 2” too long. Soft, Warm, Stylish, and Perfect Length Most Amazon clothing tends to be a letdown, with odd fits that don't always work out. But every now and then, a piece surprises me—and these Fleece-Lined Sweatpants are a rare find.These sweatpants are thick and well-made without feeling overly heavy, providing the warmth I need. The interior has a soft, thick lining that feels incredibly comfortable against the skin. They feature an elastic waistband with a drawstring for adjustability and two practical side pockets. The back of the pants has a curved stitching just below the waistband, adding a subtle, flattering touch to the design. Additionally, a matching-color stripe down each leg adds a bit of style, setting them apart from typical sweatpants.The material includes some cotton, so they feel breathable even with the fleece lining. And the best part? The length. Finally, a pair of sweatpants that doesn’t look like I’m wearing something that shrank in the wash! At 5'7", they fall about half an inch from the ground without shoes—just the way I like it.At 128 pounds, I find these fit slightly loose, which I prefer for sweatpants. Absolutely my new favorite.

Nice fleece lined athletic pants I am a fan of fleece lined pants for the winter. These are many features to like about these sweatpants:1. The fleece lining is warm and soft. I would rather have a little more room than have these be tight. They’re a little loose, but they are comfortable and don’t look oversized. They are also long enough! They are true to the 30” inseam.3. The sweatpants fabric is actually really nice. It’s closer to a ponte knit. I plan to not only use these for my walks in the winter, but also as casual wear with a sweater or jacket.4. The drawstring waist is substantial. And there are two side pockets.Overall, I’m very pleased with these. I may order a second pair as back up. They’re just as nice as similar items I’ve ordered from other brand name catalogs. They are a good value for the price.

Waistband is small! These pants are super adorable, and very nice quality. I absolutely LOVE the pink color. However, here are the issues I have with these pants: 1. The waistband is soooo tight. I stretched them out on the back of a chair for two days hoping it would make the elastic give a little. I even steamed it while stretched but neither gave me much, if any relief. 2. I am tall, 5'9" and pants are always short on me. I can make these long enough by pulling them down from my waist (which then makes the elastic even tighter when lowered to my hip) but I have a lot of sagging in the crotch. I will still wear these but will have to wear them with a long silk shirt that will cover up the crotch area. 3. Last things to know are that you should NOT wear any lotion of any kind when wearing these pants. I mistakenly had some on when I wanted to wear these and this fabric really shows moisture so I couldn't wear them and had to send them to the wash. Which, MAKE SURE YOU WASH THEM SEPARATELY. The pink dye in these pants bled onto other items in my wash. Nothing was important so I didn't care but everyone should know and take heed of this warning. Anyway, super cute pants. I may just take the elastic out completely (if that is possible) and make it a drawstring waist because I DO want to wear these.

Comfortable, but not necessarily flattering I LOVE these pants. At this point, I own about 8 pairs. They are my go-to 'sitting around at home' pant and I wear them almost every day. They're thin enough to wear in hot weather, but aren't sheer, and the waistband is great (where it cinches in pretty far but can stretch a ton, in the XL it's 15 inches across the waistband un-stretched, and 30 inches when stretched all the way out). There are a few things that I would make a note of, though. Firstly, the different colors have different qualities. The grey is my favorite, it's the stretchiest and softest. The black is stiffer and thicker (but still a go-to for me), and the green is sheerer and more like an upscale legging or almost similar to a thin dress pant (and is my least favorite). These pants can also be quite unflattering. This is because the fit is a little weird (which is sort of expected with 'parachute' pants) but isn't my favorite. I am a little over 200lbs with a mildly athletic build (like, 'athlete who's let themselves go for a few years'), and primarily have these in size XL. The inseam is one of the main issues for me. I have normal length of leg, if not slightly short, where the inside of my leg is about 31 inches long. The inseam for these pants is 24.5 inches long, and there is about 15 inches between the end of the inseam and the top of the waistband. In order to get rid of that extra room, I roll the waistband 1-2x over, so that they generally look and fit like normal pants and so my legs to stick together when I walk. this makes the pants hit me in a weird place, though, like lower calf. The pants also tend to be thin enough to show, if you are not very thin/toned, an unfortunate texture on the thighs. Between these factors, the pants are so ugly even my friends and family have commented that these pants need to stay as house pants and never be worn in public. The only other issue with these is that they do stretch out when you wear them, so they'll be pretty tight for 2-3 wears, then get really loose, then go back to being tight after you wash them. They also wear in certain places and eventually get holes, but it takes a while to get to that point. In general, I find the grey and black versions of this to be amazing for working at home, doing chores, or laying around (or even sleeping), but are not great for going out. They're super comfortable and work in most types of mild weather, and because of that, I plan to continue buying more of these in the future.

Mixed Fit; Skip Ceylon I’ve owned the black pair for almost a year now and I love them! The fabric isn’t sweatshirt, but it’s still decently warm (I’ve been able to wear it year-round). Comfortable and cute I’m 5’3 and these land a few inches above my ankle just for reference. These are definitely not at thick as the pictures show, but the material isn’t bad, it’s still a nice soft cotton feel. I really like these actually. The waist is high-waisted which I think is flattering with crop tops. The pockets do kind of add some bulk but if you adjust them in just the right spot it’s not that bad. Overall I’m pleased with these!

Im 5ft 4, ordered xsmall. The quality seems fine to me. The fabric actually thicker/heavier than I anticipated but no complaints. It is a bit baggy in the crotch area, as most pants are on me. However, I was mostly surprised by the length. I’m 5 ft 4 and if I were any taller these would be too short.

Way shorter than advertised The size guide charge says medium is 41+in. You can see here these are not even 30! I have a 35” inseam and am super hard to fit so I was so excited. Tried to return and cannot due to missed window. Tried contacting the seller and cannot. These are not stretchy either. They are just stiff and thin.

Great Pants, Long Shipping Would've gave 5 stars, but they took around a month to get to me. Other than that, they are exactly what was pictured. I'm not really a fan of the fake back & zipper pockets, but the cargo pockets are pretty big & I can fit almost my whole hand in the top side pockets. I'm also glad I went with the extra large, because I feel like they're made small & they aren't made with a stretchy material. A large definitely wouldn't have fit. For reference, I'm 4'11", roughly 160lbs & I think my waist is around 35"-36" so I'm a bit chunky in the midsection. They are also a little long, but they will rest on my shoes so that's okay. If you're 5'4 maybe 5'5 I think the length would be perfect. They're also high waisted, which I also like. If you prefer hip huggers or low rise, the crotch might hang near your knees😂 All in all they're nice, they're comfortable & they aren't thick which is nice because they are for work. Breathable is better. Hope this review was , I tried to be as detailed as possible :-)

Perfect Pants for the Holidays These tartan plaid pants are really cute! The perfect pant for Christmas celebrations. The red color is vibrant, and the pattern is really beautiful. The big bow that ties in the front adds festive style! I found them to be plenty long enough for my long legs (I normally wear 32 inch inseam jeans). They did run a touch snug though in the waist, so if you are between sizes in that area you may want to size up. There was a lot of extra room in the hip area though. I like the cut with the gathered waistline and loose, wide leg. Very on trend currently. The fabric was different than I expected. It is heavier and a bit more stiff than other dress pants in my closet. It feels like a quality fabric that should last. I do wish that there were pockets. For that missing element, I took off one star. All told, I am quite pleased with these fun red plaid pants. Looking forward to wearing them at the family holiday celebration!

Perfect These are so cute and have a hidden zipper. I love the design and the tie front. These may not be great for women over 5’6

Strange Fit I was excited for the vintage vibe of these pants. But the fit is so strange. My best guess would be to say size down. I am wide through the middle - 27-28" waist. The waist band (button - no elastic) fit me fine. Length and rise were ok/good (I typically wear Petite 2/4 in brands like Loft). Hips and thighs were so big. The fabric is thin which gives these pants a pajama bottom look due to the poor fit. Maybe these are perfect for someone who is petite and curvy? That is not me. For reference, I am 5'3" - 125# - 36B with an athletic/boyish build.

Nice pants, came slightly damaged though I really like these pants, they fit well! I got a size large and I’m 5’3” and 130 lbs, for context. They’re comfortable and omg the POCKETS 🤩 however I can’t give 5 stars cuz they came with one of the snaps broken off. I was sooo disappointed 😞
Worth the price Great pants. I’m 5’2 135lb bought a medium. I usually wear a size 6 jeans. They were somewhat thinner but not see through. No tummy control. Worked perfect!

Cute, but a bit big and a timst bit See through…not much tho I liked these pants, they are a good fabric to me personally, but they were a bit big in the legs. But I’m also super skinny and wear a 000-00 in jeans (hopefully that helps give you an idea) and they fit nicely, but not as snug as in the photos. I’d get them if there was an xs size, but idk. THEY ARE ALSO SUPER SUOER LONG. In those photos I have 5-6 inch heels on and they make me 5’4-5’5 so that should also give you a little idea. I’m also pretty short so they probably look weird on me, I’m originally 4’11 btw, but if you get them, just know they are very long, but they are cute and stretchy and worth the price I paid
